The high-quality wheat ensures your dough is easy to knead and your rotis, chapatis, and parathas stay soft for longer.
Description:
The Secret to Extra Soft Rotis: Pillsbury Chakki Fresh Atta - 5kg

Experience the joy of making perfectly soft, fluffy, and wholesome Indian flatbreads every time with Pillsbury Chakki Fresh Atta in a generous 5kg value pack. This premium quality whole wheat flour from the globally trusted Pillsbury brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is made from 100% high-quality wheat grains. It is processed using a traditional "chakki-grinding" method, which helps to retain the natural dietary fiber and nutrients, ensuring your rotis are not only delicious but also nutritious.

Why Choose Pillsbury Chakki Fresh Atta?

Pillsbury is a household name for a reason. Their Chakki Fresh Atta is specially formulated for the perfect flatbread experience:

  • Exceptionally Soft Rotis: Pillsbury's unique blend of wheat and traditional grinding process is renowned for producing dough that results in incredibly soft and pliable rotis, chapatis, and phulkas that stay soft for longer.
  • 100% Whole Wheat Goodness: Made from the whole wheat grain, this atta is a great source of natural dietary fiber, which is essential for a healthy digestive system.
  • Easy to Knead Dough: The fine texture and quality of the flour make the dough easy to knead, resulting in a smooth and elastic consistency that is a pleasure to work with.
  • Fluffy & Delicious Results: Create light, airy phulkas that puff up perfectly and delicious, soft parathas and puris.
  • Trusted International Brand: Pillsbury's commitment to quality ensures a consistent and reliable product that delivers perfect results every time.
  • Value 5kg Pack: This large pack is ideal for families and for ensuring your pantry is always stocked with this essential staple.

Perfect for All Your Favourite Indian Breads

  • Roti & Chapati: Ideal for making soft, thin chapatis for your daily meals.
  • Phulka: Perfect for creating light rotis that puff up beautifully on the flame.
  • Paratha: Create delicious plain, stuffed (like Aloo Paratha), or flaky (Lachha Paratha) flatbreads.
  • Puri: Make fluffy, deep-fried puris that are a festive favorite.
  • Other Breads: Can also be used as a wholesome base for other types of Indian or whole-grain breads.

 Tips for the Softest Rotis

  • Use lukewarm water to knead the dough for a softer result.
  • Allow the kneaded dough to rest, covered, for at least 15-30 minutes before rolling. This allows the gluten to relax, making the rotis softer.
  • Store your cooked rotis in a cloth-lined container or a roti box (casserole) to keep them warm and soft.

The "Chakki-Grinding" Process for Superior Rotis

Aashirva-ad uses a traditional "chakki-grinding" process to create its atta, which is crucial for achieving the perfect texture and nutritional value.

  • What is "Chakki-Grinding"? This method mimics traditional stone mills ("chakkis"), grinding the wheat slowly and at a low temperature. This process is important because it helps to preserve the natural dietary fiber, bran, and nutrients of the whole wheat grain.
  • The Result for You: The atta produced has a unique texture that absorbs water more efficiently, leading to a dough that is easier to knead and produces rotis that are exceptionally soft and remain so for longer.

Why Aashirva-ad Shudh Chakki Atta is India's No. 1 Atta Brand

  • Made from 100% Whole Wheat: Crafted from the choicest wheat grains sourced from across India, ensuring you get the full nutritional benefit of whole wheat.
  • Guaranteed Softness: Aashirva-ad's primary promise is rotis that are so soft, they practically melt in your mouth.
  • Rich in Dietary Fiber: Being 100% whole wheat, it's a natural source of fiber, which is important for a healthy digestive system.
  • No Maida Added: The brand prides itself on providing pure whole wheat atta with no added refined flour (maida).
  • Trusted Brand: Aashirva-ad is a household name in India, trusted by millions of mothers for its consistency and quality.

Perfect for All Your Favourite Indian Breads

  • Daily Roti & Chapati: The ideal choice for making soft, wholesome flatbreads to accompany every meal.
  • Fluffy Phulkas: Create light rotis that puff up beautifully on the flame, a sign of a great dough.
  • Delicious Parathas: Use it to make flavourful plain, stuffed (like Aloo Paratha), or flaky layered parathas.
  • Puri: Make soft, fluffy deep-fried puris for festive meals or special breakfasts.

Name: TRS Gram Flour (Besan/Chickpea Flour) 1kg - Versatile Kitchen Staple
Short description:
TRS Gram Flour(Besan)

\n

A must-have in every Indian kitchen, TRS Gram Flour (Besan/Chickpea Flour) 1kg! This high-quality, finely ground flour made from chana dal is incredibly versatile. Perfect for making crispy pakoras and bhajis, delicious besan ladoos, thickening curries, creating batters, and as a gluten-free flour alternative.
Description:
The Versatile Kitchen Staple: TRS Gram Flour (Besan) - 1kg)

Unlock a world of culinary possibilities with TRS Gram Flour (Besan/Chickpea Flour) in a convenient 1kg pack. This fundamental ingredient in Indian and Pakistani cuisine, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a finely milled flour made from chana dal (split brown chickpeas). Renowned for its nutty flavor, binding properties, and incredible versatility, TRS's quality besan is perfect for a wide array of savory snacks, traditional sweets, batters, and even as a gluten-free thickening agent. This 1kg pack is ideal for regular home use.

 The Goodness of Gram Flour (Besan)
Besan is much more than just flour; it's a culinary cornerstone:

  • Nutty & Earthy Flavor: Imparts a distinct, slightly sweet, and nutty flavor to dishes, which deepens beautifully when roasted.

  • Excellent for Batters & Binding: Its unique properties make it ideal for creating crispy batters for pakoras, bhajis, and other fried snacks. It also acts as a great binding agent.

  • Foundation for Sweets (Mithai): The star ingredient in beloved Indian sweets like Besan Ladoo, Mysore Pak, and Besan Barfi.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: A fantastic alternative to wheat flour for those with gluten sensitivities.

  • Rich in Protein & Fiber: Gram flour is a good source of plant-based protein and dietary fiber.

  • Trusted TRS Quality: TRS is a well-regarded brand, ensuring this gram flour is finely ground and of high quality for optimal results.

Culinary Uses for TRS Gram Flour.

  • Savory Snacks:

    • Pakoras & Bhajis: Create crispy, deep-fried fritters with various vegetables or paneer.

    • Cheela/Pudla: Make savory, protein-rich pancakes.

    • Khandvi & Dhokla (often with other flours): A key component in these popular snacks.

  • Sweets (Mithai):

    • Besan Ladoo: Classic sweet balls made with roasted besan, ghee, and sugar.

    • Besan Barfi/Mysore Pak: Rich, fudge-like sweets.

  • Curries & Gravies:

    • Thickening Agent: Used to thicken some curries and Kadhi (yogurt-based gravy).

    • Koftas: As a binder for vegetable or koftas.

  • Batters: For coating items before frying.

  • Traditional Skincare: Besan is also traditionally used in homemade face packs. (Note: this product is sold as food-grade).

 Tips for Using Gram Flour.

  • Sift gram flour before use to remove any lumps for a smooth batter or dough.

  • Roasting gram flour (bhuna besan) lightly before using it in sweets or some savory dishes significantly enhances its nutty aroma and flavor.

Your Stockholm Source for Quality Flours & Staples.
Ideal Indiska is committed to providing essential, high-quality ingredients like TRS Gram Flour. This 1kg pack is perfect for stocking your Stockholm kitchen for all your authentic Indian and Pakistani cooking and sweet-making.

 The Goodness of Gram Flour (Besan)
Besan is much more than just flour; it's a culinary cornerstone:

  • Nutty & Earthy Flavor: Imparts a distinct, slightly sweet, and nutty flavor to dishes, which deepens beautifully when roasted.

  • Excellent for Batters & Binding: Its unique properties make it ideal for creating crispy batters for pakoras, bhajis, and other fried snacks. It also acts as a great binding agent in cutlets, koftas, and vegetarian burgers.

  • Foundation for Sweets (Mithai): The star ingredient in beloved Indian sweets like Besan Ladoo, Mysore Pak, Besan Barfi, and Mohanthal.

Culinary Uses for TRS Gram Flour.

  • Savory Snacks:

    • Pakoras & Bhajis: Create crispy, deep-fried fritters with vegetables like onions, potatoes, spinach, paneer, or chillies.

    • Cheela/Pudla: Make savory, protein-rich pancakes.

    • Khandvi & Dhokla (often with other flours): A key component in these popular steamed Gujarati snacks.

    • Sev & Gathiya: Used to make these popular crunchy extruded snacks.

  • Sweets (Mithai):

    • Besan Ladoo: Classic sweet balls made with roasted besan, ghee, and sugar.

    • Besan Barfi/Mysore Pak: Rich, fudge-like sweets.

  • Curries & Gravies:

    • Thickening Agent: Used to thicken some curries and particularly Kadhi (a yogurt-based gravy).

    • Koftas: As a binder for vegetable or even some non-vegetarian koftas.

  • Batters: For coating vegetables or fish before frying.

  • Face Packs (Traditional Skincare): Besan is also traditionally used in homemade face packs for its cleansing and exfoliating properties. (Note: this product is sold as food-grade).

Tips for Using Gram Flour.

  • Sift gram flour before use to remove any lumps and ensure a smooth batter or dough.

  • Roasting gram flour (bhuna besan) lightly in a dry pan or with a little ghee before using it in sweets like ladoos or some savory dishes significantly enhances its nutty aroma and flavor.

The Foundation of Perfect Rotis: Elephant Atta Medium - 25kg Bulk Bag.

Ensure your family enjoys soft, fluffy, and wholesome Indian flatbreads every day with Elephant Atta Medium Chapati Flour in a generous 25kg bulk bag. This iconic and trusted brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, has been a staple in kitchens for generations, renowned for its consistent quality and its ability to produce perfectly soft rotis, chapatis, parathas, and puris. This large bag offers exceptional value for families and frequent users.

The Elephant Atta Legacy.
Elephant Atta is celebrated for its commitment to quality and tradition in milling whole wheat flour:

  • Premium Quality Whole Wheat: Made from carefully selected whole wheat grains, ensuring natural goodness and nutritional value, including dietary fiber.
  • Consistently Soft & Fluffy Rotis: The "Medium" grind is specifically designed to produce dough that is easy to work with and bakes into delightfully soft and pliable flatbreads that puff up beautifully.
  • Authentic Taste & Texture: Delivers the traditional taste and texture expected from homemade chapatis and rotis.
  • Stone-Ground Tradition (Often Claimed): (Elephant Atta often highlights a traditional stone-grinding process which helps retain nutrients and flavor. 
  • Versatile for All Flatbreads: Perfect not only for everyday chapatis and rotis but also for layered parathas, stuffed parathas, and fluffy puris.
  • Loved by Families for Generations: A household name trusted for its reliability and quality, making it a preferred choice for daily meals.
  • Exceptional Value 25kg Bag: This bulk size is ideal for large families, those who make flatbreads daily, or for stocking up, providing significant savings.

 Tips for Making Perfect Rotis with Indian Atta.

  1. Take the desired amount of Elephant Atta Medium flour in a mixing bowl.
  2. Gradually add lukewarm water, kneading to form a soft, smooth, and pliable dough. (Some add a pinch of salt or a little oil, but it's not always necessary).
  3. Cover the dough and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes.
  4. Divide the dough into small, equal-sized balls.
  5. Dust with a little dry atta and roll out each ball thinly into a round circle (chapati/roti).
  6. Cook on a hot tawa (flat griddle), flipping once. Then, cook directly over a flame (using tongs) or continue cooking on the tawa, pressing gently with a cloth, until it puffs up.
  7. Serve hot, often with a touch of ghee.

Description:
The Versatile Kitchen Staple in Bulk: Heera Gram Flour (Besan) - 2kg)

Unlock a world of culinary possibilities with Heera Gram Flour (Besan/Chickpea Flour) in a generous 2kg value pack. This fundamental ingredient in Indian and Pakistani cuisine, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a finely milled flour made from chana dal (split brown chickpeas). Renowned for its nutty flavor, binding properties, and incredible versatility, Heera's quality besan is perfect for a wide array of savory snacks, traditional sweets, batters, and even as a gluten-free thickening agent. This large pack is ideal for families and avid cooks.

: The Goodness of Gram Flour (Besan)
Besan is much more than just flour; it's a culinary cornerstone:

  • Nutty & Earthy Flavor: Imparts a distinct, slightly sweet, and nutty flavor to dishes, which deepens beautifully when roasted.

  • Excellent for Batters & Binding: Its unique properties make it ideal for creating crispy batters for pakoras, bhajis, and other fried snacks. It also acts as a great binding agent in cutlets, koftas, and vegetarian burgers.

  • Foundation for Sweets (Mithai): The star ingredient in beloved Indian sweets like Besan Ladoo, Mysore Pak, Besan Barfi, and Mohanthal.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: A fantastic alternative to wheat flour for those with gluten sensitivities or celiac disease, suitable for many gluten-free recipes.

  • Rich in Protein & Fiber: Gram flour is a good source of plant-based protein and dietary fiber, contributing to satiety and digestive health.

  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera is a well-regarded brand, ensuring this gram flour is finely ground and of high quality for optimal results in your cooking and baking.

  • Economical 2kg Value Pack: Perfect for those who use besan regularly, offering great savings and ensuring you're always stocked up.

 Culinary Uses for Heera Gram Flour.

  • Savory Snacks:

    • Pakoras & Bhajis: Create crispy, deep-fried fritters with vegetables like onions, potatoes, spinach, paneer, or chillies.

    • Cheela/Pudla: Make savory, protein-rich pancakes.

    • Khandvi & Dhokla (often with other flours): A key component in these popular steamed Gujarati snacks.

    • Sev & Gathiya: Used to make these popular crunchy extruded snacks.

  • Sweets (Mithai):

    • Besan Ladoo: Classic sweet balls made with roasted besan, ghee, and sugar.

    • Besan Barfi/Mysore Pak: Rich, fudge-like sweets.

  • Curries & Gravies:

    • Thickening Agent: Used to thicken some curries and particularly Kadhi (a yogurt-based gravy).

    • Koftas: As a binder for vegetable or even some non-vegetarian koftas.

  • Batters: For coating vegetables or fish before frying.

  • Face Packs (Traditional Skincare): Besan is also traditionally used in homemade face packs for its cleansing and exfoliating properties. (Note: this product is sold as food-grade).

 Tips for Using Gram Flour.

  • Sift gram flour before use to remove any lumps and ensure a smooth batter or dough.

  • Roasting gram flour (bhuna besan) lightly in a dry pan or with a little ghee before using it in sweets like ladoos or some savory dishes significantly enhances its nutty aroma and flavor.

Name: Shan Karahi Masala 100g
Short description: Shop Shan Karahi Gosht Masala 100g at Ideal Indiska, Stockholm. Authentic Pakistani spice blend for a rich, spicy & aromatic Karahi-style meat curry. Perfect for lamb or chicken!
Description:
The Sizzling, Aromatic Flavors of a Lahori Classic

Recreate the sizzling, aromatic, and intensely flavorful experience of traditional Karahi Gosht (or Kadhai Gosht) with Shan Karahi Gosht Masala 100g. This signature spice blend from Pakistan's renowned Shan brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is expertly crafted with a unique combination of whole and ground spices, specifically designed to help you achieve the authentic taste of this beloved stir-fried meat curry.

What is Karahi Gosht?

Karahi Gosht is a popular dish from the Indian subcontinent, particularly famous in Pakistani cuisine. "Gosht" refers to meat (typically mutton, lamb, or chicken) and "Karahi" is a type of deep, circular cooking pot similar to a wok. The dish is characterized by its rich, thick gravy, with prominent flavors of fresh tomatoes, ginger, garlic, green chillies, and a robust blend of coarsely ground spices. It's traditionally cooked and served in the same karahi.

Authentic Flavour, Simplified

  • The Taste of Lahore: Shan has perfected this masala to deliver the genuine, spicy, tangy, and deeply savory notes that make Karahi Gosht a restaurant and home-cooking favorite.
  • A Perfect Blend of Spices: Contains a harmonious mix of spices like coriander, red chilli, paprika, cumin, ginger, garlic, and often larger pieces of spices like dried red chillies or crushed coriander seeds for texture and aroma. This takes the guesswork out of your seasoning.
  • Ideal for High-Heat Cooking: The masala is designed to release its flavors beautifully when cooked with meat, tomatoes, and other ingredients in a karahi or similar pan over relatively high heat.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: Simplifies the process of making this flavorful dish by providing all the essential spices in one perfectly proportioned pack.
  • Trusted Shan Quality: A household name, Shan guarantees high-quality ingredients and authentic taste in every spice blend.

How to Use Shan Karahi Gosht Masala

(Shan's packaging includes detailed, easy-to-follow recipe instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Heat oil or ghee in a karahi or heavy-bottomed pan. Sauté chopped onions (if using, some recipes omit them). Add ginger-garlic paste and cook.
  2. Add your choice of meat (mutton, lamb, or chicken pieces) and brown it on high heat.
  3. Stir in chopped fresh tomatoes (a key ingredient) and the Shan Karahi Gosht Masala. Cook, stirring frequently, until the tomatoes soften and the oil begins to separate from the masala.
  4. Add a little water or yogurt if needed, cover, and cook until the meat is tender and the gravy has thickened to coat the meat pieces.
  5. Garnish generously with fresh julienned ginger, green chillies, and chopped coriander leaves.
  6. Serve piping hot directly from the karahi with hot naan, roti, or chapati.

Name: TRS Toor Dal (Arhar Dal/Split Pigeon Peas) 1kg
Short description:
TRS Toor Dal (Arhar Dal/Split Pigeon Peas) 1kg - For Authentic Dal & Sambar

\n

A staple in every Indian kitchen, TRS Toor Dal is the key to delicious and comforting dals. This 1kg pack contains premium quality, unpolished split pigeon peas (Arhar Dal). Perfect for making classic Dal Tadka, South Indian Sambar, and sweet & sour Gujarati Dal.
Description:
The Heartbeat of the Indian Kitchen: TRS Toor Dal (Arhar Dal)

Discover the essential and most beloved lentil of Indian cuisine with TRS Toor Dal in a convenient 1kg pack. Known as Arhar Dal or Split Pigeon Peas, this versatile and nutritious lentil, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is the foundation for countless iconic dishes. From the simple, everyday Dal Tadka that comforts the soul, to the complex and tangy South Indian Sambar, Toor Dal is the indispensable ingredient for an authentic taste of home.

Understanding Toor Dal (Arhar Dal)

Taur Dal is a skinned and split pigeon pea that is celebrated for its wonderful cooking properties. When cooked, it breaks down into a beautifully thick, creamy, and flavorful soup or stew, all while carrying a mild, nutty flavor that readily absorbs the spices it's cooked with.

  • The Foundation for Countless Dals: This is the most common dal used across the length and breadth of India for making the classic "yellow dal" or Dal Tadka. Its creamy consistency is unparalleled.
  • Essential for Sambar: The authentic and traditional base for the famous South Indian lentil and vegetable stew, Sambar.
  • Rich in Protein & Fiber: A fantastic source of plant-based protein and dietary fiber, making it both a delicious and a highly nutritious part of a vegetarian or vegan diet.
  • Unpolished & Natural: (If applicable) This Toor Dal is unpolished, retaining more of its natural nutrients and authentic flavor.
  • Trusted TRS Quality: TRS is a well-regarded brand, known for its commitment to providing clean, high-quality staple ingredients for authentic cooking.

How to Cook with TRS Toor Arhar Dal

Like most dried lentils, Toor Dal needs to be cooked before it can be enjoyed.

  1. Rinse Thoroughly: Measure the desired quantity of dal and rinse it under running water 3-4 times, or until the water runs mostly clear.
  2. Soaking (Optional): Soaking the dal for 30-60 minutes can help reduce cooking time and improve digestibility.
  3. Cooking: The quickest way to cook Toor Dal is in a pressure cooker. Combine 1 part rinsed dal with 3 parts water, a pinch of turmeric powder, and a few drops of oil (to prevent frothing). Pressure cook for about 4-6 whistles, or until the dal is completely soft and mushy. It can also be cooked in an open pot, which will take longer (45-60 minutes or more).
  4. Tempering (Tadka): Once the dal is cooked, prepare a "tadka" by heating ghee or oil and sizzling spices like cumin seeds, mustard seeds, dried red chillies, garlic, and hing (asafoetida). Pour this aromatic tempering over the cooked dal.

Serve your delicious homemade dal hot with steamed Basmati rice or fresh rotis.

Short description:
Heera Red Peanuts 1kg - For Roasting, Cooking & Snacks

\n

Stock your kitchen with high-quality Heera Raw Red Peanuts! This 1kg value pack offers whole, shelled red peanuts, perfect for achieving a fresh, rich nutty flavour when roasted. An essential ingredient for tempering (tadka), making creamy chutneys and satays, blending into nut butter, or crafting homemade savoury snacks (namkeen).
Description:
The Versatile, Nutty Essential for Cooking and Snacking

Unlock the rich, earthy, and satisfying flavor of fresh peanuts with Heera Raw Red Peanuts in a generous 1kg pack. These peanuts, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, are raw and shelled, ready for you to roast, boil, or incorporate directly into your favorite recipes. Peanuts (often called groundnuts or moongphali) are a fundamental ingredient in Indian, Pakistani, Southeast Asian, and global cuisines, valued for their crunch, flavor, and high protein content.

What Makes Red Peanuts Unique?

These raw red peanuts (often Spanish or Valencia types) are distinguished by the reddish/purplish skin that covers the kernel.

  • Superior Freshness: Buying raw allows you to roast them exactly when you need them, ensuring maximum flavor and crunch, superior to pre-roasted versions.
  • Rich, Nutty Flavor: Roasting these peanuts brings out a deep, satisfying nutty taste and a fragrant aroma.
  • High Protein Content: Peanuts are an excellent source of plant-based protein, dietary fiber, and healthy fats.
  • Versatile Culinary Use: Essential for both savory dishes (like curries and stir-fries) and sweet preparations (like chikki or ladoos).
  • Value 1kg Pack: This bulk size is economical and ideal for families or those who frequently use peanuts in large quantities for making chutneys, snacks, or homemade spice mixes.

Culinary Uses for Heera Raw Red Peanuts

  • Roasting & Snacking: Dry roast or shallow fry them and toss with salt, chilli powder, or Chaat Masala for a delicious, fresh savory snack (namkeen).
  • Tempering (Tadka): Used whole or split, peanuts are a key ingredient in the tempering for Upma, Poha, and various South Indian curries and vegetable stir-fries.
  • Chutneys & Sauces: The essential base ingredient for classic peanut chutney (often served with dosa and idli), satay sauce, or African-style peanut stews.
  • Sweets: Use in traditional Indian sweets like Chikki (peanut brittle), peanut butter, or energy bars and ladoos.
  • Curry Base: Ground peanuts add richness and thickness to gravies in certain regional cuisines (e.g., Maharashtrian and some South Indian curries).

A Snack Connection for Sweden and Europe:

You can think of Heera Raw Red Peanuts as a blank slate for delicious homemade snacks. Similar to how Swedes enjoy their various forms of crunchy and salted nuts, these peanuts offer a base for crafting a uniquely South Asian 'hemmagjord snacks' that is packed with flavor and protein.

How to Roast Raw Peanuts

  1. Dry Roast: Heat a wide, heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add the peanuts and stir constantly for 8–12 minutes until they are aromatic, slightly darker, and the skin begins to flake off.
  2. Cool: Remove immediately and spread them out to cool. The residual heat will continue to cook them.
  3. Use: Rub the peanuts gently to remove the skins if desired before use.

Name: Kaalar Super Kernal Premium Basmati Rice 5kg
Short description:
Super Kernal Premium Basmati Rice

\n

Experience the authentic aroma and delicate texture of Kaalar Super Kernal Premium Basmati Rice. This 5kg value pack offers superior quality, extra-long Basmati grains that cook up fluffy, separate, and wonderfully fragrant. Ideal for creating exquisite biryanis, flavorful pulaos, or as a perfect accompaniment to your everyday Indian and Pakistani curries.
Description:
The Royal Standard of Basmati: Kaalar Super Kernal Premium Rice - 5kg.

Indulge in the unparalleled quality, exquisite aroma, and delicate, fluffy texture of Kaalar Super Kernal Premium Basmati Rice in a generous 5kg value pack. This superior Basmati variety, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is celebrated for its exceptionally long grains that elongate further upon cooking, its captivating natural fragrance, and its ability to remain separate and non-sticky, making it the connoisseur's choice for biryanis, pulaos, and fine dining.

Understanding Super Kernal Basmati Rice.
"Super Kernal" is a highly esteemed grade of Basmati rice, known for its:

  • Exceptional Grain Length & Slenderness: One of the longest Basmati grains available, it provides an elegant and appealing presentation on the plate.
  • Distinctive Natural Aroma: Possesses the signature sweet, nutty, and floral fragrance that Basmati rice is globally renowned for.
  • Fluffy & Separate Grains: When cooked correctly, each grain remains distinct, light, and fluffy, never sticky – a hallmark of premium Basmati.
  • Delicate Texture: Offers a soft and tender bite that complements a wide range of dishes.
  • Aged for Perfection (Often): Premium Basmati rice like Super Kernal is often aged for a period (e.g., 1-2 years) which enhances its aroma, flavor, and cooking characteristics (reduces stickiness and increases elongation).
  • Premium Quality by Kaalar: Kaalar selects high-grade Super Kernal Basmati to ensure a consistently superior rice experience.
  • Value 5kg Pack: This large pack is ideal for families, regular Basmati consumers, or for entertaining, offering excellent value for a premium product.

Culinary Uses for Kaalar Super Kernal Basmati Rice.

  • Biryanis & Pulaos: The ultimate choice for creating show-stopping biryanis (like Hyderabadi, Lucknowi, or Chicken Biryani) and fragrant pulaos, where the long, separate grains are essential.
  • Special Occasion Meals: Perfect for festive gatherings, celebrations, and when you want to serve the best quality rice.
  • Everyday Fine Dining: Elevates even simple everyday meals when served alongside dals, curries, and sabzis.
  • Plain Steamed Basmati: Its natural aroma and flavor shine through beautifully when served as simple steamed rice.
  • Fried Rice (Delicate Styles): Can be used for lighter, more aromatic styles of fried rice.

Cooking Instructions for Kaalar Super Kernal Basmati Rice.
(Always refer to the pack for Kaalar's specific guidance)

  1. Rinse 1 cup of Kaalar Super Kernal Basmati Rice gently in cold water 2-3 times, or until the water runs relatively clear. Avoid vigorous washing.
  2. Soak the rice in fresh water for at least 30 minutes. This allows the grains to absorb water and cook evenly, resulting in longer, fluffier rice.
  3. For an open pot method: Use approximately 1.5 to 2 cups of water for every 1 cup of soaked rice. Bring water to a boil, add the soaked and drained rice, and a pinch of salt if desired. Cook uncovered on medium-low heat until most of the water is absorbed and craters appear on the surface (about 8-12 minutes). Cover tightly, reduce heat to very low, and let it steam for another 5-10 minutes.
  4. For a closed pot/rice cooker: Follow the manufacturer's instructions, typically using a water-to-soaked-rice ratio of around 1.5:1.
  5. Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes before fluffing very gently with a fork or a flat rice paddle to separate the grains.

Ideal Indiska is proud to offer Kaalar Super Kernal Premium Basmati Rice, bringing an exceptional quality grain to your Stockholm kitchen, perfect for creating truly memorable and authentic Indian and Pakistani meals.

India Gate Classic Basmati Rice in a Value Size: India Gate Classic - 5kg

Indulge in an unparalleled culinary experience with India Gate Classic Basmati Rice in a generous 5kg value pack. This flagship offering from India's most loved and trusted Basmati rice brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is perfect for families and regular connoisseurs of fine rice. Sourced from the finest Basmati fields at the foothills of the Himalayas, each grain of India Gate Classic is handpicked, meticulously aged for a minimum of one to two years, and processed with utmost care to deliver exceptionally long, slender grains that are rich in aroma, exquisite in taste, and cook up with a beautifully fluffy and separate texture.

 The Hallmarks of India Gate Classic.
India Gate Classic is not just rice; it's a promise of quality and an authentic Basmati experience, now in a size that offers great value:

  • Aged to Perfection: The aging process is crucial for Basmati rice. It reduces moisture content, concentrates the aroma, and ensures the grains elongate significantly (often more than twice their original length) without breaking, resulting in a non-sticky, fluffy texture.
  • Extra-Long Slender Grains: Visually stunning, the long, pearly white grains create an elegant presentation on any plate.
  • Exquisite Aroma & Delicate Flavour: Delivers the signature, sweet, and nutty fragrance that true Basmati is celebrated for, along with a subtle, delicate flavor that enhances your dishes.
  • Fluffy & Perfectly Separate: Each grain cooks up distinct, light, and fluffy, never sticky, making it the ideal canvas for rich curries and flavorful biryanis.
  • The Choice of Connoisseurs: Favored by chefs and discerning home cooks who demand the best quality and authentic taste.
  • Economical 5kg Pack: Ideal for households that consume Basmati rice regularly, ensuring you always have this premium staple on hand while enjoying better value.
  • Trusted India Gate Legacy: India Gate has a long-standing reputation for providing premium quality Basmati rice.

 Culinary Masterpieces with India Gate Classic.

  • Show-Stopping Biryanis & Pulaos: Its ability to remain separate and absorb flavors makes it the ultimate choice for creating aromatic and visually appealing layered biryanis and rich pulaos.
  • Elegant Plain Steamed Basmati: Serve as a perfect, fragrant accompaniment to elevate any Indian curry, dal, or sabzi.
  • Festive & Special Occasion Rice Dishes: Ideal for Zafrani Pulao (saffron rice), wedding biryanis, and other celebratory meals where quality is paramount.
  • Refined Side Dish for Global Cuisine: Its delicate flavor and beautiful texture also complement sophisticated Middle Eastern, Persian, and other international dishes.
 Cooking Instructions for Flawless India Gate Classic Basmati.
(Always refer to the pack for India Gate's specific guidance)
  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of India Gate Classic Basmati Rice in cold water 2-3 times.
  2. Soak (Crucial): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30 minutes (up to 1-2 hours recommended for Classic variants).
  3. Cook (Open Pot): Drain soaked rice. Add to 1.5-1.75 cups boiling water with salt (optional) and oil/ghee (optional). Cook covered on low heat for 10-12 mins or until water is absorbed.
  4. Cook (Closed Pot/Rice Cooker): Use approx. 1.5 cups water per 1 cup soaked rice. Follow cooker instructions.
  5. Rest: Once cooked, let stand covered for 10-15 mins.
  6. Fluff: Gently fluff with a fork.

Cooking Instructions for Exquisite Basmati Rice

(General instructions, always refer to the pack for India Gate's specific guidance)

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of India Gate Exotic Basmati Rice in cold water until the water runs mostly clear. Handle gently to preserve the long grains.
  2. Soak (Crucial): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30-45 minutes. Soaking is essential for this premium variety to achieve its full, magnificent length.
  3. Cook (Open Pot Method): Drain the soaked rice. Add it to 1.5 to 1.75 cups of boiling water with a pinch of salt. Cook on medium-low heat, covered, for 10-12 minutes, until all water is absorbed.
  4. Cook (Closed Pot/Rice Cooker): Use approximately 1.5 cups of water for every 1 cup of soaked rice. Follow your rice cooker's instructions.
  5. Rest (Vital): Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the rice stand covered and undisturbed for 10-15 minutes. This steaming process is vital for the grains to firm up and separate perfectly.

Name: India Gate Idli Rice 5kg - For Perfectly Soft & Spongy South Indian Idlis
Short description:
Idli Rice

\n

Create authentic, perfectly soft, and fluffy South Indian Idlis at home with India Gate Idli Rice! This 5kg value pack contains premium quality, short-grain parboiled rice, specially selected for making the ideal fermented batter. The trusted choice for spongy idlis and crispy dosas.
Description:
The Authentic Foundation for Perfect South Indian Breakfasts

Unlock the secret to making truly authentic, soft, and spongy South Indian idlis and crispy dosas with India Gate Idli Rice in a generous 5kg value pack. This premium quality rice from the trusted India Gate brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a specific variety of short-grain parboiled rice. Its unique characteristics are essential for creating the perfect fermented batter that results in the light, airy texture that makes idlis a world-famous, healthy delicacy.

What Makes Idli Rice Special?

Idli Rice is not the same as Basmati or other regular eating rice. It is specifically chosen and processed for batter-making:

  • Parboiled for Perfect Fermentation: The rice is parboiled (steamed in its husk before milling), which alters its starch structure. This process is key to achieving a beautifully fermented batter that rises well and creates a soft, spongy, and porous texture in the finished idlis.
  • Ideal Starch Content: It has the right balance of starches that, when combined with Urad Dal, provides the structure needed for idlis that are soft yet hold their shape perfectly.
  • Creates White, Fluffy Idlis: India Gate Idli Rice is known for producing idlis that are appealingly white and wonderfully fluffy.
  • The Authentic Choice: While other rice can be used, using specific Idli Rice is the traditional and most reliable way to achieve restaurant-quality results at home.
  • Trusted India Gate Quality: India Gate is a leading brand renowned for its commitment to sourcing and processing high-quality rice varieties.
  • Excellent Value 5kg Pack: This large pack is perfect for families who regularly enjoy homemade South Indian breakfasts, offering great economy.

How to Use India Gate Idli Rice for Idli & Dosa Batter

This rice is the primary ingredient for creating the batter from scratch:

  1. Soaking: Typically, you would soak the India Gate Idli Rice and Urad Dal separately for at least 4-6 hours. A common ratio is 4 parts Idli Rice to 1 part Urad Dal.
  2. Grinding: Grind the soaked dal and rice separately into smooth batters using a wet grinder or a high-powered blender, adding water as needed.
  3. Combining & Fermenting: Mix the two batters together with your hands, adding salt. Cover the batter and leave it in a warm place to ferment for 8-12 hours, or overnight, until it has doubled in volume.
  4. Making Idlis: Gently stir the fermented batter. Pour into greased idli molds and steam for 10-15 minutes.
  5. Making Dosas: You can often use the same batter (sometimes thinned with a little water) to make crispy dosas on a hot tawa.

Serve hot with traditional accompaniments like Sambar, Coconut Chutney, and Idli Podi.

En Svensk Koppling: Mer Än Bara Risgrynsgröt

För våra svenska kunder kan Idli Rice verka likt grötris på grund av dess korta korn. Men medan grötris är perfekt för krämig risgrynsgröt, är Idli Rice speciellt framtaget för att jäsa och ångas. Tänk på det som ett specialiserat "brödris". Precis som du skulle använda ett specifikt mjöl för att baka det perfekta surdegsbrödet, använder man Idli Rice för att skapa de perfekta, luftiga och svampiga ångkokta riskakorna som är en hörnsten i den sydindiska frukosten. Det är en spännande ny värld av ris att utforska!

Understanding 1121 Basmati Sella Rice.

The "1121" variety is one of the world's longest Basmati grains, prized for its elongation upon cooking. The "Sella" process involves parboiling the rice in its husk before milling. This helps to:

  • Retain Nutrients: Pushes nutrients from the bran into the grain.
  • Create Firmer Grains: The grains become less prone to breaking during cooking.
  • Produce Non-Sticky, Separate Rice: Ideal for dishes where distinct grains are crucial, as it cooks up fluffier and less sticky than regular white rice.
  • Slight Golden Hue: Sella rice often has a characteristic light golden color before cooking, which turns white upon cooking.

Why Choose Kaalar 1121 Basmati Sella Rice?

  • Extraordinary Grain Length: The 1121 Basmati variety is celebrated for its exceptionally long and slender grains that elongate further upon cooking, offering an elegant presentation.
  • Fluffy & Separate Grains Guaranteed: The Sella process ensures that the rice cooks up perfectly separate, non-sticky, and fluffy every time – a hallmark of great biryani.
  • Authentic Basmati Aroma: Retains the delightful, subtle fragrance that Basmati rice is famous for.
  • Ideal for Layered Dishes: Its firm texture makes it perfect for layered biryanis and pulaos, as the grains hold their shape well.
  • Less Breakage: More resilient during cooking compared to regular white Basmati.
  • Value 5kg Pack: This large pack is perfect for families, caterers, or those who frequently prepare rice dishes, offering excellent value.

Cooking Instructions for Kaalar 1121 Basmati Sella Rice.

(Sella rice often requires slightly different cooking than regular white Basmati. Always refer to the pack for Kaalar's specific guidance.)

  • Rinse 1 cup of Kaalar 1121 Basmati Sella Rice thoroughly in cold water.
  • Soak the rice in water for at least 30-60 minutes (soaking is highly recommended for Sella rice to ensure even cooking and maximum elongation).
  • For an open pot method: Use approximately 1.5 to 2 cups of water for every 1 cup of soaked rice. Bring water to a boil, add the soaked and drained rice, and a pinch of salt if desired. Cook uncovered on medium heat until the water is absorbed and rice is tender (cooking time might be slightly longer than regular Basmati).
  • For a closed pot/rice cooker: Follow the manufacturer's instructions, adjusting water as Sella rice can sometimes absorb less water than regular white Basmati. A common ratio is around 1.5:1 water to soaked rice.
  • Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for 10-15 minutes before fluffing gently with a fork.

Specially cultivated and processed, this whole grain brown rice retains the nutrient-rich bran layer, making it an excellent choice for health-conscious individuals and families looking for a wholesome alternative to white rice.

India Gate, a trusted name in Basmati rice, brings you this "Active Health Watchers Special" variant designed to support a balanced lifestyle. Unlike polished white rice, brown Basmati rice is a good source of dietary fiber, which aids in digestion and promotes a feeling of fullness. It also typically has a lower Glycemic Index (GI) compared to white rice, helping to manage blood sugar levels more effectively.

The long, slender grains of India Gate Brown Basmati Rice cook up fluffy and separate, carrying a distinctive nutty flavor and a delightful aroma that Basmati is celebrated for. It's incredibly versatile and can be used to prepare a wide array of delicious and nutritious dishes.

Key Benefits of India Gate Brown Basmati Rice:

  • Whole Grain Goodness: Retains the bran and germ layers, offering more fiber, vitamins, and minerals than white rice.

  • High in Dietary Fiber: Promotes healthy digestion and contributes to satiety.

  • Lower Glycemic Index (Low GI): A better choice for sustained energy release and managing blood sugar levels.

  • Authentic Basmati Aroma & Flavour: Enjoy the characteristic nutty taste and pleasant fragrance that India Gate Basmati is known for.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: A suitable grain for individuals with gluten intolerance or celiac disease.

  • Versatile for Healthy Cooking: Perfect for creating healthy biryanis, pulaos, pilafs, side dishes, or as a wholesome accompaniment to curries and dals.

  • Value 5kg Pack: This generous pack size is ideal for families and regular consumers, offering great value.

Cooking Instructions:
(General instructions, but always refer to the pack for specific guidance from India Gate)

  1. Rinse the rice thoroughly in cold water 2-3 times.

  2. Soak the rice in water for at least 30 minutes (optional, but recommended for fluffier grains).

  3. For an open pot method: Use approximately 2 to 2.5 cups of water for every 1 cup of brown rice. Bring water to a boil, add the rice, and cook covered on low heat for about 35-45 minutes, or until all the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.

  4. For a closed pot/rice cooker: Follow the manufacturer's instructions, generally using a similar water-to-rice ratio.

  5. Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes before fluffing with a fork.

Description:
The Perfect Blend of Health & Aroma: India Gate Brown Basmati Rice - 1kg)

Experience the wholesome goodness and authentic Basmati fragrance with India Gate Brown Basmati Rice (Active Health Watchers Special) 1kg. This premium offering from the trusted India Gate brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a whole grain brown rice that retains its nutrient-rich bran layer. It's an excellent choice for health-conscious individuals seeking a nutritious alternative to white rice without compromising on the delightful aroma and taste that Basmati is renowned for.

 Why Choose India Gate Brown Basmati Rice?
India Gate's "Active Health Watchers Special" Brown Basmati is designed with well-being in mind:

  • Whole Grain Nutrition: Unlike polished white rice, brown Basmati rice keeps the bran and germ intact, providing significantly more dietary fiber, vitamins (like B vitamins), and minerals.

  • Rich in Dietary Fiber: The high fiber content aids in healthy digestion, promotes a feeling of fullness (satiety), and can contribute to better weight management.

  • Lower Glycemic Index (Low GI): Brown Basmati rice typically has a lower GI than white rice, meaning it releases sugar into the bloodstream more slowly, making it a better option for sustained energy and for individuals managing blood sugar levels.

  • Authentic Basmati Aroma & Nutty Flavour: Despite being a whole grain, it retains the characteristic pleasant aroma and a distinct, slightly nutty flavor that enhances any meal.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: A suitable and nutritious grain for those with gluten intolerance or celiac disease.

  • Versatile for Healthy Cooking: Perfect for creating wholesome versions of your favorite rice dishes.

  • Trusted India Gate Quality: India Gate is a leading name in Basmati rice, ensuring consistent quality and authentic taste.

Culinary Uses for India Gate Brown Basmati Rice.

  • Healthy Everyday Meals: Serve as a nutritious base with dals, curries, sabzis, and grilled vegetables or proteins.

  • Wholesome Biryanis & Pulaos: Create healthier versions of classic biryanis and pulaos, enjoying both flavor and fiber.

  • Salads & Bowls: A great addition to hearty salads or nutritious grain bowls.

  • Stir-fries: Works well in vegetable or protein stir-fries for a filling and healthy meal.

  • Side Dish: A simple yet nutritious accompaniment to a wide variety of main courses.

 Cooking Instructions for Brown Basmati Rice.
(Brown rice generally requires more water and longer cooking time than white rice. Always refer to pack instructions.)

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of India Gate Brown Basmati Rice in cold water 2-3 times.

  2. Soak (Highly Recommended): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30-60 minutes. This helps to soften the bran layer and ensures more even cooking.

  3. Cook (Open Pot): Drain the soaked rice. Add it to 2 to 2.5 cups of boiling water with a pinch of salt (optional). Cook on medium-low heat, covered, for about 35-45 minutes, or until all water is absorbed and the rice is tender and fluffy.

  4. Cook (Closed Pot/Rice Cooker): Use approximately 2 cups of water for every 1 cup of soaked rice. Follow the rice cooker's instructions, which might have a specific setting for brown rice.

  5. Rest: Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes. This allows the grains to firm up and separate.

  6. Fluff: Gently fluff the rice with a fork before serving.

 Your Stockholm Choice for Nutritious Indian Rice.

Name: India Gate Jeerakasala Rice (Kaima/Jeeraga Samba Rice) 5kg - Aromatic Short Grain for Biryani
Short description:
India Gate Jeerakasala Rice

\n

Experience the authentic taste and unique aroma of South Indian cuisine with India Gate Jeerakasala Rice (Kaima/Jeeraga Samba). This 5kg value pack offers premium quality, aromatic short to medium grain rice, renowned for its distinct fragrance (reminiscent of cumin/jeera) and its ability to cook into fluffy, flavorful grains. Ideal for Malabar Biryani, Ghee Rice, and other traditional delicacies.
Description:
The Aromatic Jewel of South India: India Gate Jeerakasala Rice - 5kg)

Unlock the secret to truly authentic and fragrant South Indian rice dishes with India Gate Jeerakasala Rice (Kaima/Jeeraga Samba Rice) in a generous 5kg value pack. This premium quality short to medium grain rice, from the trusted India Gate brand and available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is highly prized in Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Sri Lanka. It's celebrated for its unique, naturally aromatic fragrance (often likened to cumin or "jeera," hence its name), its small grain size, and its wonderful ability to absorb spices and flavors, making it the traditional choice for iconic dishes like Malabar Biryani and Ghee Rice.

 Understanding Jeerakasala / Kaima Rice)
Jeerakasala rice is distinct from Basmati and other long-grain varieties. Its characteristics make it special:

  • Unique Cumin-like Aroma: Its most defining feature is a natural, subtle fragrance that many compare to cumin (Jeera) or a delicate blend of spices, which blooms beautifully when cooked.

  • Small to Medium Grain Size: The grains are shorter and often plumper than Basmati, contributing to a different mouthfeel in dishes.

  • Excellent Flavor Absorption: The structure of the grain allows it to absorb the flavors of masalas, ghee, and other ingredients exceptionally well, resulting in deeply flavorful rice dishes.

  • Fluffy & Non-Sticky (When Cooked Right): Despite being a shorter grain, when cooked properly, it yields fluffy, separate grains perfect for biryanis and pulaos.

  • Traditional Choice for Specific Biryanis: The preferred rice for Malabar Biryani (Thalassery Biryani) and other regional South Indian and Sri Lankan biryani styles, where its unique aroma and texture are paramount.

  • Trusted India Gate Quality: India Gate is a leading brand known for its commitment to sourcing and providing high-quality rice varieties.

 Culinary Uses for India Gate Jeerakasala Rice.

  • Malabar Biryani / Thalassery Biryani: The quintessential rice for this famous, aromatic, and layered biryani from the Malabar region of Kerala.

  • Ghee Rice (Nei Choru): A simple yet incredibly flavorful dish where the rice is cooked with ghee and whole spices, allowing the Jeerakasala's aroma to shine.

  • Other South Indian Pulaos & Flavored Rice: Excellent for various coconut milk-based pulaos or lemon rice where a fragrant, non-sticky short grain is desired.

  • Everyday Meals (South Indian Style): While often used for special dishes, it can also be enjoyed as a delicious everyday rice with curries and sambar.

  • Payasam/Kheer (Sometimes): Its aromatic quality can lend itself to some rice-based sweet puddings.

Cooking Instructions.
(General instructions, always refer to the pack for India Gate's specific guidance)

  1. Rinse 1 cup of India Gate Jeerakasala Rice thoroughly in cold water until the water runs relatively clear.

  2. Soak the rice in water for 20-30 minutes (optional, but can help achieve fluffier grains).

  3. For an open pot method: Use approximately 1.5 to 2 cups of water for every 1 cup of rice (Jeerakasala often requires less water than Basmati). Bring water to a boil, add the rice, and cook covered on low heat for about 10-15 minutes, or until all water is absorbed and the rice is tender.

  4. For a closed pot/rice cooker: Follow the manufacturer's instructions, generally using a water-to-rice ratio of around 1.5:1 or 1.75:1.

  5. Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes before fluffing gently with a fork.

Your Stockholm Source for Premium South Indian Rice.
Ideal Indiska is proud to offer a diverse range of authentic Indian rice varieties, including the prized India Gate Jeerakasala Rice. This 5kg pack ensures you have a plentiful supply for creating memorable and aromatic South Indian meals in your Stockholm kitchen.

Description:
The Aromatic Essence of Basmati, Great Value: Tilda Broken Basmati Rice - 10kg)

Experience the delightful fragrance and a softer texture of Basmati rice in an economical format with Tilda Broken Basmati Rice (Tukda/Kinki Basmati) in a generous 10kg value pack. From the renowned Tilda brand, known for its premium quality Basmati, this offering available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm consists of Basmati grains that have been broken during the milling process. While shorter than full-length grains, they retain the characteristic aroma and much of the delicate flavor of Tilda Basmati, making them an excellent and affordable choice for a variety of dishes.

 Understanding Broken Basmati Rice (Tukda/Kinki)
Broken Basmati rice, often called "Tukda" or "Kinki" Basmati, offers several advantages:

  • Authentic Basmati Aroma & Flavor: These broken grains come from authentic Basmati rice, so they carry the signature fragrance and a similar taste profile to their whole-grain counterparts.
  • Softer Texture & Quicker Cooking: Broken grains tend to cook slightly faster and often result in a softer, more tender texture, which is desirable for certain dishes.
  • Economical Choice: Provides the Basmati experience at a more budget-friendly price point, making it ideal for everyday consumption or for dishes where grain length is not a primary concern.
  • Ideal for Specific Dishes: Perfect for preparations like kheer (rice pudding) where the grains break down to create a creamy consistency, or for khichdi (a rice and lentil porridge).
  • Trusted Tilda Quality: Even as broken grains, you get the assurance of quality associated with the Tilda brand, a leader in Basmati rice.
  • Value 10kg Pack: This large pack is perfect for families, regular users, or catering needs, offering significant value.

Culinary Uses for Tilda Broken Basmati Rice.

  • Kheer (Rice Pudding): The most popular use. The broken grains cook down beautifully to create a rich, creamy, and aromatic kheer.
  • Khichdi: Perfect for making soft, comforting, and easy-to-digest khichdi with lentils and vegetables.
  • Everyday Meals: An excellent choice for daily rice servings with dal, curries, or sabzis, especially if a softer rice texture is preferred.
  • Fried Rice: Works well in fried rice dishes where the grains are mixed with other ingredients.
  • Idli/Dosa Batter (Ground): Can be ground (after soaking) as part of the rice component for idli or dosa batter.
  • Soups & Porridges: Adds body and a delicate rice flavor to savory or sweet porridges and can be used to thicken some soups.
  • Stuffings: Can be used in stuffings for vegetables or meats.

 Cooking Instructions.
(General instructions, always refer to the pack for Tilda's specific guidance)

  1. Rinse 1 cup of Tilda Broken Basmati Rice thoroughly. Soaking is often not as critical as for whole grains but can be done for 15-20 minutes if desired.
  2. For an open pot method: Use approximately 1.5 to 2 cups of water for every 1 cup of broken rice. Bring water to a boil, add the rice, and cook covered on low heat for about 10-12 minutes, or until all water is absorbed and the rice is tender.
  3. For a closed pot/rice cooker: Follow the manufacturer's instructions, generally using a similar water-to-rice ratio.
  4. Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for a few minutes before serving.

Your Stockholm Source for Value & Quality Basmati.

Description:
The Iconic Taste of Pakistan: Tapal Danedar Loose Tea

Experience the truly authentic, rich, and robust flavor of South Asia's favorite daily beverage with Tapal Danedar Loose Tea 450g. Tapal is one of Pakistan's leading tea companies, and their "Danedar" (meaning granular/flaked) blend is celebrated for its strength and consistent quality. Available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, this tea is the perfect foundation for your morning cup, whether you enjoy it black or as a traditional milk tea (Chai).

What Makes Tapal Danedar Unique?

Tapal has perfected its blending process to deliver a superior tea experience:

  • Danedar (Granular) Blend: The tea leaves are processed into small granules, which helps them release their color and flavor quickly and intensely during brewing. This process ensures a robust, strong, and highly satisfying cup of tea.
  • Rich Colour and Aroma: Danedar tea is known for its beautiful, bright amber hue and a fresh, invigorating aroma that signals quality.
  • The Perfect Base for Chai: Its inherent strength makes it the ideal foundation for traditional milk tea, as it cuts through the richness of milk and sugar, resulting in a perfectly balanced chai.
  • Trusted Brand: Tapal has over 70 years of heritage, establishing itself as Pakistan's most trusted tea company.
  • Versatile Brewing: Excellent for both brewing in a teapot or for the traditional method of boiling directly in milk (doodh pati).

How to Brew the Perfect Cup of Tapal Danedar Chai

  1. Boil Water: Bring water to a vigorous boil.
  2. Add Tea: Add 1 teaspoon of Tapal Danedar Loose Tea per cup (adjust strength to your liking) to the boiling water. Let it steep for 2-3 minutes.
  3. Add Milk & Sugar: Add milk and sugar (or  Sakkar for an authentic touch). Bring the mixture back to a rolling boil.
  4. Simmer & Serve: Simmer for another minute or two to allow the flavors to meld. Strain into cups and enjoy your perfectly brewed, strong chai.
    (For Masala Chai, add a pinch of MDH T-Plus Masala or freshly crushed ginger/cardamom along with the tea leaves).

Name: KTC Pure Mustard Oil (Sarson ka Tel) 1 Litre - Pungent & Authentic Flavour
Short description:
Buy KTC Pure Mustard Oil (Sarson ka Tel) 1L | Ideal Indiska Stockholm

\nShop KTC Pure Mustard Oil 1L at Ideal Indiska, Stockholm. Authentic, pungent Sarson ka Tel for traditional Indian cooking, pickles & massage. A rich, traditional flavour!
Description:
The Authentic Pungency of Traditional Cooking

Unlock the distinctive, sharp, and deeply traditional flavors of authentic South Asian cuisine with KTC Pure Mustard Oil (Sarson ka Tel) 1L. This essential cooking oil from the trusted KTC brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a staple in many Indian and Bangladeshi kitchens. Extracted from mustard seeds, this pure oil is celebrated for its characteristic pungent aroma and strong, savory taste that adds an unmistakable character to a variety of dishes, and is also valued for its traditional use in massage.

The Unique Qualities of Mustard Oil (Sarson ka Tel)

Mustard oil is not just a cooking medium; it's an integral part of culinary heritage in many regions, especially North India, Punjab, and Bengal.

  • Pungent & Strong Flavor: KTC Pure Mustard Oil delivers that authentic, sharp, and slightly spicy kick (known as "jhaans") that is essential for many traditional recipes.
  • High Smoke Point: Mustard oil generally has a high smoke point, making it versatile and suitable for high-heat cooking methods like deep frying, stir-frying, and sautéing.
  • Essential for Pickling (Achaar): Its strong preservative qualities and distinct flavor make it the number one oil of choice for many traditional Indian pickles, from mango and lime to complex mixed vegetable pickles.
  • Traditional Massage Oil: In Ayurveda and traditional home practices, mustard oil is often warmed and used for body massage. It is believed to be a warming oil (taseer), improving circulation and helping to relieve muscle soreness, especially in colder weather.
  • Authentic KTC Quality: KTC is a well-regarded brand, ensuring you receive pure mustard oil for your culinary and traditional needs.

How to Use KTC Pure Mustard Oil in Your Kitchen

  • Traditional Cooking: The base oil for many regional vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes. It is famously used for fish curries in Bengali cuisine, like Sarson Maach (mustard fish).
  • Tempering (Tadka): Used to bloom spices for dals and curries, imparting its unique pungent flavor.
  • Frying: Ideal for shallow or deep frying snacks and savories like pakoras and samosas, giving them a characteristic taste.
  • Pickles (Achaar): The traditional and essential oil for preserving and flavoring homemade pickles.

A Swedish Connection: A Bold Flavour Alternative

För våra svenska kunder är senapsolja en spännande och smakrik upplevelse. Medan svensk rapsolja (rapeseed oil) är känd for sin neutrala smak, är KTC Senapsolja raka motsatsen – den är djärv, pepprig och full av karaktär. Tänk på den som en matlagningsolja som tillför sin egen unika, kryddiga dimension, lite som en mycket stark skånsk senap men i oljeform. Den är perfekt för att ge en autentisk och "hettande" smak till indiska rätter eller för att experimentera med när du vill ha en mer robust smak i dina stekta grönsaker eller potatis.

(Swedish Connection Translation: For our Swedish customers, mustard oil is an exciting and flavorful experience. While Swedish rapsolja (rapeseed oil) is known for its neutral taste, KTC Mustard Oil is the complete opposite – it is bold, peppery, and full of character. Think of it as a cooking oil that adds its own unique, spicy dimension, a bit like a very strong Skånsk senap (Scanian mustard) but in oil form. It's perfect for giving an authentic and "heating" flavor to Indian dishes or for experimenting with when you want a more robust taste in your fried vegetables or potatoes.)

Important Note on Cooking:
To mellow its intense pungency slightly, many traditional cooks heat the mustard oil in a pan until it just begins to smoke lightly, then let it cool for a moment before adding other ingredients. This process is highly recommended for those new to cooking with it.

Name: Khanum Butter Ghee 2kg
Short description:
Khanum Butter Ghee (Pure Clarified Butter) 2kg \n

Add a rich, nutty, and authentic flavor to your dishes with Khanum Butter Ghee! This 2kg value tin contains 100% pure clarified butter, an essential cooking fat in South Asian cuisine. With its high smoke point and delicious aroma, it's perfect for deep frying, sautéing (tadka), baking, and making traditional sweets (mithai).
Description:
The Golden Elixir of South Asian Cooking

Discover the rich, aromatic, and authentic taste that only pure ghee can provide with Khanum Butter Ghee, available in a generous 1kg value tin at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Ghee, or clarified butter, is a cornerstone of Indian and Pakistani cuisine, revered for its distinctive nutty flavor, high smoke point, and its place in traditional wellness practices. Khanum is a trusted brand that delivers a high-quality, pure ghee perfect for all your culinary needs.

What is Ghee and Why is it So Prized?

Ghee is made by simmering butter to remove the milk solids and water, leaving behind pure, golden butterfat. This process gives it several unique properties:

  • Rich, Nutty & Caramelized Flavor: The simmering process toasts the milk solids before they are removed, which imparts a deep, nutty, and slightly caramelized aroma and taste to the ghee that is far more complex than plain butter.
  • High Smoke Point: Ghee can be heated to high temperatures (approx. 250°C / 482°F) without burning or smoking. This makes it a superior and healthier choice for high-heat cooking, deep-frying, and sautéing compared to many oils or butter.
  • Lactose & Casein Free (Virtually): The clarification process removes most of the lactose and casein, making ghee a suitable option for many people with dairy sensitivities.
  • Long Shelf Life: Ghee is a stable fat that does not require refrigeration, making it a convenient pantry staple.
  • Ayurvedic & Traditional Value: In Ayurveda, ghee is considered a highly sattvic (pure) food, believed to aid digestion, nourish the body, and enhance the properties of herbs.

Why Choose Khanum Butter Ghee?

When you're searching for "pure ghee in Stockholm" or an "authentic cooking fat for Indian food," Khanum is a reliable choice.

  • 100% Pure Butter Ghee: Made from high-quality butter, ensuring an authentic and rich flavor.
  • Perfect for High-Heat Cooking: Its stability at high temperatures makes it ideal for all your frying and sautéing needs.
  • Delivers Authentic Flavor: It's the secret ingredient that gives dishes like Dal Makhani, Biryani, and Halwa their characteristic richness.
  • Value 1kg Tin: This large tin is economical and perfect for families or anyone who uses ghee regularly in their cooking.

How to Use Khanum Butter Ghee in Your Kitchen

  • Tempering (Tadka/Chaunk): The perfect medium for sizzling whole spices like cumin seeds, mustard seeds, and hing to create an aromatic base for dals and curries.
  • Deep Frying: Ideal for frying snacks like samosas, puris, or jalebis, imparting a rich flavor.
  • Sautéing & Roasting: Use it to sauté vegetables, roast potatoes, or pan-fry parathas for a golden, crispy finish.
  • Indian Sweets (Mithai): An essential ingredient for countless Indian desserts, including Gajar ka Halwa, Sooji Halwa, Ladoos, and Barfis.
  • Drizzling: A spoonful of melted ghee over hot steamed rice, dal, or fresh rotis adds a final touch of aroma and richness that is simply divine.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Cooking Staples

At Ideal Indiska, your dedicated Indian and Pakistani grocery store in Stockholm, we understand the importance of quality staples. We are proud to offer trusted brands like Khanum. With our reliable local Stockholm delivery and DHL shipping across Sweden and all of Europe, you can easily stock your pantry with the authentic ingredients that make your cooking special.

Description:
Does Your Indian Cooking Lack That Authentic, Restaurant-Quality Richness? (Problem)

Have you ever followed a recipe for Dal Makhani or Butter Chicken perfectly, only to find it's missing that deep, nutty, and satisfying aroma you remember from your favorite restaurant or your mother's kitchen? You've used the right spices, the best ingredients, but something is still not quite right. Often, the secret doesn't lie in the spices alone, but in the very foundation of the dish: the cooking fat.

The Frustration of Using the Wrong Fats (Agitation)

Using regular butter can lead to burning at the high temperatures needed for a proper "tadka," resulting in a bitter taste. Standard vegetable oils, while functional, are completely neutral; they add no character or richness, leaving your dishes tasting flat and one-dimensional. You're left with a meal that's good, but not great. It lacks the soul, the comforting aroma, and the authentic taste that defines true Indian and Pakistani cuisine.

The Solution: Unlock Authentic Flavour with Khanum Butter Ghee (Solution)

This is where Khanum Butter Ghee changes everything. Available in a convenient 500g tin at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, this is 100% pure clarified butter, the golden elixir that has been the cornerstone of South Asian cooking for centuries. Ghee isn't just oil; it's a flavor ingredient in its own right.

Why Ghee is the Answer:

  • Unmatched Nutty Flavor: The process of making ghee involves simmering butter, which toasts the milk solids before they are removed. This simple step creates a profound, nutty, and slightly caramelized flavor that infuses your entire dish with an authentic, rich taste.

  • Perfect for High-Heat Cooking: Ghee boasts a very high smoke point (around 250°C / 482°F). This means you can achieve the perfect sizzle for your tadka (tempering) with cumin seeds and hing without the risk of burning and bitterness. It's the ideal fat for deep-frying samosas or pan-frying parathas to a perfect golden-brown.

  • Aromatic & Pure: The aroma of spices blooming in pure ghee is the true scent of an authentic Indian kitchen. It's a sign that something delicious is about to happen. In Ayurveda, ghee is also considered a "sattvic" or pure food, making it the preferred choice for preparing prasad and lighting diyas during pooja.

Experience the Versatility of Khanum Ghee

This 500g tin is the perfect size for any home cook to explore the myriad uses of ghee:

  • The Foundation of Curries: Start your curries and dals by making a tadka in Khanum Ghee.
  • For Decadent Sweets (Mithai): Ghee is the essential ingredient for achieving the authentic texture and richness in sweets like Gajar ka Halwa, Sooji Halwa, and Ladoos.
  • A Flavorful Finish: Drizzle a spoonful of melted ghee over hot steamed Basmati rice or fresh, soft rotis to elevate them from simple staples to a divine experience.

Description:
The Everyday Essential: Annam Toor Dal (Arhar Dal) - 2kg)

Ensure your kitchen is always ready for delicious and nutritious Indian meals with Annam Toor Dal (Arhar Dal/Split Pigeon Peas) in a generous 2kg value pack. This staple lentil, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is one of the most widely consumed dals in India, cherished for its mild, nutty flavor, its ability to cook down to a creamy consistency, and its excellent nutritional profile. This larger pack is perfect for families and regular dal eaters.

Understanding Toor Dal (Split Pigeon Peas)
Toor Dal, also known as Arhar Dal, consists of skinned and split pigeon peas. These yellow lentils are a cornerstone of Indian vegetarian cuisine.

  • Mild & Nutty Flavor: Has a pleasant, slightly sweet and nutty taste that pairs well with a wide variety of spices and vegetables.
  • Creamy Texture When Cooked: Breaks down easily when cooked, creating a smooth, creamy texture ideal for dals and stews.
  • Rich in Plant-Based Protein: An excellent source of protein for vegetarian and vegan diets, contributing to a balanced meal.
  • Good Source of Dietary Fiber: Aids in digestion and promotes satiety.
  • Versatile Culinary Staple: The foundation for countless dal recipes, sambhar, and can be used in khichdi, soups, and even some savory snacks.
  • Quality by Annam: Annam is a trusted brand for South Asian groceries, providing quality lentils for your everyday cooking.
  • Economical 2kg Value Pack: Offers great value and convenience for households that consume dal regularly.

Culinary Uses for Annam Toor Dal

  • Everyday Dal Tadka/Fry: The most common preparation, where cooked toor dal is tempered with spices like cumin, mustard seeds, turmeric, asafoetida, and chillies.
  • Sambhar: A key ingredient in this iconic South Indian lentil and vegetable stew, typically served with idli, dosa, or rice.
  • Gujarati Dal: Often used to make the sweet, sour, and spicy Gujarati-style dal.
  • Khichdi: Combined with rice to make a comforting and nutritious one-pot meal.
  • Soups & Stews: Adds body, protein, and flavor to various vegetarian soups and stews.
  • Dal Palak/Other Vegetable Dals: Cooked with spinach or other vegetables for added nutrition and flavor.
  • Puran Poli Filling (Chana Dal is more common, but Toor Dal sometimes used): Can be used to make the sweet lentil filling for this Maharashtrian flatbread.
Cooking Instructions
  1. Rinse 1 cup of Annam Toor Dal thoroughly in water.
  2. Soak for at least 30 minutes to 1 hour (optional, but reduces cooking time and improves digestibility).
  3. Pressure cook with 2.5-3 cups of water, a pinch of turmeric, and a little salt for 4-6 whistles, or cook in an open pot until soft and easily mashed (this will take longer).
  4. Once cooked, proceed with your chosen dal recipe (e.g., by adding tempering/tadka).

Name: Patak's Tandoori Paste 312g - Authentic Spice Paste for Grilling & BBQ
Short description:
Tandoori Paste

\nRecreate authentic, restaurant-style Tandoori flavors at home with Patak's Tandoori Paste! This 312g jar contains a rich, aromatic, and tangy blend of traditional Indian spices, ginger, garlic, and tamarind. Perfect for marinating chicken, paneer, fish, or vegetables for a delicious smoky taste when grilled, baked, or barbecued.
Description:
The Authentic Taste of the Tandoor, Simplified: Patak's Tandoori Paste.

Bring the celebrated, smoky, and aromatic flavors of traditional Indian tandoori cooking into your home kitchen with Patak's Tandoori Marinade Paste 312g. This expertly crafted paste from the globally renowned Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, offers a convenient and authentic blend of spices, ginger, garlic, tamarind, and natural colorants. It's designed to create the perfect marinade for chicken, meat, fish, paneer, or vegetables, infusing them with that irresistible tandoori taste before grilling, baking, or barbecuing.

 Understanding Tandoori  Paste.
Tandoori cooking traditionally involves marinating food in a yogurt and spice mixture before cooking at high heat in a cylindrical clay oven (tandoor). Patak's Tandoori Paste provides the essential spice base for this marinade.

  • Authentic Tandoori Flavor Profile: Carefully blended to deliver the characteristic tangy, spicy (usually mild to medium), smoky, and deeply aromatic notes of classic tandoori dishes.
  • Rich Blend of Spices: Typically includes ginger, garlic, tamarind for tang, coriander, cumin, paprika (for color), garam masala spices, and other traditional Indian condiments.
  • Perfect for Marinades: Designed to be mixed, often with yogurt, to create a thick marinade that clings to food, tenderizes it, and infuses it with flavor.
  • Vibrant Natural Colour: Often imparts an appealing reddish-orange hue to the marinated food, reminiscent of tandoor-cooked dishes.
  • Versatile for Grilling, Baking, or BBQ: While traditionally for tandoor, this paste is excellent for modern cooking methods, achieving delicious results on your home grill, in the oven, or on the barbecue.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: This ready-to-use paste significantly reduces the effort of sourcing and grinding numerous individual spices for your tandoori marinade.
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's has a long-standing reputation for producing high-quality, authentic Indian food products.

 How to Use Patak's Tandoori Paste.

Patak's jars typically provide detailed usage instructions and recipe ideas.

  1. For approximately 4 servings, mix 2-3 tablespoons of Patak's Tandoori Marinade Paste with 2-3 tablespoons of plain yogurt (optional, but traditional for creaminess and tenderizing). You can also add a squeeze of lemon juice.
  2. Score your chosen protein (e.g., chicken pieces, paneer cubes, fish fillets) or prepare your vegetables (e.g., cauliflower florets, bell pepper chunks, onions).
  3. Coat the protein/vegetables thoroughly with the tandoori marinade.
  4. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1-2 hours, or preferably for several hours (or overnight for meat) to allow the flavors to penetrate deeply.
  5. Cook as desired:
  • Grill/BBQ: Thread onto skewers and grill over medium-hot coals or on a barbecue until cooked through and nicely charred.
  • Oven: Bake in a preheated oven (around 200°C/400°F) until cooked through.
  • Pan-Fry: Can also be pan-fried.
  • Serve hot with naan, rice, salad, and mint chutney.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic Patak's Marinades.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a wide range of Patak's authentic cooking pastes, including this flavorful Tandoori Marinade Paste. Make your grilling and BBQ sessions in Stockholm extra special with the taste of India!

  • Brand: Patak's
  • Product: Tandoori Marinade Paste
  • Weight/Volume: 312g
  • Form: Thick Spice Paste
  • Flavor Profile: Tangy, Aromatic, Smoky (implied tandoori), Mildly to Medium Spicy
  • Key Ingredients: Water, Ginger Purée, Ground Spices (Coriander, Cumin, Paprika, Spices), Tamarind, Salt, Acids (Acetic Acid, Lactic Acid), Garlic Powder, Colours (E160c, E122, E129 - it is CRUCIAL to list specific colorants as per packaging). (This is a typical Patak's Tandoori Paste ingredient list - verify and list accurately from the specific product packaging you stock.)
  • Allergen Information: May contain Mustard, Peanuts, and Nuts. (Always check the specific product packaging for the most accurate and complete allergen information from Patak's).
  • Dietary Information: Vegetarian (Often Vegan, but verify if any dairy derivatives are present, though usually not in the paste itself).
  • Origin: (Typically UK for Patak's products)
  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place. Once opened, refrigerate and use within a specified period (e.g., 6 months, or by best before date – check jar).

Name: Patak's Madras Curry Paste 283g - Hot & Tangy South Indian Spice Blend
Short description:
Patak's Madras Curry Paste

\nIgnite your taste buds with the authentic, fiery heat of Patak's Madras Curry Paste! This 283g jar contains a rich and aromatic blend of chillies, coriander, cumin, tamarind, and other classic South Indian spices. Perfect for creating robust and tangy Madras curries with chicken, lamb, beef, or vegetables.
Description:
The Fiery Flavours of South India: Patak's Madras Curry Paste.

Experience the bold, vibrant, and satisfyingly spicy taste of authentic South Indian cuisine with Patak's Madras Curry Paste 283g. This expertly crafted paste from the globally renowned Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, captures the essence of a traditional Madras curry – a rich, hot, and tangy blend of aromatic spices, chillies, and tamarind. It's the perfect base for creating deeply flavorful curries that pack a delightful punch.

 Understanding Madras Curry.
Originating from the city of Madras (now Chennai) in South India, Madras curry is characterized by its generous use of chillies, a distinct tanginess (often from tamarind), and a complex blend of spices that typically includes coriander, cumin, turmeric, fenugreek, and mustard seeds.

  • Authentic Hot & Tangy Flavor: Patak's Madras Paste is carefully balanced to deliver a genuine Madras curry taste that is robustly spicy, tangy, and deeply aromatic.
  • Rich Blend of Spices & Chillies: Features a classic mix of ground spices, with a prominent chilli content for significant heat, complemented by the sourness of tamarind and the earthiness of other traditional spices.
  • Versatile for Various Proteins & Vegetables: While excellent for Chicken Madras or Lamb Madras, this paste also creates wonderfully spicy and flavorful curries with beef, fish, prawns, or a medley of vegetables.
  • Builds a Deeply Flavored Base: Provides a concentrated flavor foundation, allowing you to create restaurant-quality Madras curry at home with ease.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: This ready-to-use paste significantly reduces preparation time, eliminating the need to source, toast, and grind numerous individual spices.
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's has a rich heritage in producing authentic Indian pastes, pickles, and sauces, trusted by cooks around the world for consistent quality and taste.

 How to Use Patak's Madras Curry Paste.

Patak's jars typically provide recipe suggestions. A general guide is below.

  1. Heat a little oil in a pan. Sauté chopped onions until soft (optional).
  2. Add your choice of diced chicken, meat, seafood, or vegetables and stir-fry for a few minutes.
  3. Stir in Patak's Madras Curry Paste (typically 2-3 tablespoons per serving, adjust to your heat preference and as per jar instructions) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, ensuring it doesn't burn.
  4. Add other ingredients like chopped tomatoes, coconut milk (to temper heat if desired), or stock to create your desired sauce consistency. Bring to a simmer.
  5. Cover and cook gently until your main ingredients are tender and cooked through, and the sauce has thickened.
  6. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
  7. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, roti, or idiyappam (string hoppers).

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic & Spicy Patak's Pastes.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a range of Patak's authentic curry pastes, including this fiery and flavorful Madras Curry Paste. Spice up your meals and explore the bold tastes of South India in your Stockholm kitchen!
