Name: Patak's Korma Curry Paste 283g
Short description:
Patak's Korma Curry Paste

\nCreate an authentic, mild, and creamy Korma effortlessly with Patak's Korma Curry Paste! This 283 g jar contains a delicate blend of coconut, aromatic spices, and herbs, expertly formulated by Patak's. Perfect for making delicious Chicken Korma, Lamb Korma, or a flavorful Vegetable Korma. Just add your protein/veg, cream/yogurt, and simmer!
Description:
The Authentic Taste of Mild & Creamy Korma: Patak's Korma Curry Paste

Whip up a deliciously mild, creamy, and aromatic Korma curry, a beloved classic from the Indian subcontinent, with the convenience of Patak's Korma Curry Paste 283 g. This expertly blended paste from the globally recognized Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, combines delicate coconut with a subtle mix of traditional spices like ginger, garlic, cardamom, and coriander to create the authentic, rich, and subtly sweet flavor profile that Korma is famous for.

Understanding Korma Curry
Korma is a type of curry characterized by its mildness, creamy texture (often from yogurt, cream, or coconut milk), and aromatic, often slightly sweet, spice blend. It's a popular choice for those who prefer less heat but still want a deeply flavorful Indian dish.

  • Authentic Mild & Creamy Flavor: Patak's Korma Paste is carefully balanced to deliver a genuine Korma taste that is rich, aromatic, and not overly spicy, making it suitable for the whole family.
  • Key Ingredient: Coconut: Coconut is a traditional and essential ingredient in many Korma recipes, lending a subtle sweetness and creamy texture, which is well-represented in this paste.
  • Versatile for Various Proteins & Vegetables: Perfect for creating classic Chicken Korma, Lamb Korma, Prawn Korma, or a delicious Navratan (nine-gem) Vegetable Korma.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: This ready-to-use paste significantly reduces preparation time, eliminating the need to grind and blend multiple individual spices.
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's has a long heritage of producing authentic Indian pastes, pickles, and sauces, trusted by cooks worldwide.
  • A Crowd-Pleasing Dish: Korma's mild and creamy nature makes it a universally appealing curry, great for entertaining or as a comforting family meal.

How to Use Patak's Korma Curry Paste

(Patak's jars typically provide recipe suggestions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Heat a little oil in a pan and sauté onions until soft (optional).
  2. Add your choice of diced chicken, lamb, prawns, or mixed vegetables and stir-fry for a few minutes.
  3. Stir in the Patak's Korma Curry Paste (typically 2-3 tablespoons per serving, adjust to taste and as per jar instructions) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
  4. Add water, stock, coconut milk, or yogurt/cream to create the sauce. Bring to a simmer.
  5. Cover and cook gently until your protein/vegetables are tender and cooked through, and the sauce has thickened slightly.
  6. Garnish with fresh coriander, flaked almonds, or a swirl of cream if desired.
  7. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti.

Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic Patak's Curry Pastes
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a range of Patak's authentic curry pastes, including this popular Korma Curry Paste. Making delicious, restaurant-quality Indian meals at your Stockholm home has never been easier!

Name: Patak's Garam Masala Paste 283g - Aromatic & Warming Indian Spice Paste
Short description:
Patak's Garam Masala Paste.

\n

Add authentic Indian warmth and complex aroma to your dishes with Patak's Garam Masala Paste! This 283g jar contains a perfectly balanced blend of traditional Garam Masala spices in a convenient paste form. Ideal for easily incorporating into curries, dals, lentil soups, marinades, or as a flavorful finishing touch
Description:
The Heart of Indian Flavour, in a Convenient Paste: Patak's Garam Masala Paste. 

Experience the quintessential aromatic warmth of traditional Indian Garam Masala in an easy-to-use paste with Patak's Garam Masala Paste 283g. This innovative product from the globally trusted Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, blends a harmonious selection of classic Garam Masala spices into a convenient paste, ready to infuse your curries, dals, marinades, and other dishes with a rich, complex, and deeply satisfying flavor.

 Understanding Garam Masala Paste.
Garam Masala, meaning "hot/warming spice blend," is a cornerstone of Indian cuisine, typically used as a finishing powder. Patak's Garam Masala Paste offers the same aromatic profile in a different format:

  • Authentic Garam Masala Flavours: Carefully blended to deliver the characteristic warm, sweet, and savory notes of spices like cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, cumin, coriander, and black pepper.
  • Convenient Paste Form: Unlike dry powder, this paste can be easily stirred into dishes during various stages of cooking, blending seamlessly with other ingredients. It may also contain oil or other wet ingredients that help carry the spice flavors.
  • Aromatic & Warming: Imparts a deep, comforting aroma and a gentle warming sensation (not necessarily chilli heat) to your food, characteristic of good Garam Masala.
  • Enhances a Wide Range of Dishes: Perfect for adding a final flourish of authentic Indian flavor to vegetarian and non-vegetarian curries, lentil preparations, rice dishes, and even some snacks.
  • Time-Saving Solution: Saves you from grinding individual whole spices, while offering a more integrated flavor than simply sprinkling powder at the end (depending on how it's used).
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's commitment to authentic flavors ensures this paste delivers a genuine taste experience.

How to Use Patak's Garam Masala Paste.

Patak's jars typically provide usage suggestions. A general guide is below.

  • During Cooking: Stir a tablespoon or two (to taste) into your curry, dal, or vegetable dish towards the middle or end of the cooking process to allow the flavors to meld.
  • In Marinades: Add to yogurt or oil-based marinades for chicken, lamb, paneer, or vegetables to infuse them with a warm, aromatic spice.
  • As a Finishing Touch: While a paste, a small amount can still be stirred in right before serving to boost the aroma.
  • Sauté with Onions & Tomatoes: Can be added along with ginger and garlic paste when sautéing the base for your curries.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Innovative Patak's Products.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a range of Patak's authentic Indian cooking solutions, including this versatile Garam Masala Paste. Simplify your cooking and enhance the flavor of your Indian meals in Stockholm with this convenient and aromatic paste.

Name: Heera Kalonji Seeds 300g
Short description:
Heera Kalonji (Nigella Seeds/Black Onion Seeds) 300g - Pungent & Aromatic Spice

\n

Add a unique, pungent, and slightly peppery flavor to your dishes with Heera Kalonji (Nigella Seeds). This 300g pack contains small, black, tear-drop shaped seeds, essential for sprinkling on naan bread, flavouring authentic Indian pickles (achaar), and for use in tempering (tadka) for various curries and vegetable dishes.
Description:
The Distinctive Pungent Seed of Flavour

Discover the uniquely aromatic and pungent flavour of Heera Kalonji (Nigella Seeds) in a generous 300g pack. This essential spice, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, consists of small, black, tear-drop shaped seeds that are often mistakenly called "black onion seeds" or "black caraway," but are in fact from the Nigella sativa plant. Kalonji is a staple in Indian, Pakistani, Middle Eastern, and Polish cuisines, prized for its distinctive taste that is a complex blend of oregano, black pepper, and a slight bitterness.

Understanding Kalonji (Nigella Seeds)

Kalonji has a rich history both in the kitchen and in traditional wellness practices. Its flavour is best released when it is dry-roasted or bloomed in hot oil.

  • Unique Pungent Flavor: Offers a complex taste that is sharp, slightly bitter, peppery, and highly aromatic. It should not be confused with black sesame seeds or black cumin.
  • Essential for Naan Bread: One of its most iconic uses is being sprinkled on top of naan bread before baking, where it imparts a beautiful look and a characteristic pungent aroma.
  • Key Pickling Spice: A vital component in many traditional Indian pickles (achaar), especially those with mango, lime, and chillies. It's a key ingredient in the famous Bengali five-spice blend, Panch Phoron.
  • Used in Tempering (Tadka): In some regional cuisines, kalonji is added to hot oil or ghee along with other spices to create a flavorful base for lentil dishes (dals) and vegetable curries (sabzis).
  • Traditional Wellness Beliefs: In Islamic and Ayurvedic traditions, Kalonji (often called "black seed") is highly revered and believed to possess numerous health benefits.
  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera provides high-quality, clean kalonji seeds, ensuring authentic taste and aroma for your culinary needs.
  • Value 300g Pack: This larger pack is perfect for those who use Kalonji regularly in their cooking or for making pickles.

Culinary Uses for Heera Kalonji

  • Breads: Sprinkle generously over naan, parathas, or other flatbreads before cooking.
  • Pickles (Achaar): A fundamental spice in the masala for a wide variety of Indian pickles.
  • Vegetable Dishes (Sabzis): Adds a unique flavor to stir-fried or slow-cooked vegetable dishes, especially with potatoes, pumpkin, or eggplant.
  • Lentil Dishes (Dals): Used in the tempering (tadka) for some regional dal preparations.
  • Spice Blends: A key ingredient in Bengali Panch Phoron.
  • Savory Pastries & Snacks: Can be sprinkled on or mixed into the dough for savory snacks like mathri or kachori.

Tips for Using Kalonji

  • To bring out its full flavour, lightly dry-roast the seeds in a pan for a minute or two until they become fragrant before using.
  • Use in moderation, as its flavor is quite strong and can become overpowering if used in excess.

Name: TRS Javentri (Whole Mace Blades) 50g - Aromatic & Delicate Spice
Short description:
TRS Javentri (Whole Mace Blades) 50g

\n

Add a warm, delicate, and subtly sweet-spicy aroma to your gourmet dishes with TRS Javentri (Whole Mace). This 50g pack contains the lacy, red-orange outer covering of the nutmeg seed, an essential spice in rich biryanis, kormas, kebabs, and authentic Mughlai cooking.
Description:
The Delicate Sibling of Nutmeg: TRS Javentri (Whole Mace)

Discover the elegant and aromatic secret of gourmet Indian and Pakistani cooking with TRS Javentri (Whole Mace Blades) 50g. This unique and prized spice, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is the lacy, net-like covering (known as an aril) that surrounds the nutmeg seed. While related to nutmeg, Mace has its own distinct character – a warmer, more delicate, and slightly sweeter flavor, often described as a combination of cinnamon and pepper with a floral hint.

The Allure of Whole Javentri (Mace)

Mace is a cornerstone of rich, aromatic cuisines, particularly Mughlai, and is valued by discerning chefs for its sophisticated flavor profile.

  • Warm & Aromatic Flavor: Imparts a complex, warm, and fragrant aroma that is both sweet and savory. It is less pungent than nutmeg, offering a more refined and delicate note.
  • Essential for Royal Cuisine (Mughlai): A key ingredient in the spice blends for authentic Shahi Korma, Biryani, Pulao, and rich meat dishes. It adds a signature royal fragrance.
  • Perfect for Kebabs: Ground mace is often used in the marinades for succulent kebabs, like Galouti or Seekh Kebabs, for its unique flavor and aroma.
  • Component of Garam Masala: A vital part of many traditional and homemade Garam Masala blends, contributing to their complexity.
  • Superior Freshness in Whole Form: Keeping mace in its whole blade form preserves its volatile oils and delicate aroma far better than pre-ground powder. Grind just what you need for the freshest, most potent flavor.

How to Use TRS Javentri (Mace)

  • For Grinding: Lightly toast a blade of Javentri in a dry pan for a few seconds to make it more brittle and enhance its aroma. Let it cool, then grind in a spice grinder or with a mortar and pestle. A small amount is usually sufficient.
  • For Infusion: You can add a whole blade or a large piece to the oil/ghee during the initial tempering (tadka) or directly into the simmering liquid of a korma or biryani preparation to infuse the dish with its aroma. It is typically removed before serving.

Short description:
Chakoli (Crispy Rice Flour Spirals) 200g - Savory Indian Snack

Enjoy the irresistible, crunchy goodness of Haldiram's Chakoli! This 200g pack offers a classic Indian savory snack (namkeen), featuring spiral-shaped, deep-fried crisps made from rice flour and a blend of mild spices. Perfectly crunchy and delicious, it's the ideal snack for tea time, parties, or any time cravings strike.
Description:
The Irresistible Crunchy Spiral: Haldiram's Chakoli

Experience the delightful and satisfying crunch of a true Indian classic with Haldiram's Chakoli in a convenient 200g pack. This beloved savory snack ("namkeen") from India's iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a deep-fried, spiral-shaped crisp made primarily from rice flour and a subtle blend of traditional spices. Its unique shape, incredible crunch, and savory flavor make it an all-time favorite snack.

What is Chakoli (Chakli/Murukku)?

Chakoli, known as Chakli in Maharashtra and Murukku in South India, is a traditional snack cherished across the country, especially during festivals like Diwali. It's made from a dough of rice flour, sometimes mixed with other flours like gram flour (besan) or urad dal flour, which is then extruded into its characteristic spiral or coil shape and deep-fried to golden perfection.

  • Incredibly Crunchy & Crispy: The hallmark of a good Chakole is its satisfyingly loud crunch, and Haldiram's delivers on this perfectly.
  • Mildly Spiced & Savory: Seasoned with a gentle blend of spices that often includes cumin, sesame seeds, and a hint of chilli, making it flavorful but not overly spicy. It's a taste that is loved by all ages.
  • Authentic Traditional Snack: Captures the authentic taste and texture of homemade Chakolee, saving you the effort of making this intricate snack from scratch.
  • The Perfect Tea-Time Companion (Chai Time): Its savory and crunchy nature makes it an ideal partner for a hot cup of Indian tea or coffee.
  • Great for Parties & Gatherings: A crowd-pleasing snack that is perfect to serve to guests or to include in a festive snack platter.
  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: From a brand renowned for its commitment to quality, hygiene, and authentic Indian flavors.

How to Enjoy Your Haldiram's Chakoli

Haldiram's Chakoli is a ready-to-eat snack that is perfect for any occasion:

  • Enjoy it straight from the pack for a satisfying, crunchy treat.
  • Serve with tea, coffee, or a cold beverage as an evening snack.
  • Add it to your "namkeen" platter when hosting guests.
  • Pack it as a convenient and tasty on-the-go snack.

Name: Haldiram's Besan Ladoo (Gram Flour Sweet Balls) 400g - Traditional Indian Mithai
Short description:
Haldiram's Besan Ladoo

\nIndulge in the timeless taste of Haldiram's Besan Ladoo! This 400g pack offers rich, melt-in-your-mouth sweet balls made from roasted gram flour (besan), pure ghee, and sugar, lightly flavored with cardamom. A beloved traditional Indian mithai (sweet), perfect for festivals, celebrations, or as a delightful treat.
Description:
The Classic Taste of Celebration: Haldiram's Besan Ladoo

Experience the authentic and heartwarming sweetness of a true Indian classic with Haldiram's Besan Ladoo 400g, a signature mithai from India's renowned Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. These golden, spherical delights are crafted from slow-roasted gram flour (besan), pure ghee (clarified butter), and sugar, creating a uniquely nutty, rich, and melt-in-your-mouth texture that has been cherished for generations.

 

 What Makes Haldiram's Besan Ladoo So Special?

Besan Ladoo is more than just a sweet; it's a symbol of joy, celebration, and tradition in Indian culture. Haldiram's stays true to the authentic recipe, ensuring every ladoo is a bite of bliss.

  • Authentic Recipe & Taste: Haldiram's uses a traditional recipe, perfectly roasting the besan to develop its characteristic nutty aroma and flavor, then skillfully blending it with high-quality ghee and the right amount of sweetness.

  • Melt-in-Your-Mouth Texture: The slow roasting and careful preparation result in a ladoo that is delightfully soft and crumbly, dissolving beautifully on the palate.

  • Rich & Nutty Flavor: The combination of roasted gram flour and pure ghee imparts a distinctive, rich, and satisfyingly nutty taste.

  • Subtly Aromatic: Often lightly flavored with cardamom (elaichi) and sometimes garnished with nuts like pistachios or almonds, adding a delicate fragrance.

  • Perfect for All Occasions: Whether it's Diwali, Raksha Bandhan, a wedding, a birth announcement, or simply a desire for a delicious traditional sweet, Besan Ladoo is always a welcome treat.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Enjoy the consistent quality, hygiene, and authentic taste that Haldiram's is famous for.

 Suggestion: Serving Your Besan Ladoo

Haldiram's Besan Ladoos are ready to eat and enjoy:

  • Serve them as a dessert after a meal.

  • Offer them to guests during festive gatherings or celebrations.

  • Enjoy one with a cup of tea or coffee for a delightful afternoon treat.

  • They make a wonderful gift for friends and family.

 Your Stockholm Destination for Authentic Indian Sweets
Ideal Indiska is proud to bring you a wide selection of authentic Indian sweets (mithai) from trusted brands like Haldiram's. Indulge in the traditional taste of Haldiram's Besan Ladoo and make any occasion sweeter in Stockholm.

This convenient ready-to-cook offering from India's iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, brings you classic, plain, multi-layered whole wheat flatbreads that cook up soft on the inside and delightfully flaky and golden on the outside.

 The Versatility of Home Style Paratha.
"Home Style" Parathas are the quintessential plain, layered flatbreads that form a staple accompaniment to many Indian meals. Their simple yet delicious character makes them incredibly versatile.

  • Authentic Layered & Flaky Texture: Haldiram's aims to replicate the traditional texture of homemade parathas, with subtle layers that become flaky and slightly crisp when cooked.

  • Simple, Wholesome Taste: Made primarily from whole wheat flour (atta), offering a comforting, mildly nutty, and savory flavor that pairs well with a vast array of dishes.

  • Ready to Cook Convenience: These frozen parathas save you the time and effort of kneading dough, rolling, and layering from scratch.

  • Cooks from Frozen in Minutes: Easily cooked on a tawa (griddle) or flat pan directly from the freezer.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: From a brand renowned for its authentic Indian foods, ensuring a quality product.

  • The Perfect Accompaniment: An ideal partner for all types of Indian curries (vegetarian or non-vegetarian), dals (lentil preparations), sabzis (vegetable dishes), yogurt, pickles, or even with a simple dollop of butter or ghee.

 Easy Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  1. Do Not Thaw: Cook directly from frozen for best results.

  2. Heat Tawa/Pan: Preheat a flat pan or tawa over medium heat.

  3. Cook Paratha: Place the frozen Home Style Paratha on the heated tawa. Cook for about 1-2 minutes on one side, or until golden brown spots appear.

  4. Flip & Cook: Flip the paratha. Add a little ghee, butter, or oil around the edges and on top (optional, for extra crispness and flavor). Cook the other side for another 1-2 minutes, pressing gently with a spatula, until it's golden brown, flaky, and cooked through. The paratha may puff up slightly.

  5. Serve Hot: Serve immediately while warm and flaky.

Your Stockholm Go-To for Classic Frozen Indian Breads.

Description:
The Perfect Companion to Every Indian Meal: Malik Foods Plain Paratha

Discover the ultimate convenience and authentic taste of a classic Indian flatbread with Malik Foods Frozen Plain Paratha, available in a generous pack of 20 pieces. This ready-to-heat offering, stocked at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, brings you simple, soft, and wholesome plain parathas that taste just like they were made in a home kitchen. They are the perfect, versatile canvas for your favourite curries, dals, and sabzis, making every meal complete.

What is a Plain Paratha? Your Quick Guide

Paratha is a staple unleavened flatbread from the Indian subcontinent, and the "Plain Paratha" is its most fundamental and versatile form.

  • What is the difference between a Paratha and a Roti/Chapati? While both are flatbreads, a paratha is typically slightly thicker and more substantial than a chapati. It is often made by folding the dough with a little ghee or oil and is pan-fried, which gives it a softer texture with a slightly crisp exterior. A roti or chapati is usually thinner and can be puffed up on a flame.
  • What does it taste like? A good plain paratha has a mild, wholesome, wheaty flavor, making it the perfect neutral partner to soak up the rich flavors of a curry.

Why Choose Malik Foods Frozen Plain Parathas?

When you're searching for "easy Indian bread for curry" or a "quick side for Dal Makhani," frozen parathas are the perfect solution.

  • Ultimate Convenience: Saves you the time and effort of making dough, resting it, and rolling. Enjoy hot, fresh-tasting parathas in minutes, directly from your freezer.
  • Authentic Home-Style Texture: Crafted to cook up soft and pliable, with a satisfying, slightly chewy bite – just like a homemade paratha.
  • Incredibly Versatile: The plain flavor makes this paratha the perfect partner for literally any Indian or Pakistani dish. It doesn't compete with the main course; it complements it.
  • Generous 20-Piece Pack: This value pack is ideal for families, for stocking up your freezer, or for when you're hosting a dinner party.
  • Trusted Brand: Malik Foods is a brand known for providing quality staple ingredients for authentic South Asian cooking.

How to Cook Frozen Parathas & What to Serve Them With

Simple Cooking Instructions:

  1. Heat Tawa/Pan: Preheat a flat pan or tawa over medium heat. Do not thaw the paratha.
  2. Cook Paratha: Place the frozen Plain Paratha on the heated tawa. Cook for about 1-2 minutes on one side.
  3. Flip & Finish: Flip the paratha. You can add a teaspoon of ghee or oil to achieve a crispier, golden-brown finish. Cook for another 1-2 minutes, pressing gently with a flat spatula, until it's cooked through.

Perfect Pairings (What to eat with Plain Paratha):
Plain parathas are the ultimate all-rounder. Serve them with:

  • Rich Dals: Perfect for scooping up Dal Makhani or Dal Tadka.
  • Vegetable Curries: A fantastic companion to Paneer Butter Masala, Aloo Gobi, or Mixed Vegetable Korma.
  • Meat & Chicken Curries: Ideal for dishes like Chicken Karahi or Lamb Korma.
  • Breakfast: Enjoy with a simple side of plain yogurt (dahi), your favourite mango pickle (achaar), or even wrapped around an omelette.

Your Stockholm & Europe-Wide Source for Convenient Indian Breads

At Ideal Indiska, we are your trusted Indian and Pakistani grocery store in Stockholm. We understand that convenience should not mean compromising on authentic taste. That's why we offer quality frozen products like Malik Foods Plain Parathas. With our reliable local Stockholm delivery and DHL shipping across Sweden and all of Europe, you can easily stock your freezer with the essentials for a delicious and complete meal.

 The Authentic Punjabi Samosa Experience.

Punjabi Samosas are cherished for their crispy, flaky pastry and their hearty, savory filling. Haldiram's captures this traditional essence:

  • Classic Savory Filling: Features a delicious and traditional filling of mashed potatoes and tender green peas, perfectly seasoned with a blend of aromatic Indian spices like cumin, coriander, garam masala, and ginger.
  • Crispy Golden Pastry: Designed to fry or bake to a delightful golden brown with a satisfyingly crispy and flaky texture.
  • Ultimate Convenience - Ready to Cook: These frozen samosas save you the intricate and time-consuming process of making samosa pastry and filling from scratch.
  • Cooks from Frozen: Easily cooked by deep frying, baking, or air frying directly from the freezer for a quick and impressive snack.
  • Generous Value Pack - 1.4kg: This large pack is perfect for stocking up for parties, family gatherings, or for households that frequently enjoy samosas. 
  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Haldiram's is a globally recognized name for quality, hygiene, and authentic Indian snacks and sweets.
  • Perfect Appetizer or Snack: An ideal starter for Indian meals, a crowd-pleasing party food, or a delicious tea-time indulgence.

 Easy Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  • Deep Fry (Recommended for best results): Heat oil in a deep pan or kadai to medium-hot. Gently slide the frozen samosas into the hot oil (do not overcrowd). Fry on medium-low heat for about 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. Drain on absorbent paper.
  • Bake (Healthier Option): Preheat oven to around 200°C (400°F). Lightly brush or spray the frozen samosas with oil. Place them on a baking tray and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and heated through, flipping them halfway.
  • Air Fry: Preheat your air fryer. Lightly brush or spray the frozen samosas with oil. Cook according to your air fryer's instructions (typically around 180-200°C for 12-18 minutes), flipping halfway, until golden and crispy.

Serve hot with mint chutney, tamarind chutney, or your favorite Indian dipping sauces.

The Authentic Samosa Experience in Miniature

These mini samosas pack all the traditional flavor into a convenient, bite-sized form that is sure to be a hit with guests of all ages.

  • Classic Savory Filling: Features a delicious and traditional filling, typically a blend of spiced potatoes, peas, and other vegetables, perfectly seasoned for a balanced and authentic taste.

Easy Cooking Instructions

(Always refer to the A1 packaging for precise instructions.)

  • Deep Fry (Recommended for best results): Heat oil in a deep pan or kadai to medium-hot. Gently slide the frozen cocktail samosas into the hot oil. Fry on medium-low heat for about 6-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. Drain on absorbent paper.

Short description:
TYJ Spring Roll Pastry 

\n

Create perfect, standard-sized spring rolls at home with Spring Home TYJ Spring Roll Pastry! This pack contains 50 sheets of 190mm authentic Singaporean frozen pastry. Famously thin, strong, and easy to use, it fries up to a delightfully crispy, golden finish.
Description:
The Perfect Wrapper for Classic Spring Rolls: Spring Home TYJ Pastry 190mm (50 Sheets)

Unlock the authentic taste and texture of your favourite Asian appetizers with Spring Home TYJ Spring Roll Pastry (190mm, 50 Sheets), the world-renowned pastry from Singapore, now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. This standard 190mm size is the go-to choice for classic, appetizer-sized spring rolls, ensuring a perfect balance of crispy pastry to delicious filling in every bite.

Why Choose Spring Home Pastry?

Famous among chefs and home cooks alike, this high-quality frozen pastry is incredibly easy to work with and guarantees a light, crispy, and non-greasy result.

  • Ideal Standard Size (190mm): Perfect for making traditional spring rolls, crispy samosas, and a variety of other appetizers.
  • Generous 50-Sheet Count: This pack provides ample pastry for larger batches, parties, or for stocking your freezer.
  • Easy to Separate & Use: The sheets are designed to be easily separated after thawing, without tearing, making the rolling process smooth and hassle-free.
  • Strong & Pliable: Holds a variety of fillings securely without breaking.

How to Use Spring Home Spring Roll Pastry

  1. Thaw: Remove the desired number of pastry sheets from the freezer and let them thaw at room temperature for about 30 minutes, or until they are pliable. Keep the remaining pastry frozen.
  2. Keep Moist: While working, keep the stack of thawed pastry covered with a damp cloth to prevent them from drying out.
  3. Fill & Roll: Place a single pastry sheet on a flat surface. Place your filling, fold, and roll tightly. Seal the final edge with a little water or a cornflour-water slurry.
  4. Fry: Deep fry in hot oil (around 180°C / 350°F) for 2-3 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
  5. Drain & Serve: Serve hot with your favorite dipping sauces like sweet chilli sauce or soy-ginger dip.

  • Crispy Money Bags (Tung Thong): Create bite-sized, purse-shaped appetizers with a savory filling.
  • Sweet Dessert Rolls: Fill with bananas and chocolate, sweet red bean paste, or even apple pie filling, then fry and dust with powdered sugar.
  • Baked Appetizers: Can also be brushed with oil and baked in the oven for a healthier, though less traditional, crispy snack.

How to Use Spring Home Spring Roll Pastry

  1. Thaw: Remove the desired number of pastry sheets from the freezer and let them thaw at room temperature for about 30 minutes, or until they are pliable. Keep the remaining pastry frozen.
  2. Keep Moist: While working, keep the stack of thawed pastry covered with a damp cloth to prevent them from drying out.
  3. Fill & Roll: Place a single pastry sheet on a flat surface. Place your filling, fold, and roll tightly. Seal the final edge with a little water or a cornflour-water slurry.
  4. Fry: Deep fry in hot oil (around 180°C / 350°F) for 2-3 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
  5. Drain & Serve: Serve hot with your favorite dipping sauces like sweet chilli sauce or soy-ginger dip.

Simply heat them on a tawa (griddle) for a delicious, crispy, and satisfying accompaniment to any Indian meal.

 What is Lachha Paratha?.

Lachha Paratha is a popular North Indian flatbread known for its distinct, visible layers ("lachha" means layers or spirals). It's typically made by rolling and folding dough multiple times with ghee or oil, which creates its characteristic flaky and slightly crispy texture when cooked.

  • Authentic Multi-Layered & Flaky Texture: Haldiram's aims to replicate the traditional texture of homemade Lachha Parathas, with delightful, separable layers that are both soft and crispy.

  • Deliciously Buttery Flavor (Typically): Often made with ghee or oil incorporated into the layers, giving them a rich and satisfying taste.

  • Ultimate Convenience - Ready to Cook: These frozen parathas save you considerable time and effort involved in dough preparation, rolling, and layering.

  • Cooks from Frozen in Minutes: Easily cooked on a tawa or flat pan directly from the freezer.

  • Value Pack - 1.2kg: This large pack is perfect for families, gatherings, or for those who enjoy parathas regularly, offering great value.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Haldiram's is a renowned name for quality and authentic Indian foods, ensuring a delightful paratha experience.

  • Versatile Accompaniment: The perfect partner for a wide variety of Indian curries, dals, sabzis, yogurt, or pickles.

 Simple Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  1. Do Not Thaw: Cook directly from frozen for best results.

  2. Heat Tawa/Pan: Preheat a flat pan or tawa over medium heat.

  3. Cook Paratha: Place the frozen Lachha Paratha on the heated tawa. Cook for about 1-2 minutes on one side, or until golden brown spots appear.

  4. Flip & Cook: Flip the paratha and add a little ghee or oil around the edges and on top (optional, for extra crispness and flavor). Cook the other side for another 1-2 minutes, pressing gently with a spatula, until it's golden brown and cooked through. The layers should start to become visible.

  5. Serve Hot: Traditionally, Lachha Parathas are lightly crushed by clapping them between your palms just before serving to further separate the layers. Serve immediately.

 The Timeless Appeal of Aloo Tikki.

Aloo Tikki, meaning "potato patty" or "potato cutlet," is a beloved street food and snack across India. It's known for its soft interior and often crispy exterior, with a delightful blend of spices.

  • Classic Comfort Food Flavor: Haldiram's captures the traditional, comforting taste of Aloo Tikki, with a balanced blend of spices that is flavorful yet generally mild.

  • Ready to Cook Convenience: These frozen tikkis save you the significant time and effort of boiling, mashing, seasoning, and shaping potato patties from scratch.

  • Multiple Cooking Options: Can be shallow fried for a softer exterior, deep fried for ultimate crispness, or air fried/baked for a healthier option.

Easy Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  • Deep Fry (Traditional & Crispiest): Heat oil in a pan or kadai. Gently slide the frozen Aloo Tikkis into the hot oil. Fry on medium heat, turning occasionally, for about 5-7 minutes or until golden brown and crispy on both sides. Drain on absorbent paper.

Experience the taste that has been a cherished part of countless memories with the Maggi 2-Minute Masala Noodles Family Pack (6 x 70g). Available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, this is more than just instant noodles; it's a bowl of comfort, a quick and delicious meal, and a nostalgic flavour loved by millions across India and the world. Perfect for students, busy professionals, and families, Maggi Masala Noodles are the ultimate go-to for a satisfying meal that's ready in a flash.

The Iconic Maggi Masala Taste

For decades, the magic of Maggi has been its simplicity and its unforgettable flavour. This pack brings you that classic experience:

  • Signature Masala Tastemaker: The heart and soul of the dish. Each pack comes with a sachet of the secret "Tastemaker" masala, a unique and aromatic blend of roasted Indian spices that creates a delicious, savory, and slightly spicy broth.
  • Ready in Just 2 Minutes: True to its name, Maggi noodles are incredibly quick and easy to prepare. Just boil, add the noodles and tastemaker, and your delicious meal is ready.
  • Versatile Culinary Canvas: While delicious on its own, Maggi is famous for being a base for creativity. Customize it by adding your favourite vegetables like peas, carrots, and onions, or make it more substantial with a fried egg, paneer, or tofu.
  • Convenient Family Pack: This pack contains 6 individual servings of 70g each, making it perfect for stocking your pantry and ensuring you always have a quick meal option on hand.
  • A Taste of Home & Nostalgia: For many in the Indian and Pakistani diaspora, the taste of Maggi Masala Noodles is a comforting reminder of home, childhood, and happy times.

Simple Cooking Instructions

  1. Break the noodle cake into four pieces.
  2. Add the noodles and the Tastemaker sachet to approximately 210ml (1.5 teacups) of boiling water.
  3. Cook for 2 minutes in an open pan, stirring occasionally. Do not drain the remaining water.
  4. Let it stand for 1 minute after cooking. Serve hot and enjoy!

Your Stockholm Source for a Quick Taste of Home

At Ideal Indiska, we know that sometimes you just need a quick, easy, and comforting meal. That's why we're proud to stock the classic Maggi 2-Minute Masala Noodles. Grab this family pack and bring a smile to everyone's face, right here in Stockholm.

Name: Alibaba Pheni (Fine Fried Vermicelli Strands) 150g - For Festive Sweets
Short description:
Alibaba Pheni

\nCreate delightful traditional sweets with Alibaba Pheni! This 150g pack contains delicate, crispy, and flaky fried vermicelli-like strands. Perfect for making classic Pheni Kheer (with sweetened milk) or soaking in sugar syrup, especially cherished during festivals like Eid and Karva Chauth.
Description:
The Delicate Festive Treat: Alibaba Pheni (Fine Fried Vermicelli Strands)

Indulge in a traditional South Asian delicacy with Alibaba Pheni (Fine Fried Vermicelli Strands) 150g. This unique festive food, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, consists of fine, flaky, and crispy strands, typically made from flour and ghee, which are then deep-fried. Pheni is most famously enjoyed as a sweet dish, either soaked in warm, sweetened milk (like a kheer) or drizzled with sugar syrup, and is particularly popular during celebrations like Eid, Karva Chauth, and Teej.

Understanding Pheni (Phenian)

Pheni, also known as Phenian or Sutarfeni (though Sutarfeni can sometimes refer to a slightly different preparation), is a labor-intensive sweet to make from scratch. Alibaba Pheni offers a convenient way to enjoy this special treat:

  • Delicate & Flaky Texture: The hallmark of good Pheni is its incredibly light, flaky, and layered texture, resembling fine vermicelli or shredded pastry.
  • Crispy When Dry, Soft When Soaked: Enjoyed for its initial crispness which then beautifully softens when immersed in milk or syrup.
  • Traditional Festive Food: Deeply rooted in the culinary traditions of India and Pakistan, especially during religious festivals and special occasions.
  • Versatile Sweet Base: While often enjoyed simply with sweetened milk or syrup, it can also be garnished with nuts, cardamom, or saffron.
  • Convenient & Ready to Prepare: Alibaba provides the pre-fried Pheni strands, saving you the intricate process of making them from scratch.

How to Prepare & Enjoy Alibaba Pheni

The most common ways to enjoy Pheni are:

  1. Pheni with Sweetened Milk (Pheni Kheer/Seviyan Style):

  • Warm milk and sweeten it with sugar (or Amritsari Sakkar for a traditional touch) to your taste.
  • Optionally, add a pinch of cardamom powder or a few strands of saffron to the milk for extra flavor and aroma.
  • Gently crush the Alibaba Pheni strands slightly and add them to a bowl.
  • Pour the warm, sweetened milk over the Pheni. Let it soak for a few minutes until it softens.
  • Garnish with slivered almonds, pistachios, and raisins if desired. Serve warm.

  1. Pheni with Sugar Syrup (Sheera):

  • Prepare a light sugar syrup (sheera) by dissolving sugar in water and simmering it. You can flavor it with cardamom or rose water.
  • Arrange the Pheni strands on a plate and drizzle the warm sugar syrup over them.
  • Garnish with chopped nuts.

A Taste of Celebration in Stockholm

Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer Alibaba Pheni, allowing you to bring the joy and tradition of this special festive sweet to your home in Stockholm. Perfect for sharing with family and friends during celebratory times or as a unique dessert.

Product Details:

  • Brand: Alibaba
  • Product: Pheni (Fine Fried Vermicelli Strands)
  • Weight: 150g
  • Form: Dry, Fried, Flaky Vermicelli-like Strands
  • Flavor Profile (Plain): Neutral, Crispy (Becomes sweet when prepared as a dessert)
  • Key Uses: Making Pheni with milk (Pheni Kheer), Pheni with sugar syrup, Festive Sweets
  • Ingredients: (Typically Wheat Flour (Maida), Edible Vegetable Oil/Ghee, Water. It is crucial to list ingredients as accurately as possible from the packaging.)
  • Allergen Information: Contains Wheat (Gluten). May be fried in oil that processes nuts. (Always check the specific product packaging for the most accurate allergen information).

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in an airtight container to maintain crispness and prevent it from absorbing moisture or odors. Handle gently as the strands are delicate.

Experience a taste of tradition and pure refreshment with Hamdard Rooh Afza Syrup in a generous 800ml bottle. This iconic beverage concentrate, with a heritage spanning over a century and available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a beloved household name across India and Pakistan. Rooh Afza, meaning "Soul Refresher," is a unique and masterful blend of fruits, vegetables, herbs, and the distinct essence of rose, creating a sweet, aromatic, and beautifully crimson sharbat that is perfect for cooling down on a hot day or adding a special touch to desserts.

The Unmistakable Taste of Rooh Afza

Rooh Afza is more than just a drink; it's a nostalgic experience and a cultural icon, especially during summer and the holy month of Ramadan.

  • Unique Floral & Fruity Flavor: The signature taste of Rooh Afza is a complex symphony of sweet rose, fragrant kewra, and hints of various fruits and herbs. It's a taste that is instantly recognizable and cherished by millions.
  • Beautiful Crimson Colour: When mixed, it imparts a beautiful, inviting red hue to your beverages.
  • Incredibly Versatile Syrup: While most famously mixed with water or milk, its uses are boundless.
  • Cooling & Refreshing: Traditionally consumed to combat the heat of summer, providing a cool and revitalizing experience.
  • A Heritage Brand: Hamdard Laboratories has been producing Rooh Afza since 1907, making it a brand with an unparalleled legacy of trust and quality.
  • Ideal for Family & Gatherings: The 800ml bottle is perfect for making large pitchers of sharbat for family, guests, or Iftar gatherings.

How to Enjoy Hamdard Rooh Afza

The possibilities are endless:

  • Classic Rooh Afza Sharbat: Mix 2-3 tablespoons of Rooh Afza syrup into a tall glass of chilled water. Add a squeeze of lemon or lime for extra zest.
  • Rooh Afza Milkshake: Blend Rooh Afza with cold milk and ice cream for a rich and delicious milkshake.
  • In Lassi: Add a spoonful to plain or sweet lassi for a beautiful color and unique flavor.
  • As a Dessert Topping: Drizzle over kulfi, ice cream, puddings, or fruit salads.
  • For Falooda: The essential syrup for creating the traditional layered dessert, Falooda, with vermicelli, basil seeds, jelly, and ice cream.
  • Flavoring for Sweets: Can be used to add color and flavor to some homemade sweets or custards.

Your Stockholm Source for Iconic South Asian Beverages

Name: Heera Idli Mix 1kg - Instant Mix for Soft & Spongy South Indian Idlis
Short description:
Heera Instant Idli Mix

\n

Enjoy authentic, soft, and spongy South Indian idlis effortlessly with Heera Idli Mix! This 1kg pack provides a convenient instant mix of rice and lentil flours, expertly blended by Heera for perfect results. Just add water (and sometimes yogurt/curd) to create a smooth batter for delicious homemade idlis in minutes. Ideal for a quick, healthy breakfast or light meal.
Description:
Authentic South Indian Idlis, Effortlessly: Heera Instant Idli Mix - 1kg)

Whip up delicious, soft, and perfectly spongy South Indian idlis in a fraction of the traditional time with Heera Idli Mix 1kg. This convenient instant mix from the trusted Heera brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is expertly blended with the right proportions of high-quality rice flour and urad dal (lentil) flour, along with other necessary ingredients. It takes the hassle out of soaking, grinding, and lengthy fermentation, allowing you to enjoy this beloved healthy breakfast or snack anytime.

The Convenience of Heera Idli Mix.

Idli is a classic steamed rice cake, a staple in South Indian cuisine. MTR's Idli Mix offers:

  • Authentic Idli Flavor & Texture: Crafted to deliver the genuine taste and characteristic soft, spongy, and porous texture of traditional idlis.

  • Instant & Easy to Prepare: Simply mix with water (and often a little yogurt/curd as per pack instructions) to form a smooth batter. No soaking, grinding, or long fermentation periods are required, though a short resting time for the batter is often recommended.

  • Consistent Results: Heera's precise formulation ensures you get perfectly steamed, fluffy idlis every time.

  • Saves Significant Time & Effort: Ideal for busy mornings, quick meals, or satisfying those sudden idli cravings without the extensive traditional preparation.

  • A Healthy Breakfast/Snack Option: Idlis are naturally low in fat, steamed (not fried), and easily digestible, making them a nutritious choice.

  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera is a well-regarded brand, ensuring consistent quality for your culinary needs.

  • Value 1kg Pack: This larger pack is perfect for families or those who enjoy idlis regularly.

 How to Make Perfect Idlis with Heera Idli Mix?

(Heera packaging will provide clear, step-by-step instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Take the required amount of Heera Idli Mix in a bowl.

  2. Gradually add water (and sour yogurt/curd if specified on the pack), mixing continuously to form a smooth, lump-free batter of pouring consistency (thicker than dosa batter).

  3. Many instant mixes recommend letting the batter rest for 10-30 minutes.

  4. Lightly grease your idli molds.

  5. Gently stir the batter once more and pour it into the greased idli molds.

  6. Steam in an idli cooker or a steamer for approximately 10-15 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the center of an idli comes out clean.

  7. Let the idlis rest for a minute or two after steaming before demolding.

  8. Serve hot with sambar, coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and/or idli podi (milagai podi).

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Instant South Indian Breakfast Solutions.
Ideal Indiska is delighted to bring you convenient and authentic solutions like Heera Idli Mix, making it easy to enjoy traditional South Indian delicacies in your Stockholm home. This 1kg pack offers great value for many delicious and healthy meals.

Name: Heera Idli Rava (Rice Rava/Cream of Rice) 1kg - For Soft & Fluffy South Indian Idlis
Short description:
Heera Idli Rava | Rice Rava

\n

Make perfect, soft, and fluffy South Indian idlis every time with Heera Idli Rava (Rice Rava). This 1kg pack offers premium quality, coarsely ground rice (cream of rice) specifically processed for authentic idli preparation. Just mix with urad dal batter for a delicious and healthy breakfast or snack.
Description:
The Secret to Perfectly Soft Idlis: Heera Idli Rava - 1kg)

Create authentic, light, and delightfully fluffy South Indian idlis in your own kitchen with Heera Idli Rava (Rice Rava/Cream of Rice) in a convenient 1kg pack. This premium quality product from the trusted Heera brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is specifically made from coarsely ground rice grains, processed to the ideal texture required for making traditional soft and spongy idlis.

 Understanding Idli Rava.

Idli Rava, also known as Rice Rava or Cream of Rice, is different from regular rice flour or semolina (sooji). It's made by grinding parboiled rice into a coarser texture than flour, which is essential for achieving the characteristic porous and fluffy structure of perfect idlis.

  • Specifically for Idlis: Processed to give your idlis the desired softness and spongy texture.

  • Premium Quality Grains: Heera is known for its commitment to quality ingredients, ensuring this Idli Rava is made from good quality rice.

  • Consistent Texture: Provides a uniform granulation for reliable and consistent results in your idli batter.

  • Simplifies Idli Making: Using Idli Rava often streamlines the traditional idli-making process, typically requiring less soaking and grinding time for the rice component compared to using whole rice grains.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: Made from rice, it's a naturally gluten-free ingredient, suitable for those with gluten sensitivities.

  • A South Indian Staple: An essential ingredient for anyone who loves preparing authentic South Indian breakfast delicacies at home.

 How to Use Heera Idli Rava.

Idli Rava is primarily used in combination with ground urad dal (split black lentils) to make idli batter:

  1. Soak Urad Dal: Wash and soak urad dal for about 4-6 hours.

  2. Grind Urad Dal: Grind the soaked urad dal to a very smooth, airy, and fluffy batter, adding water gradually as needed.

  3. Prepare Idli Rava: Wash the Heera Idli Rava thoroughly under running water. Some recipes suggest a brief soak (15-30 minutes) followed by draining and gently squeezing out excess water, while others use it directly after washing.

  4. Combine & Ferment: Mix the ground urad dal batter with the prepared Idli Rava. Add salt to taste. The batter consistency should be thick yet pourable (like a thick pancake batter). Cover and let the batter ferment in a warm place for 8-12 hours, or overnight, until it has risen and appears bubbly and aerated.

  5. Steam Idlis: Gently stir the fermented batter. Pour into greased idli molds and steam for 10-15 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.

  6. Serve hot with traditional accompaniments like sambar, coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and idli podi (milagai podi).

Beyond Idlis.
While its primary purpose is for idlis, some creative cooks might experiment with Idli Rava in other steamed preparations where a similar texture is desired.

 Your Stockholm Source for Authentic South Indian Staples)

Name: Vandevi Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g
Short description:
Vandevi Compounded Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g - Authentic & Pungent Spice

\n

Add an authentic, savory, and pungent depth to your Indian dishes with Vandevi Hing (Asafoetida) Powder. This 50g container holds a strong, aromatic compounded asafoetida, an essential spice for tempering (tadka) in dals, enhancing vegetable curries, and aiding digestion. A must-have in every Indian spice box.
Description:
The Unmistakable Aroma of Authentic Indian Cooking

Unlock a fundamental flavor of authentic Indian cuisine with Vandevi Compounded Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g. This iconic spice from the trusted Vandevi brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a cornerstone of vegetarian cooking. It is prized for its strong, pungent, and savory aroma that blossoms when cooked, adding a deep, umami-rich flavor that wonderfully mimics the depth of onions and garlic. Just a tiny pinch is enough to transform a simple dish into a flavorful masterpiece.

Understanding Hing (Asafoetida): A Cook's Secret Weapon

Hing is a dried latex (gum oleoresin) exuded from the root of several species of Ferula, a perennial herb. While its raw aroma is famously potent, its true magic is revealed in the cooking process.

  • What is "Compounded" Hing? Pure asafoetida resin is incredibly strong. Vandevi's "compounded" hing is expertly blended with edible flour (like rice or wheat flour) and often turmeric (which can give it a yellowish tint). This traditional process makes the potent resin easy to use in powdered form and helps to evenly distribute its flavor.
  • The Ultimate Flavor Enhancer: It adds a deep, savory, umami-rich flavor that is especially crucial in vegetarian and lentil-based dishes where onions and garlic may not be used (such as in Jain or certain Sattvic cooking).
  • Essential for Tempering (Tadka): A key ingredient in the "tadka" or "chaunk." Adding a pinch of hing to hot oil or ghee along with other spices like cumin or mustard seeds creates an incredibly aromatic and flavorful foundation for dals, sambar, and curries.
  • Aids Digestion (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Hing is highly valued for its carminative properties. It is traditionally used to help reduce bloating and gas, making it an almost inseparable partner for legumes and lentils to improve their digestibility.

Why Choose Vandevi Hing?

When you search for authentic Indian spices in Stockholm, a quality hing is non-negotiable. Vandevi is a well-recognized and trusted brand for high-quality, potent Hing. Its strength and purity mean that this small 50g container will last for a considerable time in a home kitchen, making it a very economical choice for adding immense flavor.

How to Use Vandevi Hing

Remember, Hing should always be cooked to release its true flavor. Never sprinkle it raw on finished dishes.

  1. Heat your ghee or oil in a pan for tempering (tadka).
  2. Once the oil is hot, add a tiny pinch (often just the amount you can pick up on the tip of a knife) of Vandevi Hing Powder.
  3. It will sizzle and become aromatic in just a few seconds. Immediately add your other whole spices (like cumin seeds) and proceed with your recipe by adding lentils, vegetables, etc.

It is a classic addition to almost all lentil dishes (dals), many vegetable curries (sabzis), beans (like chana masala), and in the masala for savory snacks and pickles.

Your Authentic Spice Source in Stockholm & Europe

At Ideal Indiska, we are committed to bringing you the essential ingredients that define authentic cooking. Vandevi Hing is one of those secret ingredients that makes all the difference. We are proud to offer this essential spice with delivery across Stockholm, Sweden, and all of Europe.

Naturally gluten-free, Bajri flour is cherished for its distinctive nutty flavor and its use in making hearty flatbreads and other wholesome dishes, particularly popular in the cuisines of Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, and other parts of India.

 Why Choose TRS Bajri Flour?


Bajri (Pearl Millet) is celebrated for its health benefits and unique culinary characteristics:

  • Nutrient-Dense: A good source of dietary fiber, protein, iron, magnesium, and other essential minerals and antioxidants.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: An excellent and traditional alternative to wheat flour for those with celiac disease or gluten intolerance.

  • Distinctive Earthy & Nutty Flavor: Imparts a unique, slightly sweet, and robust nutty taste to preparations, different from wheat or rice flour.

  • Ideal for Traditional Flatbreads: The most common use is for making Bajra ki Roti or Rotla, a rustic, unleavened flatbread that is both filling and nutritious.

  • Warming Properties (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Bajra is often considered to have warming properties, making it a favored grain during colder months.

  • Good for Blood Sugar Management (Low GI): Pearl millet generally has a lower glycemic index compared to refined grains, which can be beneficial for managing blood sugar levels.

  • Trusted TRS Quality: TRS is known for providing quality ethnic food ingredients, ensuring this Bajri flour is finely milled and consistent.

 Culinary Uses for TRS Bajri Flour)

  • Bajra ki Roti / Rotla / Bhakri: The most classic preparation – unleavened flatbreads cooked on a tawa (griddle), often served with ghee, jaggery, garlic chutney, or vegetable curries.

  • Porridge (Bajra Khichdi/Raab): Cooked with water or buttermilk and sometimes spices to make a nutritious porridge.

  • Mixed Flour Breads: Can be combined with other flours like whole wheat atta or jowar flour to make multi-grain rotis or a_tta_ for added nutrition and flavor.

  • Ladoos & Sweets (Selectively): In some regional cuisines, bajri flour is roasted and used to make energy-boosting ladoos or other traditional sweets.

  • Thickening Agent (Occasionally): Can be used in small amounts to thicken some rustic stews or gravies.

  • Gluten-Free Baking: Can be incorporated into gluten-free baking recipes for bread, muffins, or cookies, usually in combination with other gluten-free flours and binding agents for best results.

Tips for Cooking with Bajri Flour)

  • Bajri flour dough can be a bit tricky to handle as it lacks gluten. Using warm water for kneading often helps make the dough more pliable.

  • Patting out the rotis by hand is a traditional method, or you can roll them gently between sheets of plastic or parchment paper.

  • Cook Bajri rotis on a well-heated tawa until lightly browned and cooked through.

 The Coarse Semolina Advantage

While fine semolina is great for smooth preparations, coarse semolina (often called Moti Suji, meaning "pearl semolina") offers unique benefits:

  • Adds Extra Crispness: When used in batters or as a coating for fried items, the coarse grains provide a superior, crunchy texture.
  • Absorbs Flavors While Retaining Texture: Soaks up the flavors of spices and liquids without breaking down completely and becoming mushy.
  • Versatile Kitchen Ingredient: A staple in many Indian households for a variety of breakfast dishes, sweets, and snacks.
  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera is a well-regarded brand, ensuring consistent granulation and quality for your culinary needs.

Culinary Uses for Heera Semolina Coarse

  • Upma: The quintessential choice for making fluffy, separate-grained savory breakfast porridge with vegetables and spices.
  • Rava Ladoo: The perfect base for making these popular sweet balls with ghee, sugar, and nuts.

Name: Annam Idli Rava 1kg
Short description:
Annam Idli Rava (Rice Rava)

\n

Make perfect, soft, and fluffy South Indian idlis every time with Annam Idli Rava (Rice Rava). This 1kg pack offers premium quality, coarsely ground rice (cream of rice) specifically processed for authentic idli preparation. Just mix with urad dal batter for a delicious and healthy breakfast or snack.

\n
Description:
The Secret to Perfectly Soft Idlis: Annam Idli Rava - 1kg)

Create authentic, light, and delightfully fluffy South Indian idlis in your own kitchen with Annam Idli Rava (Rice Rava/Cream of Rice) in a convenient 1kg pack. This premium quality product from the trusted Annam brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is specifically made from coarsely ground rice grains, processed to the ideal texture required for making traditional soft and spongy idlis.

 Understanding Idli Rava.
Idli Rava, also known as Rice Rava or Cream of Rice, is different from regular rice flour or semolina (sooji). It's made by grinding parboiled rice into a coarser texture than flour, which is essential for achieving the characteristic porous and fluffy structure of perfect idlis.

  • Specifically for Idlis: Processed to give your idlis the desired softness and spongy texture.
  • Premium Quality Grains: Annam is known for its commitment to quality ingredients, ensuring this Idli Rava is made from good quality rice.
  • Consistent Texture: Provides a uniform granulation for reliable and consistent results in your idli batter.
  • Simplifies Idli Making: Using Idli Rava often streamlines the traditional idli-making process, typically requiring less soaking and grinding time for the rice component compared to using whole rice grains.
  • Naturally Gluten-Free: Made from rice, it's a naturally gluten-free ingredient, suitable for those with gluten sensitivities.
  • A South Indian Staple: An essential ingredient for anyone who loves preparing authentic South Indian breakfast delicacies at home.

 How to Use Annam Idli Rava.

Idli Rava is primarily used in combination with ground urad dal (split black lentils) to make idli batter:

  1. Soak Urad Dal: Wash and soak urad dal for about 4-6 hours.
  2. Grind Urad Dal: Grind the soaked urad dal to a very smooth, airy, and fluffy batter, adding water gradually as needed.
  3. Prepare Idli Rava: Wash the Annam Idli Rava thoroughly under running water. Some recipes suggest a brief soak (15-30 minutes) followed by draining and gently squeezing out excess water, while others use it directly after washing.
  4. Combine & Ferment: Mix the ground urad dal batter with the prepared Idli Rava. Add salt to taste. The batter consistency should be thick yet pourable (like a thick pancake batter). Cover and let the batter ferment in a warm place for 8-12 hours, or overnight, until it has risen and appears bubbly and aerated.
  5. Steam Idlis: Gently stir the fermented batter. Pour into greased idli molds and steam for 10-15 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
  6. Serve hot with traditional accompaniments like sambar, coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and idli podi (milagai podi).

 Beyond Idlis.
While its primary purpose is for idlis, some creative cooks might experiment with Idli Rava in other steamed preparations where a similar texture is desired.

 Your Stockholm Source for Authentic South Indian Staples.
Ideal Indiska is proud to offer essential ingredients like Annam Idli Rava, enabling you to create authentic and delicious South Indian breakfast classics in your Stockholm kitchen. This 1kg pack ensures you have plenty for many enjoyable meals.

Name: Vandevi Hing Yellow Powder (Asafoetida) 50g
Short description:
Vandevi Yellow Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g - Pungent & Aromatic Spice

\n

Add an authentic, savory, and pungent depth to your Indian dishes with Vandevi Yellow Hing (Asafoetida) Powder. This 50g container holds a strong, aromatic compounded asafoetida, an essential spice for tempering (tadka) in dals, enhancing vegetable curries, and aiding digestion. A must-have in every Indian spice box.
Description:
The Unmistakable Aroma of Indian Cooking

Unlock a fundamental flavor of authentic Indian cuisine with Vandevi Yellow Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g. This iconic spice from the trusted Vandevi brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a cornerstone of vegetarian cooking, prized for its strong, pungent, and savory aroma that mimics the depth of onions and garlic. Just a tiny pinch, bloomed in hot oil or ghee, is enough to transform a simple dish into a flavorful masterpiece.

Understanding Hing (Asafoetida)

Hing is a dried latex (gum oleoresin) exuded from the rhizome or tap root of several species of Ferula, a perennial herb. In its raw form, it has a very strong, pungent smell, but once cooked, it mellows into a beautiful savory flavor. Vandevi's Yellow Hing is a compounded asafoetida, meaning the potent resin is blended with edible flour (like rice or wheat flour) and turmeric (which gives it the yellow color) to make it easier to use in powdered form.

  • The Ultimate Flavor Enhancer: Adds a deep, savory, umami-rich flavor that is especially crucial in vegetarian and lentil-based dishes where onions and garlic may not be used.
  • Essential for Tempering (Tadka/Chaunk): A key ingredient in the initial tempering process. Adding a pinch of hing to hot oil or ghee along with other spices like cumin or mustard seeds creates an incredibly aromatic and flavorful base for dals and curries.
  • Aids Digestion (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Hing is highly valued for its carminative properties. It is traditionally used to help reduce bloating, and gas, and aid in the digestion of legumes and lentils, which is why it's almost always added to dal preparations.
  • Potent & Economical: A little goes a very long way. A 50g container will last for a considerable time in a home kitchen.
  • Trusted Brand: Vandevi is a well-recognized and trusted brand for high-quality, potent Hing.

How to Use Vandevi Yellow Hing

Always cook Hing to release its true flavor. Never sprinkle it raw on finished dishes.

  1. Heat your ghee or oil in a pan for tempering (tadka).
  2. Once the oil is hot, add a tiny pinch (often just the amount you can pick up on the tip of a knife) of Vandevi Hing Powder.
  3. It will sizzle and become aromatic in just a few seconds. Immediately add your other whole spices (like cumin seeds) and proceed with your recipe by adding lentils, vegetables, etc.

It's a classic addition to almost all lentil dishes (dals), many vegetable curries (sabzis), beans (like chana masala), and in the masala for savory snacks and pickles.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Indian Spices

At Ideal Indiska, we understand that authentic flavors come from authentic ingredients. Vandevi Yellow Hing is one of those secret ingredients that makes all the difference in Indian cooking. We are proud to offer this essential spice to our community in Stockholm.

  • Aids Digestion (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Hing is highly valued for its carminative properties. It is traditionally used to help reduce bloating, and gas, and is considered essential for making legumes and lentils more digestible.

Short description:
Deepak Maida

\nA versatile kitchen staple, Deepak Maida (All-Purpose/Refined Wheat Flour) is essential for a variety of Indian and global recipes. Description:
The All-Rounder Flour for Your Culinary Creations: Deepak Maida - 500g)

Unlock a wide range of baking and cooking possibilities with Deepak Maida (All-Purpose/Refined Wheat Flour) 500g. This essential pantry staple, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a finely milled, refined wheat flour known for its smooth texture and versatility. It's the go-to flour for many traditional Indian breads, snacks, sweets, as well as a key ingredient in numerous international baking recipes.

Understanding Maida (Refined Wheat Flour)
Maida is produced from the endosperm of the wheat grain after the bran and germ have been removed. This results in a fine, white flour with a high gluten content (when derived from hard wheat), which gives elasticity to doughs.

  • Fine & Smooth Texture: Deepak Maida is finely milled to ensure a smooth consistency, ideal for delicate pastries, soft breads, and lump-free batters.

  • Versatile for Baking: The preferred flour for many cakes, cookies, pastries, and biscuits due to its ability to produce a light and tender crumb.

  • Essential for Indian Breads & Snacks: The key ingredient for making popular Indian breads like Naan, Bhatura, Roomali Roti, and for the crispy casings of Samosas, Kachoris, and some types of Puris.

  • Used in Sweets (Mithai): A component in various Indian sweets, such as Gulab Jamun (often in combination with khoya), Jalebi, and some types of barfi.

  • Thickening Agent: Can be used to thicken sauces, gravies, and soups, though cornflour is often preferred for a clearer finish.

  • Quality by Deepak: Deepak brand aims to provide consistent quality maida for your everyday cooking and baking needs.

Culinary Uses for Deepak Maida.

  • Indian Breads: Naan, Bhatura, Kulcha, Roomali Roti.

  • Fried Snacks: Samosas (for the pastry), Kachoris, Spring Rolls (for wrappers), some types of Pakoras (for a crispier coating in combination with besan).

  • Sweets (Mithai): Gulab Jamun, Jalebi, Balushahi, Nankhatai, some types of Barfi.

  • Baking: Cakes (like classic sponge cakes), cookies, biscuits, muffins, pastries, pie crusts.

  • Batters: For coating items before frying to achieve a crispy texture.

  • Sauces & Gravies: As a thickening agent (though use sparingly to avoid a pasty taste).

  • Noodles & Pasta (Homemade): Can be used as a base for making fresh noodles or pasta.

 Tips for Using Maida

  • Sift maida before use, especially for baking, to aerate it and remove any lumps.

  • When making doughs for breads like naan or bhatura, proper kneading is essential to develop the gluten for a soft and chewy texture.

 Your Stockholm Source for Essential Baking & Cooking Flours.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a variety of flours, including this versatile Deepak Maida, to equip your Stockholm kitchen for all your baking and traditional Indian cooking adventures.

 Understanding Kuttu (Buckwheat) Flour.
Kuttu flour is known for its distinctive nutty, earthy flavor and its dark color. It's valued for:

  • Ideal for Fasting (Vrat/Upvas): A permitted and widely used flour during Hindu fasts, providing energy and sustenance when common grains are restricted.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: An excellent choice for individuals with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity, offering a nutritious alternative to wheat-based flours.

  • Nutrient-Rich: Buckwheat is a good source of dietary fiber, protein, and essential minerals like magnesium and manganese. It has a lower glycemic index compared to many refined flours.

  • Versatile for Vrat Recipes: Used to prepare a variety of traditional fasting dishes, from flatbreads to savory pancakes and fritters.

  • Distinctive Flavor: Adds a unique, slightly robust, and earthy taste to preparations.

  • Quality by Malik Foods: Malik Foods provides this traditional flour, ensuring good quality for your fasting and everyday gluten-free needs.

Culinary Uses for Malik Foods Kuttu Flour.

  • Kuttu ki Puri: Deep-fried flatbreads made with kuttu flour, often mixed with mashed potatoes or arbi (taro root) and spices like sendha namak (rock salt).

  • Kuttu ki Roti/Paratha: Pan-fried or griddle-cooked flatbreads.

  • Kuttu ke Cheele: Savory pancakes, sometimes made with grated vegetables.

  • Kuttu ke Pakore (Fritters): Vegetables dipped in kuttu flour batter and deep-fried.

  • Fasting Thalipeeth/Dosa: Can be used to make savory multigrain (fasting-appropriate grains) pancakes.

  • Gluten-Free Baking (Blended): While it has a strong flavor on its own, it can be blended with other gluten-free flours for baking bread, muffins, or pancakes.

  • Thickening Agent: Can be used in small quantities to thicken some fasting-friendly gravies.

 Tips for Cooking with Kuttu Flour.

  • Kuttu flour can be slightly dense on its own. It's often mixed with mashed boiled potatoes, sweet potatoes, or other Vrat-friendly flours (like Singhare ka Atta - water chestnut flour) to improve texture and binding for puris and rotis.

  • The dough made from kuttu flour can be a bit sticky; use a little oil or dry kuttu flour while rolling.

  • Cook kuttu preparations thoroughly.

 Your Stockholm Source for Fasting Essentials & Gluten-Free Flours.
Ideal Indiska is committed to offering a range of traditional and healthy food products. Malik Foods Kuttu Flour is a perfect choice for observing fasts with authentic recipes or for anyone in Stockholm exploring nutritious gluten-free flour alternatives.

Name: MTR Dosa Mix 500 g - Instant Mix for Crispy South Indian Crepes
Short description:
MTR Dosa Mix

\n

Enjoy authentic, crispy, and golden South Indian dosas effortlessly with MTR Dosa Mix! This 500g pack provides a convenient instant mix, expertly blended by MTR for perfect results every time. Just add water (and sometimes yogurt) to create a smooth batter for delicious homemade dosas in minutes. Ideal for a quick breakfast or light meal.

\n
Description:
Authentic South Indian Dosas Made Easy: MTR Dosa Mix - 500g

Whip up delicious, crispy, and golden-brown South Indian dosas in minutes with MTR Dosa Mix 500g. This convenient instant mix from the iconic MTR brand, a name synonymous with authentic South Indian food, is now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Expertly blended with the right proportions of rice flour and lentil flour, this mix takes the hassle out of traditional dosa preparation, allowing you to enjoy this beloved dish anytime.

The MTR Promise: Authentic Taste & Convenience.

For decades, MTR Foods has been a trusted name in Indian households, known for its commitment to quality and traditional flavors. Their Dosa Mix offers:

  • Authentic Dosa Flavor & Texture: Crafted to deliver the genuine taste and characteristic crispiness of traditional dosas.
  • Instant & Easy to Prepare: Simply mix with water (and often a little yogurt/curd as per pack instructions) to form a smooth batter. No lengthy soaking, grinding, or fermentation is required.
  • Consistent Results: MTR's precise formulation ensures you get perfectly textured and flavored dosas every time.
  • Saves Time & Effort: Ideal for busy mornings, quick lunches, or satisfying those sudden dosa cravings without the extensive preparation.
  • Versatile Base: While perfect for plain dosas, you can also use this batter to make Masala Dosa (with potato filling), Onion Dosa, Rava Dosa (by adding semolina), or Uttapams (thicker pancakes with toppings).
  • Trusted South Indian Brand: MTR is a leading food brand from South India, ensuring an authentic culinary experience.

How to Make Perfect Dosas with MTR Dosa Mix.

(MTR packaging provides clear, step-by-step instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Take the required amount of MTR Dosa Mix in a bowl.
  2. Gradually add water (and yogurt/curd if specified on the pack), mixing continuously to form a smooth, lump-free batter of pouring consistency (similar to thin pancake batter). Some recipes suggest letting the batter rest for a few minutes.
  3. Heat a flat non-stick tawa (griddle) or a well-seasoned iron tawa over medium heat. Sprinkle a few drops of water; if they sizzle and evaporate quickly, the tawa is ready.
  4. Lightly grease the tawa with oil. Pour a ladleful of batter onto the center of the tawa.
  5. Quickly spread the batter outwards in a circular motion with the back of the ladle to form a thin crepe (dosa).
  6. Drizzle a little oil or ghee around the edges of the dosa.
  7. Cook on medium heat until the bottom turns golden brown and crispy, and the edges start to lift.
  8. Fold the dosa in half or into a roll.
  9. Serve hot with sambar, coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and/or idli podi.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Instant South Indian Breakfasts.
Ideal Indiska is delighted to bring you convenient and authentic solutions like MTR Dosa Mix, making it easy to enjoy traditional South Indian delicacies in your Stockholm home. This 500g pack offers great value for many delicious meals.

Name: Heera Semolina Fine (Suji/Sooji/Rava) 375g - For Upma, Halwa & More
Short description:
Heera Semolina Fine

\n

A versatile kitchen staple, Heera Semolina Fine (Suji/Sooji/Rava) is perfect for a variety of Indian dishes. This 375g pack contains finely granulated durum wheat semolina, ideal for making fluffy Upma, rich Sooji Halwa, soft Idlis (when mixed), crispy Dosas, and even as a coating for fried items.
Description:
The Versatile Grain for Indian Delicacies: Heera Semolina Fine (Suji)

Unlock a world of delicious possibilities in your Indian kitchen with Heera Semolina Fine (Suji/Sooji/Rava) 375g. This high-quality, finely granulated semolina, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is made from purified durum wheat middlings. It's an incredibly versatile ingredient, cherished for its ability to create a range of textures – from soft and fluffy in Upma and Idlis, to rich and decadent in Halwa, and crispy in Dosas or fried coatings.

Understanding Fine Semolina (Suji/Rava)
Semolina, known as Suji, Sooji, or Rava in India, is a coarse, purified wheat middling of durum wheat mainly used in making pasta, couscous, and sweet puddings. The "fine" variety means the granules are smaller, which affects its cooking properties and texture in dishes.

  • Fine Granulation: Heera's Fine Semolina has a delicate texture that cooks relatively quickly and results in smoother preparations compared to coarse semolina.

  • Versatile for Sweet & Savory: A true all-rounder, equally at home in savory breakfast dishes like Upma or Rava Dosa, and in classic Indian sweets like Sooji Halwa or Rava Ladoo.

  • Absorbs Flavors Well: Its neutral taste allows it to readily absorb the flavors of spices, sweeteners, and other ingredients it's cooked with.

  • Adds Texture: Contributes a pleasing, slightly granular yet soft texture to many dishes.

  • Quick Cooking: Fine semolina generally cooks faster than coarser varieties or whole grains.

  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera is a well-regarded brand, ensuring consistent quality for your culinary needs.

 Culinary Uses for Heera Semolina Fine.

  • Upma: A classic savory South Indian breakfast porridge made with roasted semolina, vegetables, and spices.

  • Sooji Halwa / Rava Kesari: A rich and aromatic sweet pudding made with roasted semolina, ghee, sugar, milk/water, and often flavored with cardamom and nuts.

  • Idli & Dosa (often mixed): While Idli Rava (rice rava) is specific for idlis, fine semolina (Suji) is often used to make quick Rava Idlis or Rava Dosas, typically by mixing with yogurt or buttermilk.

  • Rava Ladoo: Sweet balls made with roasted semolina, sugar, ghee, and cardamom.

  • Thickening Agent: Can be used in small quantities to thicken soups or stews.

  • Batters & Coatings: Adds crispness when used as a coating for fried fish, chicken, or vegetables, or as part of a batter.

  • Porridge/Cereal: Can be cooked with milk or water to make a simple porridge for babies or adults.

  • Pasta & Gnocchi (in some cuisines): Semolina is the traditional flour for pasta.

Tips for Cooking with Semolina).

  • Roasting fine semolina (suji) in a dry pan or with a little ghee until lightly fragrant before using it in dishes like Upma or Halwa significantly enhances its nutty flavor and prevents it from becoming lumpy.

  • When adding to liquids, stir continuously to avoid lumps.

 Your Stockholm Source for Essential Indian Flours & Grains.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a variety of quality flours and grains, including this versatile Heera Semolina Fine. Stock your Stockholm pantry for delicious and authentic Indian breakfasts, sweets, and snacks.
