What are Gol Gappy/Pani Puri Puris?
These are small, round, hollow, and crispy fried shells (puris) made from semolina (sooji/rawa) or wheat flour. They are the essential base for the beloved chaat item where they are poked open, filled with a mixture of spiced potatoes, chickpeas, onions, and then dunked into tangy and spicy flavored water (pani).

Why Choose Alibaba Ready to Fry Gol Gappy Puris?

  • Authentic Street Food at Home: Enjoy the true taste and texture of freshly fried puris, far superior to pre-fried options, with the trusted quality of Alibaba.

  • Perfectly Crispy Results: These specially made pellets are designed to puff up beautifully into light, hollow, and incredibly crispy shells when fried correctly.

  • Quick & Easy to Prepare: Simply deep fry these puris in hot oil for a few seconds, and watch them transform!

  • Endless Customization: The fun of Gol Gappy is in the fillings and flavored waters! Create your own unique combinations.

  • Ideal for Parties & Gatherings: A guaranteed crowd-pleaser and a fun, interactive food experience for guests.

How to Prepare Your Gol Gappy:

  1. Fry the Puris: Heat oil in a deep pan or kadai. Once hot, gently slide in a few puris at a time. They will puff up quickly. Fry until golden brown and crispy (usually takes a few seconds per side). Drain on absorbent paper.

  2. Prepare the Filling: Typically a mix of boiled and mashed potatoes, boiled chickpeas (kala chana or white), finely chopped onions, coriander, and chaat masala.

  3. Make the Pani (Flavored Water): Create a tangy and spicy water using mint, coriander, green chilies, tamarind pulp (imli), black salt (kala namak), roasted cumin powder, and pani puri masala. Sweet tamarind chutney (meetha pani) is also often served.

  4. Assemble & Enjoy: Gently tap a hole in the top of a puri, add your filling, dunk in the pani, and eat immediately for the best experience!

What are Samo Seeds (Barnyard Millet)?
Samo seeds, also known by various regional names like Moraiyo, Bhagar, Varai, Kuthiraivali, or Sanwa, are the seeds of a type of grass (Echinochloa frumentacea). Despite being called "seeds," they are botanically a type of millet. They are highly valued for their nutritional profile and quick cooking time.

Why Choose Malik Foods Samo Seeds?

  • Ideal for Fasting (Vrat/Upvas): Samo seeds are a permitted grain during Hindu fasts when cereals like rice and wheat are avoided. They provide essential energy and nutrients.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: An excellent choice for individuals with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity, offering a versatile alternative to wheat-based grains.

  • Rich in Nutrients: Barnyard millet is a good source of dietary fiber, protein, and essential minerals like iron and magnesium. It's known to be light on the stomach and easily digestible.

  • Quick Cooking & Versatile: These tiny grains cook much faster than rice. They can be used to prepare a variety of sweet and savory dishes.

  • Quality Assured: Malik Foods is committed to providing quality grains, ensuring you receive clean and wholesome Samo seeds.

Culinary Uses for Samo Seeds:

Malik Foods Samo Seeds can be transformed into a range of delightful dishes:

  • Samo Khichdi: A savory porridge cooked with potatoes, peanuts, green chilies, and spices – a popular fasting food.

  • Samo Kheer: A delicious, creamy pudding made with milk, sugar, and flavored with cardamom and nuts.

  • Samo Upma/Pulao: A savory dish similar to semolina upma, cooked with vegetables and mild spices.

  • Samo Flour (Bhagar Flour): The seeds can be ground into flour to make dosas, idlis, puris, or flatbreads (bhakri) suitable for fasting.

  • Porridge: A simple, nutritious porridge for a light meal.

Name: Pakistani Mangoes - Premium Quality Mango in Stockholm - Sweet & Aromatic
Short description:
Pakistani Mangoes 

\nIndulge in the unparalleled sweetness and captivating aroma of Fresh Pakistani Mangoes! This box (approx. 2kg) brings you premium, export-quality mangoes, renowned for their exceptionally sweet, non-fibrous pulp and rich flavor. A true taste of Pakistani summer, perfect for enjoying fresh or in desserts. (Seasonal availability).
Description:
Experience the Nectar of Pakistan: Fresh Mangoes - Approx. 2kg Box

Treat yourself and your loved ones to an unforgettable taste experience with our Fresh Pakistani Mangoes, available in an approximate 2kg box. Sourced for their exceptional quality and flavor, these mangoes, often hailed as some of the best in the world, are a seasonal delight now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Get ready to indulge in the rich sweetness, intoxicating aroma, and buttery-smooth texture that Pakistani mangoes are celebrated for.

The Unmatched Allure of Pakistani Mangoes

Varieties like Chaunsa, Sindhri, and Anwar Ratol from Pakistan are globally renowned for their superior taste:

  • Exceptional Sweetness: Pakistani mangoes are known for their incredibly high sugar content and a deep, honey-like sweetness that is truly special.
  • Captivating Aroma: The fragrance of a ripe Pakistani mango is intensely aromatic, fruity, and can fill a room with its delightful perfume.
  • Smooth, Non-Fibrous Pulp: Characterized by their buttery, melt-in-your-mouth texture with minimal to no fiber, making them an absolute pleasure to eat.
  • Rich & Complex Flavor: Beyond just sweetness, they offer a nuanced flavor profile that is both rich and satisfying.
  • Export Quality: We strive to bring you export-quality mangoes, ensuring you receive fruit that meets high standards.
  • A True Taste of Summer: Mangoes are the quintessential summer fruit in Pakistan and India, evoking feelings of joy and celebration.

How to Best Enjoy Your Pakistani Mangoes

  • Eat Fresh: The ultimate way to savor their pure, unadulterated flavor. Simply slice (the "hedgehog" cut is popular) or peel and enjoy.
  • Mango Lassi/Shakes: Blend the luscious pulp with yogurt or milk for a classic, refreshing beverage.
  • Aamras: Puree the mango pulp for a delicious traditional Indian dessert, often served with puris.
  • Desserts: Perfect for making mango ice cream, kulfi, mousse, cheesecakes, or as a topping for other sweets.
  • Salads: Add a touch of tropical sweetness to fruit salads.
  • Gifting: A box of premium Pakistani mangoes is a highly cherished gift.

Selecting, Ripening & Storing Your Mangoes

  • Selection: Look for mangoes that have a fragrant aroma near the stem end and yield slightly to gentle pressure. The skin should be mostly smooth, though slight blemishes or sap marks are common and don't always affect the fruit inside.
  • Ripening: If your mangoes are a bit firm, leave them at room temperature for a few days to ripen. Placing them in a paper bag or a rice container can help speed up the process. They are ripe when they feel slightly soft and are very fragrant.
  • Storage: Once ripe, Pakistani mangoes can be stored in the refrigerator for a few days to slow down further ripening. However, for the best flavor, allow them to come to room temperature before eating. Cut mango should be refrigerated and consumed quickly.

A Seasonal Treasure - Available for a Limited Time!

Pakistani mangoes are a highly anticipated seasonal fruit, typically available for a limited period during the summer months. Secure your box from Ideal Indiska in Stockholm and experience this exquisite delicacy while the season lasts!

  • Form: Whole, Fresh Fruit

  • Origin: Pakistan
  • Storage: Ripen at room temperature; store ripe mangoes in the refrigerator for a few days.

Description:
The Secret to Vibrant Colour & Mild Spice: TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder

Transform your culinary creations with the beautiful rich red hue and gentle warmth of TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder 400g, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. This special variety of chilli powder, made from select Kashmiri chillies, is a prized ingredient in Indian cuisine, renowned more for the stunning natural colour it imparts than for intense heat.

What Makes Kashmiri Chilli Powder Unique?

Kashmiri chillies are longer, fleshier, and have a lower Scoville Heat Unit (SHU) rating compared to many other Indian chilli varieties. When dried and ground, they produce a powder that is exceptionally vibrant red and relatively mild in spiciness, making it perfect for dishes where color is as important as flavor.

Why Choose TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder?

  • Vibrant Natural Red Colour: Imparts a deep, appealing red color to your food without the need for artificial food colorings.
  • Mild & Pleasant Heat: Adds a subtle warmth and a distinct, slightly fruity chilli flavor that doesn't overpower other spices or ingredients. Ideal for those who prefer less spicy food but still want authentic flavor.
  • Authentic TRS Quality: TRS ensures this powder is finely ground from genuine Kashmiri chillies, delivering consistent color and mildness.
  • Versatile Culinary Use: A staple in many North Indian, particularly Kashmiri and Punjabi, dishes.
  • Enhances Visual Appeal: Makes your curries, tandoori items, and other preparations look incredibly appetizing.

Culinary Uses for TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder:

  • Tandoori Dishes: Essential for achieving the classic red color in Tandoori Chicken, Paneer Tikka, and other tandoori marinades.
  • Rogan Josh: A key ingredient in this iconic Kashmiri lamb curry, contributing to its signature red gravy.
  • Curries & Gravies: Use in various meat, vegetable, and lentil curries where a rich red color and mild heat are desired (e.g., Butter Chicken, Korma).
  • Vegetable Preparations (Sabzis): Adds color and mild spice to everyday vegetable dishes.
  • Marinades & Spice Blends: Incorporate into marinades or create your own spice mixes where a touch of red and mild heat is needed.
  • Soups & Stews: Can add a lovely hue and subtle warmth.

Tips for Use:

  • Use generously for color; the heat level is mild.
  • Can be combined with hotter chilli powders if you desire both intense color and higher spiciness.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Indian Spices:
Ideal Indiska is proud to offer TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder, allowing you to bring authentic color and flavor to your Indian cooking adventures in Stockholm.

Description:

Transform your hair with the rich, lasting colour and natural conditioning of EcoByLife Henna Hair Colour in Brown, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. This specially formulated henna-based hair dye is designed to provide "100% grå täckning" (100% grey coverage) while being "Ingen Ammoniak" (No Ammonia), offering a gentler way to achieve your desired shade. Enriched for "Ren Henna Konditionering" (Pure Henna Conditioning), it not only colours but also leaves your hair feeling wonderfully soft, smooth, and shiny.

 The EcoByLife Difference: Colour & Care.

EcoByLife Henna Hair Colour is crafted for those seeking effective colour and hair nourishment:

  • Vibrant Shade: Specifically formulated to deliver a beautiful and lasting Dark brown hue.
  • 100% Grey Coverage ("100% grå täckning"): Effectively colours grey hair for a uniform and youthful look.
  • Pure Henna Conditioning ("Ren Henna Konditionering"): Leverages the natural conditioning properties of henna (Lawsonia inermis) to improve hair texture, add body, and impart a healthy shine.
  • Ammonia-Free Formula ("Ingen Ammoniak"): A gentler option compared to many conventional hair dyes that contain ammonia.
  • Enriched with Beneficial Ingredients: (The pack mentions "12 Örter" for the Herbal Henna version.
  • Complete Application Kit: Comes with "FRI - Tillämplig borste och handskar ingår" (FREE applicable brush and gloves inside) for a convenient home application experience.

 Application Instructions & Important Considerations.

CRITICAL: Always perform a patch test 48 hours before full application to check for any allergic reactions or sensitivities, even if you have used henna or hair dye products before. This is especially important for any product achieving black or very dark shades, or vibrant non-traditional henna colours.
Always conduct a strand test to preview the colour result on your hair type and to check development time.

  1. Preparation: Mix EcoByLife Henna Hair Colour powder with lukewarm water (or as directed on the packaging – some henna-based dyes recommend tea/coffee decoction for deeper tones) to form a smooth, thick paste. Let it sit for the recommended time if specified for dye release.
  2. Application: Wear the provided gloves. Apply the paste evenly to clean, dry or slightly damp hair, section by section, using the provided brush, starting from the roots and working towards the tips. Ensure all hair, especially greys, is thoroughly coated.
  3. Development Time: Cover your hair with a shower cap (may or may not be included). Leave the paste on for the duration specified in the product instructions (this can vary significantly, e.g., 1-3 hours, depending on desired intensity and ingredients).
  4. Rinsing: Rinse your hair thoroughly with lukewarm water until the water runs clear. It's often recommended not to use shampoo immediately after rinsing henna-based dyes for the first time (wait 24-48 hours for the colour to fully oxidize and set). You may use a conditioner if needed.
    The final colour may take a day or two to fully develop and deepen.

Important Safety Information - Reiterated.

  • This product contains ingredients that may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in certain individuals. A preliminary patch test according to accompanying directions should ALWAYS be made 48 hours before each use.
  • Do not use to dye eyelashes or eyebrows; to do so may cause blindness.
  • For external use on scalp hair only. Keep out of reach of children.
  • Avoid contact with eyes. If product gets into eyes, rinse immediately with plenty of water.
  • Wear suitable gloves during application. If irritation persists, consult a doctor.

Your Stockholm Choice for Conditioning Henna Hair Colour.
Ideal Indiska offers EcoByLife Henna Hair Colour, providing an ammonia-free option for achieving beautiful,  hair with the added benefits of henna conditioning, right here in Stockholm.

Product Details:

Example for a Dark Brown might be: Lawsonia Inermis Leaf Powder (Henna), Indigofera Tinctoria Leaf Powder (Indigo), Emblica Officinalis (Amla) Fruit Powder, Acacia Concinna (Shikakai) Fruit Powder, Para-Phenylenediamine (PPD -, Sodium Perborate, Citric Acid, Magnesium Carbonate, Sodium Sulphite, etc. For Burgundy, it might include henna and other plant powders or specific colorants. The presence or absence of PPD or other chemical dyes is extremely important for customer safety and transparency, especially when promising 100% grey coverage in dark shades.
  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight. Keep unused powder in a well-sealed container.

 The Hallmarks of India Gate Premium Basmati.

India Gate Premium Basmati Rice is selected for discerning consumers who appreciate the finest quality:

  • Extra-Long Slender Grains: Characterized by its exceptionally long grains (often doubling in length when cooked) that remain separate and distinct, creating an elegant presentation on your plate.
  • Aged for Perfection: Proper aging is key to developing the full aroma and flavor of Basmati, as well as ensuring the grains cook up fluffy and non-sticky. India Gate ensures this rice meets optimal aging standards.
  • Exquisite Natural Aroma: Delivers the signature, captivating fragrance that true Basmati is celebrated for – a delicate, slightly nutty, and wonderfully inviting scent.
  • Delicate & Distinct Flavor: Offers a subtle, clean, and slightly sweet flavor that perfectly complements a wide variety of Indian and international dishes.
  • Superb Fluffy Texture: Each grain cooks to a light, airy, and fluffy perfection, never clumpy.
  • Ideal for Biryanis & Pulaos: Its ability to remain separate and absorb flavors makes it the quintessential choice for creating authentic, layered biryanis and aromatic pulaos.

 Culinary Masterpieces with Premium Basmati

  • Show-Stopping Biryanis: The perfect canvas for chicken, mutton, vegetable, or egg biryanis.
  • Flavorful Pulaos: Create fragrant vegetable, pea, or jeera pulaos.
  • Elegant Steamed Rice: Serve as a sophisticated accompaniment to rich curries, dals, and kormas.
  • Special Occasion Dishes: Ideal for festive meals, impressing guests, and celebrating traditions.
  • Versatile Base: While perfect for Indian cuisine, its quality also shines in Persian, Middle Eastern, and other dishes calling for premium long-grain rice.

Cooking Perfect India Gate Premium Basmati Rice
(General instructions, always refer to the pack for India Gate's specific guidance)

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of India Gate Premium Basmati Rice in cold water 2-3 times, or until the water runs mostly clear.
  2. Soak (Highly Recommended): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30 minutes. This helps the grains achieve maximum length and cook evenly.
  3. Cook (Open Pot Method): Drain the soaked rice. Add to 1.5 to 2 cups of boiling water with a pinch of salt (optional) and a drop of oil/ghee. Cook on medium-low heat, covered, for 10-12 minutes (or until water is absorbed).
  4. Cook (Closed Pot/Rice Cooker): Use approx. 1.5 cups of water per 1 cup of soaked rice. Follow rice cooker instructions.
  5. Rest: Once cooked, let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes.
  6. Fluff: Gently fluff with a fork before serving.

 Your Stockholm Choice for the Finest Basmati

This convenient ready-to-heat offering from India's iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, brings you wholesome whole wheat parathas, generously filled with a delicious and traditionally spiced mashed potato (aloo) mixture. Perfect for a hearty breakfast, a fulfilling lunch, or a comforting dinner.

 The Beloved Aloo Paratha Experience.
Aloo Paratha is a staple in many Indian households, especially in Punjab and North India. It's a complete meal in itself, loved for its soft bread and flavorful, savory filling.

  • Authentic Spiced Potato Filling: Haldiram's captures the traditional taste with a well-seasoned filling of mashed potatoes, onions (often), green chillies, coriander, and a blend of classic Indian spices like cumin, coriander powder, turmeric, and amchur (dry mango powder for tang).

  • Wholesome Whole Wheat Paratha: The outer flatbread is typically made with whole wheat flour (atta), offering a nutritious base.

  • Hearty & Satisfying: A truly filling and comforting meal option.

  • Ultimate Convenience - Ready to Heat: These frozen parathas save you the significant time and effort of preparing the dough, making the stuffing, and expertly rolling and cooking the parathas.

  • Heats from Frozen in Minutes: Quickly prepared on a tawa (griddle) or flat pan.

  • Ideal Pack Size: The 400g pack it is suitable for individual meals or small families.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Haldiram's is a globally recognized name for quality, hygiene, and authentic Indian foods.

 Simple Heating Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  1. Do Not Thaw (Usually): Most frozen parathas are best cooked directly from frozen.

  2. Heat Tawa/Pan: Preheat a flat pan or tawa over medium heat.

  3. Cook Paratha: Place the frozen Aloo Paratha on the heated tawa. Cook for about 2-3 minutes on one side, or until golden brown spots appear.

  4. Flip & Cook: Flip the paratha. You can add a teaspoon of ghee or oil around the edges and on top if desired, for extra crispness and flavor. Cook the other side for another 2-3 minutes, pressing gently and evenly with a flat spatula, until it's golden brown, crispy on the outside, and the filling is heated through.

  5. Serve Hot: Best served immediately.

 What is Phulka Roti?

Phulka, also known as Roti or Chapati in many parts of India, is a thin, unleavened flatbread made from whole wheat flour (atta). It's traditionally cooked on a tawa (griddle) and then often puffed up directly over an open flame, resulting in a light, airy, and soft bread.

  • Authentic Home-Style Softness: Haldiram's aims to replicate the texture of freshly made phulkas – soft, pliable, and light.

  • Made with Whole Wheat Flour (Atta): A wholesome choice, providing the goodness of whole grains.

  • Ultimate Convenience - Ready to Heat: These frozen rotis are a lifesaver for busy days, offering a quick and easy way to have fresh-tasting Indian bread with your meals.

  • Ideal Pack Size: The 360g pack (specify approximate number of rotis if known from packaging, e.g., "Contains approx. X Phulkas") is suitable for small families or several meals.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Haldiram's is a globally recognized name for quality and authentic Indian foods.

  • Perfect for Everyday Meals: The ideal accompaniment to any Indian dal, sabzi (vegetable curry), or meat/chicken curry.

Simple Heating Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  1. Do Not Thaw (Usually): Most frozen rotis are best cooked directly from frozen.

  2. Heat Tawa/Pan: Preheat a flat pan or tawa over medium heat.

  3. Cook Roti: Place the frozen Phulka Roti on the heated tawa. Heat for about 30 seconds to 1 minute on one side.

  4. Flip & Cook: Flip the roti. You might see it start to puff. Cook the other side for another 30 seconds to 1 minute, pressing gently with a clean, folded cloth or a flat spatula, until it's heated through, soft, and ideally puffed. (Some people then hold it over a direct low flame with tongs for a moment to encourage full puffing, but be cautious).

  5. Serve Hot: Best served immediately, often with a dollop of ghee or butter.

Description:
Embrace the Fire! Samyang Buldak Hot Chicken Flavor Ramen - Big Bowl 🔥

Are you ready for a flavor inferno? Samyang Buldak Hot Chicken Flavor Ramen - Big Bowl (105g) brings the viral Korean spicy noodle sensation right to your kitchen in Stockholm, courtesy of Ideal Indiska! Famous worldwide for its intense heat and incredibly addictive taste, this "Buldak" (fire chicken) ramen is not for the faint of heart but promises an unforgettable and satisfyingly fiery meal experience. This convenient big bowl format makes it even easier to enjoy.

The Global Spicy Noodle Phenomenon
Samyang's Buldak ramen series sparked a global "fire noodle challenge" and has become a must-try for spice lovers and fans of Korean cuisine.

  • Iconic Fiery Buldak Sauce: The star of the show is the intensely spicy and slightly sweet Buldak sauce, delivering a powerful kick that builds with every bite.
  • Savory Chicken Flavor: Beneath the heat lies a delicious and savory chicken flavor that complements the spiciness perfectly.
  • Chewy & Satisfying Noodles: Samyang is known for its high-quality, thick, and chewy ramen noodles that hold up well to the rich sauce.
  • Convenient Big Bowl Format: No need for a separate pot! Just add hot water, wait a few minutes, drain (for stir-fried style), mix in the sauce and flakes, and you're ready to indulge. Perfect for a quick lunch, late-night snack, or when you need an instant spice fix.
  • Join the Challenge (or Enjoy at Your Own Pace!): Whether you're taking on the spicy noodle challenge or simply enjoy a good dose of heat, Samyang Buldak delivers.
  • Authentic Korean Taste: Experience one of South Korea's most popular and globally recognized instant noodle flavors.

How to Prepare Your Samyang Buldak Big Bowl
(Instructions are usually on the packaging, but a general guide helps)

  1. Open the lid partway. Remove the sauce packet and dried flake packet.
  2. Fill the bowl with hot water up to the indicated line inside. Close the lid and let it steep for about 3-4 minutes, or until noodles are cooked.
  3. Carefully drain most of the water, leaving a few tablespoons (or as per your preference for sauciness). Some Buldak varieties are soup-based, others are stir-fried style where most water is drained. This original black pack is typically the stir-fried style.
  4. Add the liquid sauce packet and the dried flakes (seaweed, sesame seeds).
  5. Stir well until the noodles are evenly coated. Brace yourself and enjoy the heat!

Tips for Enjoying (and Surviving!) Buldak Ramen:

  • Have a glass of milk or a dairy-based drink nearby if you're sensitive to spice.
  • Add toppings like a fried egg, melted cheese, chopped spring onions, or extra seaweed to customize and slightly temper the heat if desired.
  • Start with a small amount of the sauce if you're new to Buldak, and gradually add more to your tolerance level.

Your Stockholm Hub for Viral & Authentic Asian Flavours

Name: Forest Hen Jasmin Ris (AAAAA Jasmine Rice by SRF) 2kg - Fragrant Thai Rice
Short description:
Jasmin Ris (AAAAA Jasmine Rice by SRF) 

\nExperience the authentic aroma and delicate texture of premium Jasmine Rice with Forest Hen Brand (by SRF). This 2kg pack contains AAAAA quality "Jasmin Ris," known for its soft, slightly sticky grains and distinct pandan-like fragrance when cooked. Description:
The Fragrant Delight of Southeast Asia: Forest Hen Jasmin Ris (Jasmine Rice) - 2kg)

Elevate your Asian culinary creations with the authentic taste and captivating aroma of Forest Hen Brand Jasmin Ris (AAAAA Jasmine Rice by SRF) in a 2kg pack. This premium quality long-grain rice, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a staple in Thai, Vietnamese, Chinese, and other Southeast Asian households. It's cherished for its delicate, soft, and slightly sticky texture when cooked, and its signature fragrant aroma reminiscent of pandan leaves and popcorn.

Understanding Premium Jasmine Rice

Jasmine rice, often "Thai Hom Mali" rice, is a specific variety of long-grain aromatic rice. The "AAAAA" grading typically signifies a high standard of quality, with well-milled, whole grains.

  • Distinctive Floral Aroma: Its most prized characteristic is its natural, delicate, and appealing fragrance that blooms during cooking. Forest Hen Brand aims to deliver this authentic scent.
  • Soft & Slightly Sticky Texture: Unlike the very separate grains of Basmati, Jasmine rice cooks up soft, moist, and slightly sticky, making it perfect for eating with chopsticks or for dishes where a little cling is desirable.
  • Premium AAAAA Quality: This grading suggests superior grain length, minimal broken grains, and excellent overall quality from SRF (Swedish Fine Rice & Food).
  • Versatile for Asian Cuisine: The go-to rice for a vast array of dishes, from simple steamed rice to accompany curries and stir-fries, to more elaborate preparations.
  • Authentic Taste Experience: Essential for recreating the authentic flavors and textures of Southeast Asian meals.

Culinary Uses for Forest Hen Jasmin Ris

  • Steamed Jasmine Rice: The perfect accompaniment to Thai green curry, red curry, Vietnamese pho (as a side), Chinese stir-fried dishes, and countless other Asian main courses.
  • Fried Rice: While slightly stickier than some other varieties, it can be used to make delicious Thai or Chinese-style fried rice (best with day-old cooked rice).
  • Coconut Rice: Its aroma pairs beautifully with coconut milk for a fragrant coconut rice.
  • Mango Sticky Rice (Khao Niao Mamuang - if using sweet glutinous rice, but Jasmine can be a substitute for some): While glutinous rice is traditional for this Thai dessert, some use Jasmine rice for a less sticky version.
  • Congee/Rice Porridge: Can be cooked down into a comforting Asian-style rice porridge.

Cooking Instructions for Perfect Jasmine Rice
General instructions, always refer to the pack for SRF's specific guidance

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of Forest Hen Jasmin Ris in cold water 2-3 times, or until the water runs mostly clear. This removes excess surface starch.
  2. Water Ratio: For stovetop, typically use a water-to-rice ratio of about 1:1.25 or 1:1.5 (1 cup rice to 1.25-1.5 cups water). For rice cookers, follow the cooker's markings or use a similar ratio.
  3. Cook (Stovetop): Bring rice and water to a boil in a pot. Once boiling, reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all water is absorbed. Do not lift the lid during this time.
  4. Cook (Rice Cooker): Add rinsed rice and water to the rice cooker and switch it on.
  5. Rest: Once cooked (either stovetop or rice cooker), let the rice stand covered and undisturbed for at least 10-15 minutes. This allows the steam to finish cooking the grains and makes them fluffier.
  6. Fluff: Gently fluff the rice with a fork or rice paddle before serving.

Your Stockholm Choice for Authentic Asian Rice
Ideal Indiska is proud to offer premium rice varieties like Forest Hen Brand Jasmin Ris by SRF, ensuring our customers in Stockholm can create authentic and delicious Asian meals at home. This 2kg pack is ideal for regular enjoyment.

Short description:
Malik Foods Sattu | Chickpea Flour

\n

Description:
The Traditional Powerhouse of Nutrition: Malik Foods Sattu (Roasted Gram Flour) - 1kg)

Embrace a traditional Indian superfood renowned for its nutritional benefits and versatility with Malik Foods Sattu (Roasted Gram Flour) in a convenient 1kg pack. This unique flour, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is made by grinding roasted grams (most commonly Bengal gram/kala chana, or a mix of grains and legumes). Sattu is a staple in many parts of India, particularly Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttar Pradesh, and Punjab, cherished for its cooling properties in summer, its high protein and fiber content, and its use in a variety of delicious and energizing preparations.

Understanding Sattu - The Nutrient-Rich Flour)
Sattu is distinct from regular besan (gram flour) because the grams are roasted before being ground, which imparts a unique nutty aroma and flavor, and also makes it easier to digest.

  • Rich in Protein & Fiber: An excellent source of plant-based protein and dietary fiber, making it very satiating and beneficial for digestive health.

  • Cooling Properties (Traditional Belief): Sattu drinks are traditionally consumed in summer for their cooling effect on the body.

  • Provides Sustained Energy: Its complex carbohydrates and protein offer a slow release of energy, making it a great food for stamina.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free (Typically, if made purely from gram): When made from just roasted chickpeas or other gluten-free legumes/grains, it's a fantastic gluten-free option. (Verify specific ingredients of Malik Foods Sattu).

  • Versatile in Sweet & Savory Dishes: Can be used to make refreshing drinks, hearty porridges, savory flatbreads, and even sweets.

  • Earthy & Nutty Flavor: The roasting process gives Sattu a pleasant, distinct, and slightly smoky nutty flavor.

  • Quality by Malik Foods: Malik Foods offers this traditional flour, aiming for authenticity and nutritional value.

 How to Enjoy Malik Foods Sattu)

Sattu's versatility shines in various preparations:

  • Sattu Sharbat (Refreshing Drink): The most popular use.

    • Sweet Sattu Sharbat: Mix Sattu with cold water or milk, sugar or jaggery (sakkar), a pinch of black salt (kala namak), and sometimes lemon juice for a cooling and energizing drink.

    • Savory Sattu Sharbat (Namkeen Sattu): Mix Sattu with cold water, black salt, roasted cumin powder, chopped onions, green chillies, coriander leaves, and lemon juice.

  • Sattu Paratha: Sattu mixed with spices, onions, and herbs is used as a delicious and nutritious stuffing for whole wheat parathas.

  • Litti Chokha: Sattu stuffing is the heart of Litti, a traditional Bihari baked or fried dough ball, served with Chokha (mashed spiced vegetables).

  • Sattu Porridge/Lapsi: Cook Sattu with water or milk to make a quick and nutritious porridge, either sweet or savory.

  • Energy Balls/Ladoos: Mixed with ghee, jaggery/sugar, and nuts to create energy-boosting ladoos.

  • Thickening Agent: Can be used in small amounts as a nutritious thickener for some gravies.

 Tips for Using Sattu)

  • Sattu readily absorbs water, so add liquid gradually when making a drink or dough to achieve the desired consistency.

  • Its flavor is enhanced by complementary spices like roasted cumin, black salt, and mint.

 Your Stockholm Source for Traditional Indian Health Foods)
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer Malik Foods Sattu, bringing this nutritious and versatile traditional Indian flour to your Stockholm kitchen. Perfect for healthy drinks, energizing meals, and exploring regional Indian cuisine.

It's designed to provide a satisfying texture and taste, making it an excellent alternative to traditional wheat-based atta for individuals with celiac disease, gluten sensitivity, or those simply looking to incorporate more diverse grains into their meals.

 The Power of a Multigrain Gluten-Free Blend)

Sai Multigrain Gluten Free Atta offers several benefits:

  • Nutrient-Rich Composition: By blending various gluten-free grains (such as sorghum/jowar, pearl millet/bajra, finger millet/ragi, rice flour, chickpea flour/besan, tapioca starch, etc. - exact grains need to be verified from packaging), this atta provides a broader spectrum of nutrients, including fiber, protein, vitamins, and minerals, compared to single-grain gluten-free flours.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: Specifically created for those who need to avoid gluten, ensuring a safe and reliable option for making traditional Indian flatbreads.

  • Aids in Soft Rotis/Chapatis: Formulated to help achieve a good texture and softness in gluten-free flatbreads, which can sometimes be challenging with single gluten-free flours.

  • Wholesome Alternative: A great way to enjoy your favorite Indian meals with flatbreads that are both gluten-free and packed with the goodness of multiple grains.

  • Versatile for Various Flatbreads: Suitable for making rotis, chapatis, parathas (with appropriate fillings), and potentially other types of gluten-free flatbreads.

 How to Use Sai Multigrain Gluten Free Atta)

Making gluten-free flatbreads often requires slightly different techniques than wheat-based ones:

  1. Kneading: Gradually add warm water (and sometimes a little oil or yogurt, as per preference or pack instructions) to the Sai Multigrain Gluten Free Atta. Knead gently to form a soft, pliable dough. Gluten-free doughs may feel different (less elastic) than wheat dough.

  2. Resting: Allow the dough to rest for 15-30 minutes, covered. This helps the flours to hydrate properly.

  3. Rolling: Take small portions of the dough and roll them out into thin circles (rotis/chapatis) using a rolling pin. Dust lightly with more gluten-free atta if the dough is sticky. Gluten-free doughs can be more delicate, so handle with care.

  4. Cooking:

    • Heat a tawa (griddle) over medium heat.

    • Cook the roti on one side until small bubbles appear. Flip and cook the other side.

    • You can then cook it directly over a low flame (using tongs) for it to puff up, or continue cooking on the tawa, pressing gently with a clean cloth or spatula, until golden brown spots appear on both sides and it's cooked through.

  5. Serve hot, optionally brushed with ghee or butter, with your favorite curries and dals.

 Your Stockholm Source for Speciality Gluten-Free Flours)

Name: MTR Sambar Powder (Spice Blend for Lentil Stew) 200g - Authentic South Indian Taste
Short description:
MTR Sambar Powder

\nCreate authentic, flavourful South Indian Sambar at home with MTR Sambar Powder! This 200g pack contains a perfectly balanced blend of roasted lentils and aromatic spices, expertly formulated by MTR since 1976 for that traditional tangy and savory taste. Your shortcut to delicious, perfect Sambar every time.
Description:
The Authentic Taste of South India: MTR Sambar Powder

Bring the heartwarming and complex flavors of authentic South Indian cuisine to your table with MTR Sambar Powder 200g. From the legendary MTR brand, a name synonymous with quality and tradition since 1924, this signature spice blend is available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. It's expertly crafted with a unique medley of roasted lentils, chillies, and aromatic spices, meticulously chosen to help you effortlessly recreate the traditional tangy, savory, and fragrant taste of Sambar.

What is Sambar?

Sambar is a cornerstone of South Indian cuisine, a delicious and nutritious stew made with toor dal (split pigeon peas), a variety of vegetables, tangy tamarind, and a special spice blend – the Sambar Powder. It's a versatile dish, famously served with steamed rice, idlis, dosas, vadas, and uttapam.

Why Choose MTR Sambar Powder?

  • The Original & Authentic Taste: MTR has been perfecting its Sambar Powder recipe since 1976, delivering a genuine, time-tested flavor that is loved in households across India and the world.
  • Perfectly Balanced Blend: Takes the complex guesswork out of sourcing, roasting, and grinding numerous individual spices and lentils. MTR ensures a consistent and delicious flavor profile every single time.
  • Rich & Aromatic: The blend of roasted lentils, coriander, cumin, chilli, fenugreek, and other spices provides a deep, aromatic, and full-bodied flavor to your Sambar.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: Simplifies the cooking process, allowing you to achieve authentic Sambar flavor without extensive spice preparation.
  • Trusted Heritage Brand: With a legacy stretching back to 1924, MTR is a name you can trust for quality and authentic Indian taste.

How to Use MTR Sambar Powder

(MTR packaging provides detailed instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Cook toor dal until soft. Boil your choice of vegetables (like drumsticks, carrots, pumpkin, eggplant, onions) separately.
  2. Combine the cooked dal and vegetables. Add tamarind extract and salt to taste.
  3. Stir in the required amount of MTR Sambar Powder (as per package instructions or to taste) and bring to a boil.
  4. Simmer for a few minutes to allow the flavors to meld beautifully.
  5. Prepare a tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds, dried red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida (hing) in oil or ghee, and pour over the Sambar.
  6. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

Your Stockholm Destination for Authentic MTR Products
Ideal Indiska is delighted to bring you iconic South Indian staples like MTR Sambar Powder, making it easy to prepare this nutritious and beloved stew in your Stockholm kitchen.

Name: MTR Rice Idli Ready Mix 500g - Instant Mix for Soft & Spongy Rice Cakes
Short description:

This 500g pack provides a convenient instant mix of rice and urad dal flour, expertly blended by MTR for perfect results. Just add sour yogurt/curd, allow to ferment briefly, and steam for a delicious and healthy breakfast.
Description:
Authentic South Indian Breakfast, Simplified: MTR Rice Idli Ready Mix - 500g

Savor the taste of traditional, perfectly soft, and spongy rice idlis without the hours of preparation with MTR Rice Idli Ready Mix 500g. This iconic instant mix from the legendary MTR brand, a name synonymous with authentic South Indian food since 1924, is now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Expertly blended with the right proportions of rice and urad dal (lentil flour), this mix takes the hassle out of traditional idli making, allowing you to enjoy this beloved breakfast delicacy anytime.

The MTR Advantage for Perfect Idlis

Idli is a classic steamed rice and lentil cake, a staple in South Indian cuisine known for being light, healthy, and easily digestible. MTR's Rice Idli Mix makes it simple:

  • Authentic Fermented Taste: Unlike some other instant idli mixes, this MTR recipe calls for mixing with sour yogurt/curd and allowing a fermentation period, which helps to develop that authentic, slightly tangy taste of traditionally made idlis.
  • Soft & Spongy Texture: Crafted to deliver the genuine texture of perfect idlis – light, airy, porous, and incredibly soft.
  • "Goodness of Grains": As highlighted on the pack, this mix is a wholesome choice for a "Steamed Breakfast."
  • Consistent, Reliable Results: MTR's precise formulation ensures you get beautifully steamed, fluffy idlis every single time.
  • Saves Significant Time: Eliminates the need for a full day of soaking, grinding, and overnight fermentation of rice and lentils.
  • Trusted Heritage Brand: MTR is a leading food brand from South India, guaranteeing an authentic culinary experience with every product.

How to Make Perfect Rice Idlis with MTR Mix

(MTR packaging provides clear, step-by-step instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Prepare the Batter: In a bowl, combine 1 measure of MTR Rice Idli Mix with 1 measure of sour yogurt/curd and 1.5 measures of water (or as directed on the pack). Mix into a smooth, lump-free batter.
  2. Ferment: Cover the bowl and set it aside in a warm place for about 1-2 hours (or as recommended) to allow for a quick fermentation, which enhances the taste and texture.
  3. Steam the Idlis: Lightly grease your idli molds. Gently stir the fermented batter once more and pour it into the molds.
  4. Steam in an idli cooker or a steamer for approximately 10-15 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the center of an idli comes out clean.
  5. Let the idlis rest for a minute after steaming before carefully demolding them.
  6. Serve hot with traditional accompaniments like hot sambar, creamy coconut chutney, tangy tomato chutney, and/or flavorful idli podi (milagai podi).

Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic South Indian Breakfast Mixes

Ideal Indiska is delighted to bring you convenient and authentic solutions like MTR Rice Idli Ready Mix, making it easy to enjoy traditional South Indian delicacies in your Stockholm home. This 500g pack offers great value for many delicious and healthy meals.

Short description:
MTR Khaman Dhokla Ready Mix

\n

Enjoy soft, spongy, and delicious Khaman Dhokla at home effortlessly with MTR Khaman Dhokla Ready Mix! This 500g pack provides a convenient instant mix of gram flour and spices, expertly blended by MTR for perfect results. Just add water, steam, and enjoy this classic tangy and savory Gujarati snack.
Description:
The Authentic Taste of Gujarat, Made Simple: MTR Khaman Dhokla Ready Mix

Whip up the iconic, light, and spongy Khaman Dhokla, a beloved savory cake from Gujarat, in a matter of minutes with MTR Khaman Dhokla Ready Mix [Weight]. This convenient instant mix from the legendary MTR brand, a name synonymous with authentic Indian food, is now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Expertly blended with the right proportions of gram flour (besan) and spices, this mix takes all the guesswork out of making perfect, fluffy dhoklas every time.

 What is Khaman Dhokla?
Khaman Dhokla is a popular vegetarian snack and breakfast item from the Indian state of Gujarat. It's a soft, porous, steamed savory cake made from gram flour. It's known for its tangy, sweet, and mildly spicy flavor, and is typically garnished with a tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds, curry leaves, and green chillies.

  • Authentic Gujarati Flavor: MTR's recipe is crafted to deliver the genuine taste of traditional Khaman Dhokla – tangy, savory, and slightly sweet.
  • Soft & Spongy Perfection: The mix is formulated to produce exceptionally light, airy, and spongy dhoklas with a perfect porous texture.
  • Instant & Easy to Prepare: No need for overnight soaking or lengthy fermentation. Simply mix with water, let it rest for a few minutes, and steam!
  • Consistent Results: MTR's precise formulation ensures you get perfectly risen and textured dhoklas every time.
  • Saves Time & Effort: Ideal for quick breakfasts, evening snacks, or as an appetizer for parties and gatherings.
  • Trusted Heritage Brand: MTR Foods has been a trusted name in Indian households for a century, guaranteeing quality and authentic taste.

 How to Make Perfect Khaman Dhokla with MTR Mix

(MTR packaging provides clear, step-by-step instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Prepare the Batter: Empty the contents of the MTR Khaman Dhokla Mix into a bowl. Gradually add water (and often oil, as per pack instructions), whisking continuously to form a smooth, lump-free batter. Let the batter stand for 5-10 minutes.
  2. Prepare for Steaming: Grease a steaming tray or a flat-bottomed steel container with a little oil.
  3. Steam the Dhokla: Pour the batter into the greased tray. Place the tray inside a steamer or a large pot with boiling water at the bottom (ensure the water doesn't touch the tray). Cover and steam on medium heat for approximately 15-20 minutes, or until a skewer inserted comes out clean.
  4. Cool & Cut: Allow the steamed dhokla to cool slightly, then cut it into square or diamond shapes.
  5. Prepare Tempering (Tadka): Heat a little oil in a small pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add curry leaves and slit green chillies. You can also add asafoetida (hing) and sesame seeds. Some recipes call for adding a little water and sugar to the tempering to create a syrup.
  6. Garnish: Pour the hot tempering evenly over the cut dhokla pieces. Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves and sometimes grated coconut.
  7. Serve with green chutney or tamarind chutney.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic Indian Snack Mixes
Ideal Indiska is delighted to bring you convenient and authentic solutions like MTR Khaman Dhokla Ready Mix, making it easy to enjoy traditional Indian delicacies in your Stockholm home.

Description:
The Soulful & Spicy Soup of the South: MTR Rasam Powder - 200g

Experience the authentic and invigorating flavors of a South Indian culinary classic with MTR Rasam Powder 200g. This signature spice blend from the legendary MTR brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is expertly crafted with a unique combination of ground lentils and spices, including coriander, black pepper, and cumin. It's designed to help you effortlessly recreate the traditional tangy, spicy, and deeply aromatic taste of Rasam – a beloved soupy dish.

 What is Rasam?

Rasam is a light, fluid, soup-like dish from South India, known for its distinctive sour and spicy flavor profile. It's typically made with a tamarind or tomato base, along with dal water, and seasoned with a special blend of spices – the Rasam Powder. It can be consumed as a soup, an appetizer, or mixed with steamed rice.

  • Authentic South Indian Flavor: MTR's recipe, perfected over decades, captures the genuine taste of traditional Rasam, balancing tanginess, pepperiness, and savory notes.
  • Perfectly Balanced Spice Blend: Takes the guesswork out of sourcing, roasting, and grinding numerous individual spices like coriander, cumin, black pepper, red chilli, and fenugreek. MTR ensures a consistent and delicious flavor.
  • Aromatic & Comforting: The blend of spices creates a wonderfully fragrant and warming soup that is both comforting and believed to have digestive and cold-relieving properties in traditional home remedies.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: Simplifies the cooking process by providing the essential spice foundation in one perfectly proportioned pack.
  • Trusted Heritage Brand: MTR Foods has been a staple in South Indian kitchens for a century, guaranteeing quality and authentic taste.

 How to Use MTR Rasam Powder

(MTR packaging usually provides detailed recipe instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Cook a small amount of toor dal until very soft. Mash it and keep aside with its cooking water.
  2. Prepare a tamarind extract by soaking tamarind in hot water and squeezing out the juice.
  3. In a pot, combine the tamarind extract, chopped tomatoes, and the required amount of MTR Rasam Powder (as per package instructions or to taste). Add salt and a pinch of turmeric.
  4. Bring to a boil and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes for the raw taste of the tamarind and spices to cook out.
  5. Add the cooked, mashed dal with its water. Adjust consistency with more water if needed. Let it simmer for a few more minutes, but do not boil vigorously after adding the dal.
  6. Prepare a tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds, dried red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida (hing) in ghee or oil, and pour over the Rasam.
  7. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

Enjoy as a soup or mix with hot steamed rice and a dollop of ghee.

Your Stockholm Destination for Authentic South Indian Spices
Ideal Indiska is delighted to bring you iconic South Indian staples like MTR Rasam Powder, making it easy to prepare this classic and wholesome dish in your Stockholm kitchen.

The Essence of Tikka Masala

Tikka Masala is a creamy, tomato-based curry with a complex, aromatic flavor profile. While its origins are debated, its taste is universally loved.

  • Authentic Mild & Creamy Flavor: Patak's paste is carefully balanced to deliver a genuine Tikka Masala taste that is rich and flavorful without being hot, making it perfect for all palates.
  • Rich Blend of Aromatic Spices: Features a classic mix of spices like coriander, cumin, and turmeric, combined with the tang of tomato and a hint of yogurt for an authentic base.
  • Versatile for Chicken, Paneer & Veggies: While classic with chicken (Chicken Tikka Masala), this paste is equally delicious with paneer, mixed vegetables, chickpeas, or even fish.

How to Use Patak's Tikka Masala Paste

(Patak's jars typically provide recipe suggestions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Heat oil in a pan and brown your choice of diced chicken, paneer, or vegetables.
  2. Stir in Patak's Tikka Masala Paste (typically 2-3 tablespoons per serving) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

  4. Bring to a simmer and cook until your main ingredients are tender and the sauce has thickened.
  5. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot with basmati rice or naan bread.

Name: Taaza Paneer (Authentic Indian Cottage Cheese) 500g - Fresh & Creamy
Short description:
Taaza Paneer Half kg

\nEnjoy the fresh, creamy taste of authentic Indian cottage cheese with Taaza Paneer! This 450g block is made with natural ingredients for a soft yet firm texture that doesn't crumble. Perfect for cubing and adding to classic dishes like Palak Paneer, Paneer Butter Masala, or for grilling as Paneer Tikka.
Description:
The Heart of Vegetarian Indian Cuisine: Taaza Paneer - 450g

Discover the authentic taste and superb quality of Taaza Paneer (Authentic Cottage Cheese), a 450g block of fresh, versatile Indian cheese, now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. "Taaza" means fresh, and this paneer lives up to its name, offering a creamy, milky flavor and a firm, non-crumbly texture that makes it the perfect protein-rich foundation for countless beloved vegetarian dishes from the Indian subcontinent.

What Makes Taaza Paneer a Kitchen Essential?

Paneer is an unaged, non-melting fresh cheese made by curdling milk with a fruit or vegetable acid, like lemon juice or vinegar. Its unique characteristics make it a culinary star:

  • Authentic Creamy Flavour: Made with natural ingredients, Taaza Paneer provides a clean, milky, and slightly sweet taste that beautifully absorbs the flavors of the spices and sauces it's cooked in.
  • Firm, Non-Crumbly Texture: This paneer is pressed to achieve a firm block that holds its shape perfectly when cubed and cooked, making it ideal for curries, stir-fries, and grilling. It doesn't melt like other cheeses.
  • Rich in Protein: A fantastic source of vegetarian protein, making it a satisfying and wholesome component of any meal.
  • 100% Vegetarian: A pure vegetarian product, as highlighted on the packaging.
  • Incredibly Versatile: The culinary possibilities with paneer are endless, from rich gravies and dry sabzis to grilled appetizers and even some desserts.

Culinary Inspiration for Your Taaza Paneer

  • Classic Curries: Create iconic dishes like Palak Paneer (spinach), Paneer Butter Masala (creamy tomato gravy), Shahi Paneer (royal creamy curry), and Matar Paneer (peas).
  • Grilled Appetizers: Marinate cubes in yogurt and spices to make delicious Paneer Tikka, perfect for the grill, oven, or air fryer.
  • Dry Preparations (Sabzi): Sauté with spices, onions, and bell peppers for a quick and easy Kadai Paneer or Paneer Bhurji (scrambled paneer).
  • Snacks: Use as a filling for samosas, puffs, or sandwiches.
  • Indo-Chinese Dishes: Perfect for making crispy Chilli Paneer.
  • Salads: Can be lightly pan-fried and added to salads for a protein boost.

How to Prepare Taaza Paneer

For the best texture, it's often recommended to soak the paneer in warm water for 15-20 minutes before cooking. This makes it even softer.

  1. Remove the paneer from the packaging.
  2. (Optional but Recommended) Place the block in a bowl of warm water for 15-20 minutes.
  3. Drain the water and gently pat the paneer dry.
  4. Cut into cubes or your desired shape and use as per your recipe.

Your Stockholm Source for Fresh & Authentic Paneer

Ideal Indiska is proud to offer high-quality dairy products like Taaza Paneer, ensuring you have the essential ingredients for authentic and delicious Indian vegetarian cooking right here in Stockholm.

Bombaywala Motichoor Ladoo

\n

Indulge in the quintessential Indian festive sweet with Bombaywala Motichoor Ladoo! Description:
The Sweet Jewels of Indian Celebration

Experience one of India's most beloved and iconic sweets with Bombaywala Motichoor Ladoo, presented in a generous 400g pack. This timeless delicacy, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a true testament to the art of Indian sweet-making (mithai). "Motichoor" literally translates to "crushed pearls," perfectly describing the ladoo's unique texture – thousands of tiny, golden droplets (boondi) made from gram flour, which are fried in ghee, soaked in a fragrant sugar syrup, and then expertly hand-rolled into delectable, melt-in-your-mouth spheres.

A Symphony of Texture and Taste

Bombaywala Motichoor Ladoo is a cherished sweet across India, a staple at weddings, festivals like Diwali and Ganesh Chaturthi, and all joyous occasions. What sets it apart is its unique combination of sensory delights:

  • Melt-in-Your-Mouth Texture: Unlike other ladoos, the Motichoor variant is incredibly soft and moist, with the tiny boondi pearls dissolving effortlessly on the palate.
  • Rich & Aromatic Flavor: Crafted with high-quality gram flour (besan) and pure ghee (clarified butter), it boasts a rich, nutty, and buttery flavor. The sugar syrup is often delicately infused with cardamom or saffron, lending a warm and inviting aroma.
  • A True Festive Delicacy: The vibrant orange-golden hue and the intricate texture make it a visually stunning addition to any festive platter. It's a symbol of happiness and auspicious beginnings.
  • Ready-to-Eat Convenience: Enjoy this classic, labor-intensive sweet without spending hours in the kitchen. Bombaywala provides them ready for your immediate enjoyment, capturing the authentic homemade taste.

How to Best Enjoy Your Motichoor Ladoo

This traditional sweet is a complete treat on its own, but here are some popular ways to enjoy it:

  • As a Festive Offering: An essential part of the sweets offered during Diwali, religious ceremonies (pooja), and other celebrations.
  • A Delightful Dessert: Serve as a perfect, satisfying end to a traditional Indian meal.
  • With a Warm Drink: Pairs beautifully with a cup of tea or a glass of warm milk.
  • As a Thoughtful Gift: A box of Motichoor Ladoos is a classic and much-appreciated gift when visiting friends and family during festive seasons.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Indian Mithai

At Ideal Indiska, we are passionate about bringing you the authentic taste of India's rich culinary heritage. We are proud to offer Bombaywala Motichoor Ladoo, a sweet that embodies the spirit of celebration and tradition. Add this box of golden happiness to your cart and share the joy with your loved ones in Stockholm.

Name: Idhayam Sesame Oil ( Gingelly Oil/Nallennai) 1 Litre - Traditional South Indian Oil
Short description:
Idhayam Sesame Oil

\n

Experience the authentic taste and rich aroma of South Indian cooking with Idhayam Gingelly Sesame Oil (Nallennai). This 1-litre bottle contains traditionally prepared sesame oil, prized for its distinct nutty flavor. An essential for mixing with idli podi, tempering dishes, and various traditional wellness practices.
Description:
The Heart of South Indian Flavour: Idhayam Gingelly Sesame Oil

Discover the cornerstone of authentic South Indian and Chettinad cuisine with Idhayam Gingelly Sesame Oil (Nallennai) in a generous 1-litre bottle. Available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, "Idhayam"—meaning "heart" in Tamil—is a brand that has been a trusted staple in kitchens for generations. This sesame oil is celebrated for its distinctive, rich, nutty aroma and a robust flavor that is integral to a vast array of traditional dishes, from simple accompaniments to complex curries.

The Authentic Taste of Nallennai

Gingelly oil, or "Nallennai" (good oil) as it's lovingly called in Tamil, is much more than just a cooking medium; it's a flavour enhancer with a deep cultural significance.

  • Rich & Nutty Aroma: Idhayam Sesame Oil is known for its pure, strong, and authentic nutty fragrance that blooms beautifully when heated, a tell-tale sign of high-quality sesame seeds.
  • Distinctive Robust Flavour: Unlike lighter, toasted sesame oils from East Asia, this Indian gingelly oil has a deeper, more robust, and earthy flavor profile that is uniquely suited for South Indian cooking.
  • Traditional Preparation: Idhayam is known for its careful selection of sesame seeds and its traditional preparation methods, which aim to preserve the natural goodness and authentic taste of the oil.
  • A Culinary Essential: It is the irreplaceable secret ingredient that gives many South Indian dishes their characteristic taste.
  • Ayurvedic & Wellness Traditions: Sesame oil holds a revered place in Ayurveda, often used for oil pulling and as a warming massage oil (Abhyanga) due to its perceived nourishing and balancing properties.

How to Use Idhayam Sesame Oil in Your Kitchen

This versatile oil is a must-have for anyone looking to create genuine South Indian meals:

  • With Idli Podi (Milagai Podi): The most classic use! A generous swirl of Idhayam gingelly oil mixed with spicy idli podi creates a delicious, fiery, and aromatic dip for soft idlis and crispy dosas.
  • For Tempering (Tadka/Thaalipu): Used to bloom mustard seeds, urad dal, curry leaves, and dried red chillies for flavoring sambar, rasam, kootu, and various vegetable preparations (poriyals).
  • In Gravies & Curries: It forms the flavour base for many Chettinad-style curries (like chicken or mutton kuzhambu) and other South Indian gravies.
  • Pickles (Achaar): Used in the preparation of many South Indian pickles for its preservative qualities and unique flavor.
  • Drizzling: A small drizzle over certain finished dishes can enhance their aroma and taste.

Beyond the Kitchen: Traditional Wellness

  • Oil Pulling: Swishing a tablespoon of sesame oil in the mouth for several minutes is a traditional Ayurvedic practice believed to support oral hygiene.
  • Massage Oil (Abhyanga): Used as a base oil for traditional body massage, often warmed slightly.

The Hallmarks of Garimaa Gold Basmati

Garimaa Gold is selected for home cooks who appreciate the finest quality in their staple grain:

  • Premium Long Grains: Characterized by its elegant, slender grains that elongate significantly upon cooking, offering a beautiful presentation on your plate.

Culinary Uses for Garimaa Gold Basmati Rice

  • Side Dish for Global Cuisine: Its delicate flavor also complements Middle Eastern, Persian, and other international dishes that call for high-quality long-grain rice.

Cooking Perfect Garimaa Gold Basmati Rice
(General instructions, always refer to the pack for specific guidance)

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of Garimaa Gold Basmati Rice in cold water 2-3 times, or until the water runs relatively clear.
  2. Soak (Highly Recommended): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30 minutes. This allows the grains to absorb water, cook evenly, and achieve maximum length.
  3. Cook (Open Pot Method): Drain the soaked rice. Add it to 1.5 to 2 cups of boiling water with a pinch of salt. Cook on medium-low heat, covered, for 10-12 minutes (or until all water is absorbed).
  4. Cook (Closed Pot/Rice Cooker): Use approximately 1.5 cups of water for every 1 cup of soaked rice. Follow your rice cooker's instructions.
  5. Rest: Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes. This allows the steam to finish cooking the grains and helps them become more separate.
  6. Fluff: Gently fluff the rice with a fork before serving.

Your Stockholm Choice for Premium, Aromatic Basmati

Short description:

\n

Discover the traditional beauty secret with Neha Rachni Mehandi! This 500g value pack contains 100% pure, finely sifted natural henna (Mehndi) powder, renowned for its quality. Perfect for achieving a rich, reddish-brown stain for intricate body art or as a natural conditioner and colourant for strong, shiny hair.
Description:
The Timeless Art of Natural Adornment: Neha Rachni Mehandi

Experience the pure, unadulterated beauty of traditional henna with Neha Rachni Mehandi (Natural Henna Powder) in a generous 500g value pack. Available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, this is the quintessential beauty staple for anyone seeking to create stunning Mehndi body art or to naturally colour and condition their hair. Neha is a trusted brand, renowned for providing high-quality, finely sifted henna powder made from the best Lawsonia inermis leaves, ensuring a smooth paste and a rich, beautiful stain.

 What is Rachni Mehandi and How Does it Work?

Rachni Mehandi refers to high-quality henna powder that "stains well" or "takes color beautifully" (from the Hindi word 'rachna'). It's pure, natural henna, free from synthetic dyes or chemicals.

  • Source: It is a powder made from the dried leaves of the henna plant, Lawsonia inermis.
  • Active Compound: The magic lies in a molecule called "lawsone," which naturally binds to the keratin in your skin and hair.
  • The Result: This binding process leaves a temporary stain that starts as bright orange and oxidizes to a deep reddish-brown over 24-48 hours. On hair, it imparts reddish to auburn tones and provides a strengthening, conditioning treatment.

Is Neha Rachni Mehandi Suitable for Both Hair and Body Art?

Absolutely! That is the beauty of 100% pure henna powder.

  • For Hair (Natural Colour & Conditioning): Neha Rachni Mehandi is an excellent natural hair dye and conditioner. It coats the hair shaft, adding thickness and incredible shine while imparting a beautiful reddish-brown hue. The final colour depends on your original hair colour – it will give a vibrant red tint to lighter hair and a rich auburn sheen to darker hair. Many mix it with other herbs like Amla for deeper reds or Indigo for brownish to black shades.
  • For Body Art (Mehndi): Its fine sift ensures a smooth, non-clogging paste that is perfect for creating intricate, detailed designs on hands and feet for weddings, festivals like Eid and Diwali, or for beautiful temporary tattoos.

How to Achieve the Best Results with Your Henna

Creating the perfect henna paste is a rewarding ritual. Here's a simple guide:

  1. Mixing the Paste: For the deepest stain, mix Neha Rachni Mehandi powder with a slightly acidic liquid like lemon juice or strong black tea until you get a smooth, thick consistency like mashed potatoes. Avoid using plain water if you want a dark stain for body art.
  2. Dye Release (Crucial Step): Cover the paste and let it rest in a warm place for 6-12 hours (or overnight). You'll know it's ready when the top surface turns a darker brownish-red. This "dye release" process activates the lawsone molecule.
  3. Application:
    • For Hair: Apply the paste evenly to clean hair, from roots to tips. Cover with a shower cap and leave for 2-4 hours. Rinse thoroughly with water only (shampoo the next day for best results).
    • For Body Art: Fill a cone with the paste and create your design on clean, oil-free skin. Let it dry completely and leave it on for as long as possible (6-8 hours is ideal).

  1. Aftercare: Gently scrape off the dried paste. For body art, avoid water for the first 12-24 hours and apply a natural oil or balm. The colour will darken beautifully over the next two days.

What is meant by "Chakki Fresh Atta"?

The term "Chakki" refers to a traditional Indian stone mill used for grinding grains. "Chakki Fresh Atta" signifies that this whole wheat flour has been ground in a way that mimics this method. This slow, low-temperature grinding process is crucial as it helps to preserve the natural dietary fiber, bran, and nutrients of the whole wheat grain. This not only makes the atta more nutritious but is also the secret to achieving that authentic, homemade taste and an incredibly soft texture in your rotis.

Why Choose Al Baker Chakki Fresh Atta?

Description:
The Golden Standard for Your Everyday Rotis

Experience the consistent quality and wholesome taste of Swarna Chakki Fresh Atta in a convenient 4.5kg pack. A trusted choice for many households and available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, "Swarna" (meaning gold) signifies the golden standard of wheat used to create this premium whole wheat flour. It is processed using a traditional chakki-grinding method, ensuring your homemade Indian flatbreads are always exceptionally soft, fluffy, and full of natural flavor.

What is meant by "Chakki Fresh Atta"?

The term "Chakki" refers to a traditional Indian stone mill used for grinding grains. "Chakki Fresh Atta" signifies that this whole wheat flour has been ground in a way that mimics this method. This slow, low-temperature grinding process is crucial as it helps to preserve the natural dietary fiber, bran, and nutrients of the whole wheat grain. This not only makes the atta more nutritious but is also the secret to achieving that authentic, homemade taste and an incredibly soft texture in your rotis.

Why Choose Swarna Chakki Fresh Atta?

  • Guaranteed Softness: Swarna's formulation and chakki-grinding process are designed specifically to produce dough that yields consistently soft and pliable rotis, chapatis, and phulkas that don't dry out quickly.
  • 100% Whole Wheat Goodness: Made from the entire wheat grain, this atta is a natural source of dietary fiber, which is essential for a healthy and balanced diet.
  • Rich in Nutrients: By preserving the bran and germ of the wheat, this atta retains more natural vitamins and minerals compared to refined flours.
  • Easy to Knead: The fine quality of the flour ensures a smooth, elastic dough that is a pleasure to work with, whether you are kneading by hand or with a machine.
  • Trusted Brand: Swarna is a well-regarded brand, known for its commitment to providing high-quality staple flours.

Perfect for All Your Favourite Indian Breads

  • Daily Roti & Chapati: The ideal choice for making soft, wholesome flatbreads to accompany your everyday dals and curries.
  • Fluffy Phulkas: Create light rotis that puff up perfectly on the flame.
  • Delicious Parathas: Use it to make flavourful plain, stuffed (like Aloo Paratha), or flaky layered parathas.
  • Puri: Make soft yet crispy deep-fried puris for festive meals or special breakfasts.

Name: Haldiram's Chana Jor Garam
Short description:
Haldiram's Chana Jor Garam (Spiced Flattened Chickpeas) 200g - Tangy & Crunchy Snack

Experience the zesty and crunchy delight of a classic Indian street food snack with Haldiram's Chana Jor Garam! This 200g pack offers crispy, flattened black chickpeas tossed in a tangy and spicy blend of traditional masalas. Incredibly addictive and perfect for snacking anytime.
Description:
The Zesty & Crunchy Sound of Indian Streets

Travel to the bustling streets of India with every bite of Haldiram's Chana Jor Garam 200g. This iconic and nostalgic "namkeen" (savory snack), from the legendary Haldiram's brand and available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a unique and incredibly addictive treat. It's made from black chickpeas (kala chana) that are cooked, flattened into small discs, and then fried or roasted until crispy, before being tossed in a mouth-watering blend of tangy and spicy masalas.

A Unique Snack Experience

"Chana Jor Garam" (which translates to "hot and strong chickpeas") is a classic snack often sold by street vendors in paper cones. Its appeal lies in its unique combination of texture and flavor.

  • Crispy, Not Puffy: Unlike roasted chickpeas, the flattening process gives Chana Jor Garam a distinct, flaky crispiness that is incredibly satisfying to crunch on.
  • Tangy & Spicy "Chatpata" Flavour: Haldiram's seasons this snack with a classic "chatpata" masala blend, featuring tangy notes from amchur (dry mango powder), a savory kick from black salt (kala chana), and warmth from chilli and other spices.
  • A Taste of Nostalgia: For many, this snack is a cherished taste of childhood and the lively atmosphere of Indian street food culture.
  • Wholesome & Protein-Rich: Made from black chickpeas, it's a good source of plant-based protein and fiber, making it a more wholesome choice than many other fried snacks.
  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Haldiram's ensures that this traditional snack is prepared hygienically and with high-quality ingredients, delivering a consistent and delicious experience every time.

A Swedish Connection?

While there's no direct equivalent, you can think of Haldiram's Chana Jor Garam as a more exotic and flavorful cousin to Swedish roasted fava beans (rostade bondbönor) or other seasoned, crunchy snacks. Imagine a crispy, savory snack, but instead of just salt, it's bursting with the tangy, zesty, and aromatic spices of India!

How to Enjoy Your Chana Jor Garam

This ready-to-eat snack is perfect as it is, but for an authentic street-style experience:

  • Toss it with finely chopped onions, tomatoes, green chillies, and fresh coriander.
  • Squeeze a generous amount of fresh lemon or lime juice over the top.
  • Enjoy it as a standalone snack with your evening tea, as a crunchy topping for salads, or as a component in a Bhel Puri mix.

Short description:
Annam Roasted Rava (Roasted Semolina/Sooji) 1kg - For Fluffy Upma & Halwa

\n

Save time and achieve perfect results with Annam Roasted Rava (Semolina/Sooji)! This 1kg pack contains high-quality semolina that has been conveniently pre-roasted to a nutty aroma. It's the perfect, lump-free start for making fluffy Upma, delicious Sooji Halwa, Rava Kesari, and other traditional Indian dishes.
Description:
The Secret to Perfect, Fluffy Upma Every Time

Discover the ultimate convenience for your Indian kitchen with Annam Roasted Rava (Roasted Semolina/Sooji) in a generous 1kg pack. This thoughtful product from the trusted Annam brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, takes one of the most crucial steps in preparing delicious semolina dishes and does it for you. This Rava is pre-roasted to perfection, ensuring a beautiful nutty aroma and guaranteeing a fluffy, non-lumpy texture in your Upma and Halwa every single time.

What is Roasted Rava and Why is it Better?

Rava, also known as Suji or Semolina, is a granulated flour made from durum wheat. In Indian cooking, the first step for many Rava dishes is to dry-roast it. This important process:

  • Develops a Nutty Aroma: Roasting brings out a deep, nutty, and fragrant aroma from the semolina grains.
  • Prevents Lumps: It helps the grains stay separate when added to water or milk, preventing a sticky or lumpy texture.
  • Enhances Flavor: It gives the final dish a richer, more complex taste.

Annam's Roasted Rava saves you this time-consuming step, making your cooking process faster, easier, and more consistent.

Effortless & Authentic Indian Delicacies

When a recipe calls for "roasted sooji," this is your go-to ingredient. It's the perfect choice for:

  • Fluffy Upma: Create the classic savory South Indian breakfast porridge. With Annam's Roasted Rava, you can be confident your Upma will be light and fluffy, not sticky.
  • Rich Sooji Halwa / Rava Kesari: The pre-roasted grains will readily absorb the ghee, sugar, and milk to create a decadent, aromatic, and perfectly textured Indian dessert.
  • Instant Rava Idli & Rava Dosa: A great base for quick, no-fermentation batters for soft idlis and crispy dosas.
  • Porridge & Sheera: Make simple sweet or savory porridges for a quick and wholesome meal.

Your Stockholm Source for Convenient Kitchen Staples

Name: Haldiram's Peanut Gajjak 400g
Short description:
Haldiram's Peanut Gajjak (Moongphali/Sing Chikki) 400g - Crispy & Nutty Brittle

Enjoy the authentic, crunchy, and nutty taste of a North Indian winter classic with Haldiram's Peanut Gajak! This 400g pack offers a delicious brittle made from high-quality roasted peanuts and jaggery or sugar syrup. Perfectly crisp and satisfyingly sweet, it's an ideal traditional treat for cold days or festive sharing.
Description:
The Crunchy & Comforting Taste of an Indian Winter

Experience the classic and wholesome sweetness of Haldiram's Peanut Gajjak (Moongphali/Sing Chikki) 400g. This traditional Indian confection from the iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a delightful brittle celebrated for its incredible crunch, nutty flavor, and comforting warmth. Made from roasted peanuts set in a crisp sheet of jaggery (gur) or sugar syrup, Gajak is a cherished winter delicacy and a staple during festivals like Lohri and Makar Sankranti.

What is Peanut Gajjak (Chikki)?

Gajak, often used interchangeably with "Chikki" in many regions, is a type of brittle or sweet bar.

  • The Texture: Its defining characteristic is its satisfyingly hard, crisp, and brittle texture that shatters with every bite.
  • The Ingredients: The core components are simple and wholesome – roasted peanuts (moongphali or sing) and a sweetener, which is traditionally jaggery (gur), cooked to a "hard-ball" stage.
  • The Flavor: A perfect harmony of the deep, nutty flavor of roasted peanuts and the rich, earthy, or caramel-like sweetness of the jaggery/sugar base. It's often enhanced with a hint of cardamom or ghee.

Why Choose Haldiram's Peanut Gajjak?

When you're searching for "traditional Indian winter sweets" or "Lohri snacks in Stockholm," Haldiram's Gajak is an authentic and convenient choice.

  • Authentic, Homestyle Taste: Haldiram's recipe captures the essence of traditionally prepared Gajak, delivering that perfect balance of nutty and sweet with an unbeatable crunch.
  • Crispy Perfection: Crafted to achieve that ideal brittle texture that is so satisfying to eat.
  • Made with Quality Ingredients: Uses high-quality peanuts and sweeteners for a delicious and wholesome treat.
  • A Traditional Winter & Festive Sweet: Deeply associated with the warmth and joy of winter festivals in North India.
  • Ready-to-Eat Convenience: Enjoy this classic sweet without the tricky process of making perfect sugar/jaggery syrup at home.
  • Trusted Brand: Haldiram's is a globally recognized name, synonymous with quality and authentic Indian sweets and snacks.

A Swedish Connection?

While unique, you can think of Haldiram's Peanut Gajak as an Indian cousin to Swedish knäck or a classic peanut brittle (jordnötsknäck). It shares the same delightful combination of caramelized sugar and nuts but often with the deeper, more complex flavor of jaggery and a satisfyingly crisp, rather than chewy, texture.
