Name: Decorative Diwali Diya Set (Set of 4) - Hand-Painted Clay Lamps
Short description:
Hand-Painted Clay Lamps

\n

Illuminate your home this Diwali with our beautiful, hand-painted Decorative Diya Sets! Each pack contains four vibrant, traditional clay lamps (diyas), essential for celebrating the Festival of Lights. Perfect for your pooja thali, home altar, or as a festive decoration around your home. Choose your favourite stunning design below!
Description:
The Radiant Heart of the Festival of Lights

Welcome the joy and light of Diwali into your home with our exquisite collection of Hand-Painted Decorative Diwali Diya Sets. Available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, each set contains four traditionally crafted clay lamps, ready to be lit to create a warm, auspicious, and beautifully festive atmosphere. These diyas are more than just decorations; they are a symbol of hope, happiness, and the victory of light over darkness, making them an essential part of your Diwali celebrations.

Discover Our Beautiful Hand-Painted Designs

We have curated a selection of vibrant designs to match your festive decor. Each diya is a small piece of art, handcrafted with care. Please select your preferred design from the options available:

  • (Variation 1) Rainbow Paisley: A classic paisley (kalka) shaped diya, painted with a stunning rainbow gradient and intricate details, bringing a modern, colourful touch to a traditional shape.
  • (Variation 2) Sparkling Floral: A beautiful, round floral-shaped diya adorned with a border of sparkling stones and vibrant colours, designed to glitter and shine in the lamplight.
  • (Variation 3) Earthy Lotus Square: A unique design featuring a lotus-shaped diya set within a square base, painted in warm, earthy tones of gold and terracotta with delicate colourful accents.
  • (Variation 4) Shimmering Silver Lotus: An elegant and classic choice. These square-based diyas are finished in a brilliant silver, intricately designed with lotus petal borders to create a sophisticated and pure glow in your home.
  • (Variation 5) Rainbow Paisley: A celebration of colour! These classic paisley (kalka) shaped diyas are painted with a stunning rainbow gradient and intricate details, bringing a modern, joyful touch to a traditional shape.
  • (Variation 6) Golden Autumn Leaf: Inspired by nature, these diyas are shaped like beautiful leaves. Painted in rich, earthy tones of deep green, maroon, and a textured golden finish, they bring a warm and rustic charm to your Diwali decorations.

What are Diyas and Why are They Important for Diwali?

For our friends in Stockholm new to this beautiful tradition, a "diya" (or diya lampa in Swedish) is a small earthen lamp, traditionally made from clay (mitti). During Diwali, the "Festival of Lights," homes are decorated with rows of lit diyas. This tradition is deeply symbolic:

  • Welcoming Goodness: The light from the diyas is believed to light the path for Goddess Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity, to enter homes and bless the family.
  • Symbol of Victory: It symbolizes the triumph of good over evil, light over darkness, and knowledge over ignorance.
  • Creating a Sacred Atmosphere: The warm, gentle glow of a diya flame is considered pure and is essential for pooja (worship) and creating a serene, spiritual ambiance.

How to Use Your Decorative Diyas

Preparing and lighting your diyas is a beautiful ritual in itself.

  1. Preparation (Optional Tip): To prevent the clay from absorbing too much oil, you can soak your new diyas in water for a few hours, then let them dry completely before use.
  2. Place the Wick: Take a cotton wick (batti), roll it slightly, and place it in the center of the diya, with the tip pointing upwards.
  3. Add Oil or Ghee: Fill the diya about three-quarters full with a suitable oil, like mustard oil, sesame oil, or preferably, pure Cow Ghee for its sacred significance.
  4. Light Up: Light the tip of the wick. Place your beautifully lit diyas on windowsills, balconies, at the entrance of your home, or on your home altar (mandir).
  5. Safety First: Always place diyas on a stable, fire-resistant surface, away from curtains, drafts, and out of reach of children and pets. Never leave lit diyas unattended.

Experience the pure, unadulterated luxury of one of India's most cherished sweets with Bombaywala Kaju Katli (Cashew Fudge) 400g. Kaju Katli (also known as Kaju Barfi) is a smooth, thin, melt-in-your-mouth fudge crafted primarily from the finest, pure cashew nuts, milk, and sugar, and is traditionally cut into its iconic diamond shape.

The Allure of Authentic Kaju Katli

Kaju Katli is a symbol of celebration, luxury, and affection in Indian culture. Its delicate flavor and smooth texture make it a universal favorite.

  • Rich & Creamy Cashew Flavor: The star of this sweet is the pure, milky, and nutty flavor of high-quality cashews. Unlike other sweets with complex spices, Kaju Katli celebrates the simple, elegant taste of the cashew nut.
  • Melt-in-Your-Mouth Texture: Expertly prepared to achieve an incredibly smooth, dense, yet soft texture that dissolves beautifully on the palate.
  • The Ultimate Festive & Gifting Sweet: A box of Kaju Katli is the quintessential gift for Diwali, Raksha Bandhan, weddings, and other joyous occasions. Its sophisticated appeal makes it a perfect choice to share with friends, family, and colleagues.
  • Ready-to-Eat Luxury: Enjoy this premium sweet without the extremely difficult and technical process of making it from scratch. Bombaywala provides this delicacy ready for your enjoyment.
  • Often Decorated with Edible Silver (Vark): While not always present, Kaju Katli is traditionally adorned with a thin layer of edible silver leaf, signifying its premium status. (Check packaging to confirm if this version includes it).

How to Enjoy Your Bombaywala Kaju Katli

Kaju Katli is a ready-to-eat delicacy, best enjoyed at room temperature.

  • Serve as a luxurious dessert after a special meal.
  • Arrange on a platter as the centerpiece of your festive mithai selection.

The Taste of Pure Sunshine, All Year Round

Indulge in the sweet, rich, and unmistakable flavor of the king of fruits with Alibaba Sweetened Mango Pulp, available in a generous 850g tin at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. It's the perfect pantry staple for effortlessly creating a wide range of delicious mango-flavored drinks, desserts, and treats, no matter the season.

The Ultimate Convenience for Mango Lovers

Making mango pulp from scratch can be a time-consuming process. Alibaba's canned pulp offers a simple and delicious solution:

  • Rich & Authentic Flavor: Made from carefully selected, ripe mangoes (often varieties like Alphonso or Kesar are used, check tin for specifics) to deliver a consistent, sweet, and authentic mango taste.
  • Smooth, Velvety Texture: Processed to a perfectly smooth, lump-free consistency, ideal for blending into drinks and desserts.

  • Year-Round Availability: Allows you to enjoy the delicious taste of mangoes even when they are not in season.
  • Versatile Kitchen Ingredient: A must-have for making many classic Indian and Pakistani beverages and sweets.

How to Use Alibaba Mango Pulp

The possibilities with mango pulp are wonderfully delicious:

  • Classic Mango Lassi: The most popular use! Simply blend the mango pulp with plain yogurt, a little milk or water to thin, and a touch of sugar if desired. A pinch of cardamom is a classic addition.
  • Mango Milkshakes: Blend with milk and a scoop of vanilla ice cream for a thick and creamy milkshake.

Name: India Gate Thin Poha 500g
Short description:
India Gate Thin Poha (Thin Rice Flakes/Paper Aval) 500g - For No-Cook Snacks & Chivda

\n

Enjoy light and delicate snacks with India Gate Thin Poha (Thin Rice Flakes). This 500g pack contains paper-thin, flattened rice that requires minimal to no cooking. Perfect for making crunchy Poha Chivda, instant soaked poha with yogurt or milk, and other quick, light Indian snacks.
Description:
The Delicate Base for Light & Crispy Snacks

Discover the delicate and versatile nature of India Gate Thin Poha (Thin Rice Flakes/Paper Aval) in a convenient 500g pack. This premium quality product from the trusted India Gate brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, consists of flattened rice flakes that are much thinner and lighter than the regular "thick" variety. This "paper-thin" quality makes it the perfect ingredient for a variety of quick, no-cook, or lightly fried Indian snacks.

Understanding the Difference: Thin vs. Thick Poha

While both are flattened rice, their uses are quite different. Thin Poha (known as Paper Aval in South India) is delicate and rehydrates almost instantly.

  • Delicate & Paper-Thin: The flakes are extremely light and crisp, which makes them ideal for certain preparations where a non-chewy texture is desired.
  • Minimal to No Cooking Required: Unlike thick poha used for the classic sautéed breakfast dish, thin poha can often be prepared by simply soaking it briefly in liquid, making it perfect for instant snacks.
  • Ideal for Crispy Chivda: When fried or roasted, thin poha becomes incredibly light and crispy, making it the preferred choice for making Poha Chivda (a savory snack mix).
  • Trusted India Gate Quality: From India Gate, a brand renowned for its commitment to high-quality grains, ensuring clean and consistent rice flakes.
  • A Wholesome & Light Snack Base: Poha is naturally gluten-free and easy to digest, making it a great choice for a light meal or snack.

How to Use India Gate Thin Poha

The delicate nature of thin poha opens up a range of quick and easy culinary possibilities:

  • Poha Chivda (Savory Snack Mix): The most popular use. Deep fry or dry roast the thin poha until it puffs up and becomes very crispy. Toss with fried nuts (like peanuts and cashews), raisins, curry leaves, and spices for a delicious and addictive snack mix.
  • Soaked Poha with Yogurt/Curd (Dahi Poha): A simple, no-cook, and refreshing dish. Gently rinse the thin poha and drain. Mix with whisked yogurt, salt, and a simple tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds and curry leaves.
  • Soaked Poha with Milk (Doodh Poha): A sweet and simple preparation. Briefly soak the thin poha in milk and sweeten with sugar or jaggery. Flavor with cardamom and garnish with nuts.
  • Garnish: Fried thin poha can be used as a crunchy topping for some chaat dishes or salads.

Important Cooking Note:

  • Thin Poha is not generally suitable for making the traditional sautéed breakfast dish "Kanda Poha" or "Batata Poha," as it will turn mushy. For those dishes, you need Thick Poha.

Name: TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura/Kurmura) 200g
Short description:
TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura/Kurmura) 200g - For Bhel Puri & Light Snacks

\n

Create the ultimate Indian street food snack, Bhel Puri, at home with TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura)! This 200g pack contains light, airy, and crispy puffed rice, ready to be tossed with chutneys, onions, potatoes, and sev. Also perfect for making light savory snacks or healthy breakfast cereals.
Description:
The Light & Airy Base for Iconic Indian Street Food

Experience the quintessential crunch of authentic Indian chaat with TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura/Kurmura) in a convenient 200g pack. This simple yet incredibly versatile ingredient, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, consists of rice grains that have been puffed under high pressure to create a light, airy, and wonderfully crispy texture. Murmura is most famously known as the star ingredient in the beloved street food snack, Bhel Puri.

What is Puffed Rice (Murmura)?

Puffed rice is a type of puffed grain made from rice, commonly used in breakfast cereals and snack foods. In India, it's a pantry staple with a neutral taste that makes it a perfect vehicle for a variety of bold flavors.

  • Light, Airy & Crispy Texture: Its defining characteristic is its feather-light weight and satisfying crunch, which provides a wonderful textural contrast in snacks.
  • The Heart of Bhel Puri: Unmistakably the main component of Bhel Puri, where it's tossed with chopped onions, potatoes, tangy tamarind chutney, spicy green chutney, and topped with crispy sev.
  • Versatile Snack Ingredient: Used in many other Indian street food snacks like Jhalmuri (a spicy puffed rice snack from Bengal) and as a component in various "chivda" or "mixture" namkeens.
  • A Healthier Snacking Choice: Puffed rice is very low in calories and fat, making it a light base for snacks.
  • Ready to Use: This product is ready to be used straight from the pack.
  • Trusted TRS Quality: TRS is a well-regarded brand, known for its commitment to providing clean, high-quality staple ingredients.

How to Use TRS Puffed Rice

The possibilities are endless, but here are the most popular uses:

  • Bhel Puri: The classic preparation. Mix TRS Puffed Rice with boiled potato cubes, finely chopped onions and tomatoes, a squeeze of lemon, and a generous amount of tamarind chutney and mint chutney. Mix well and top with a mountain of crispy sev and fresh coriander. Serve immediately to retain the crunch!
  • Jhalmuri: A spicy Bengali version, often tossed with mustard oil, spices, chopped onions, chillies, and other ingredients.
  • Savory Snacks (Chivda): Can be lightly roasted or fried with turmeric, curry leaves, peanuts, and other spices to create a simple, light namkeen.
  • Light Breakfast Cereal: Can be enjoyed with milk and a little sugar or jaggery, similar to a commercial rice cereal.
  • Energy Bars & Sweets: Used in making "murmura ladoo" or "kurmura chikki," where it's bound together with melted jaggery.

A Swedish Connection?

While unique, you can think of TRS Puffed Rice as a savory, more versatile version of plain rice breakfast cereals like Rice Krispies. Imagine using that light, crispy texture not just with milk, but as the base for a vibrant, tangy, and spicy salad-like snack!

Dabur 100% Organic Cow Ghee 470g - Pure & Traditionally Churned

Experience the ultimate in purity and flavour with Dabur 100% Organic Cow Ghee. This 470g jar contains pure, certified organic ghee, traditionally churned from the milk of free-grazing cows. Description:
The Gold Standard of Purity: Dabur 100% Organic Cow Ghee

Elevate your cooking and embrace a more wholesome lifestyle with Dabur 100% Organic Cow Ghee, available in a 470g jar at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. This premium ghee from Dabur, a brand with a 135-year legacy in Ayurvedic and natural wellness, is not just pure—it's certified organic. Crafted with the utmost care from the milk of free-grazing cows and prepared using traditional churning methods, this ghee offers an unparalleled richness in flavor, aroma, and nutritional goodness.

What Makes Dabur's Organic Ghee Superior?

When you search for the "best pure ghee in Stockholm" or a truly "natural cooking fat," Dabur Organic Ghee stands out. It's a choice for those who value purity, from source to finish.

  • Certified 100% Organic: This ghee is certified organic, which means it is made from the milk of cows that are raised on organic feed, are not treated with antibiotics or growth hormones, and are free to graze on natural pastures. This ensures a product free from chemical residues.
  • From Free-Grazing Cows: The milk's quality is directly linked to the cow's diet. Free-grazing cows that eat fresh grass produce milk that is often richer in beneficial nutrients like Omega-3s and Vitamin A.
  • Traditional Bilona-Style Churning: Made by the traditional method of churning whole curd (dahi), this slow and deliberate process is believed to preserve more nutrients and create a ghee with a superior, more authentic flavor and a perfect granular (danedar) texture.
  • Rich Aroma & Authentic Taste: Experience the deep, nutty, and slightly caramelized aroma that only comes from high-quality, traditionally prepared ghee.
  • A Staple in Ayurvedic Wellness: In Ayurveda, organic ghee from grass-fed cows is considered the highest quality. It is revered for its digestive benefits (agni stimulant), its ability to nourish the body's tissues (ojas), and as a powerful carrier (anupana) for herbs.

How to Use Your Dabur Organic Ghee

This versatile ghee is perfect for a wide range of uses where purity and flavor are paramount:

  • High-Heat Cooking: With its high smoke point, it's perfect for sautéing, frying, and making a flavorful tadka for your dals and curries.
  • A Flavorful Finish: Drizzle a spoonful over hot Basmati rice, fresh rotis, or khichdi to add a delicious, nutty richness.
  • Gourmet Sweets (Mithai): The pure, rich flavor is essential for making exquisite Halwa, Ladoos, and Barfis.
  • Sacred for Pooja: Its certified organic purity makes it an ideal choice for lighting diyas and preparing prasad for religious ceremonies.
  • Daily Wellness Routine: A teaspoon in a glass of warm milk is a classic Ayurvedic tonic believed to promote strength and vitality.

Name: Annam Unroasted Rava (Semolina/Sooji) 1kg
Short description:
Annam Unroasted Rava (Semolina/Sooji) 1kg - Versatile & Fine Durum Wheat

\n

A versatile kitchen essential, Annam Unroasted Rava (Semolina/Sooji) is the perfect base for a variety of Indian dishes. This 1kg pack contains high-quality, fine durum wheat semolina. Roast it fresh at home to unlock a rich, nutty aroma for making fluffy Upma, decadent Sooji Halwa, Rava Ladoo, and crispy batters.
Description:
The Authentic Base for Countless Indian Delicacies

Discover the versatility and freshness of Annam Unroasted Rava (Semolina/Sooji) in a generous 1kg pack. This staple ingredient from the trusted Annam brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a high-quality, finely granulated semolina made from purified durum wheat. Being unroasted, it offers you the creative freedom to roast it fresh in your own kitchen, unlocking the maximum nutty aroma and perfect texture just moments before you cook.

What is Unroasted Rava and Why Do Cooks Prefer It?

Rava, also known as Suji or Semolina, is a cornerstone of Indian cooking. While roasted rava offers convenience, many traditional cooks and culinary enthusiasts prefer unroasted rava for several reasons:

  • Maximum Freshness and Aroma: The process of roasting (bhun-na) awakens the nutty fragrance of the semolina. By roasting it yourself, you experience this aroma at its absolute peak, infusing your entire dish with unparalleled freshness.
  • Control Over Roasting: You can control the exact level of roasting – from a light toasting for a delicate Upma to a deeper roast for a richer Sooji Halwa, tailoring the flavor to your specific recipe.
  • Incredibly Versatile: It's a true all-rounder, serving as the foundation for classic savory breakfast dishes, decadent desserts, and even as a texture-enhancing ingredient in other recipes.
  • Trusted Annam Quality: Annam is a brand celebrated for its authentic South Indian staples, ensuring this Rava is clean, consistent, and of high quality.

How to Roast and Use Your Annam Unroasted Rava

Roasting is a simple but crucial step for most recipes:

  1. Heat a heavy-bottomed pan or kadai over low to medium heat.
  2. Add the required amount of Annam Unroasted Rava to the dry pan.
  3. Stir continuously for 5-7 minutes. You will notice the color changing slightly and a beautiful, nutty aroma will be released. Be careful not to let it burn.
  4. Remove from the pan and use immediately in your recipe, or let it cool completely and store in an airtight container for future use.

Culinary Inspiration:

  • Fluffy Upma: Create the classic savory South Indian breakfast porridge with your freshly roasted rava for a non-lumpy, fragrant result.
  • Rich Sooji Halwa / Rava Kesari: The freshly roasted grains will readily absorb the ghee, sugar, and milk to create a decadent, aromatic dessert.
  • Rava Ladoo: The perfect base for making these popular sweet balls with ghee, sugar, and nuts.
  • Rava Idli & Rava Dosa: Use it to create quick, no-fermentation batters for soft idlis and crispy dosas.
  • Crispy Coatings: Add to batters or use as a coating for fried fish or cutlets for an extra-crispy finish.

Name: Annam Upma Rava (Coarse Semolina/Sooji) 1kg
Short description:
Annam Upma Rava (Coarse Semolina/Sooji) 1kg - For Fluffy & Non-Sticky Upma

\n

Create the perfect, fluffy, and non-sticky Upma every time with Annam Upma Rava! This 1kg pack contains high-quality, coarsely granulated semolina (Moti Suji/Sooji), specifically chosen for making the classic South Indian savory breakfast porridge. Its texture ensures each grain stays separate and flavorful.
Description:
The Authentic Choice for Perfect, Fluffy Upma

Discover the secret to making restaurant-quality, non-sticky Upma at home with Annam Upma Rava (Coarse Semolina/Sooji) in a generous 1kg pack. This specialty product from the trusted Annam brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a coarsely granulated semolina, often known as Moti Suji or Lapsi. Its larger grain size is specifically selected for preparing Upma, the beloved savory breakfast porridge from South India, ensuring a light, fluffy, and perfectly textured dish every time.

The "Upma Rava" Difference: Coarse vs. Fine

While fine semolina is used for sweets, the coarser texture of Upma Rava is essential for achieving the classic Upma consistency.

  • Distinct Granular Texture: The larger granules absorb water without breaking down completely, preventing the dish from becoming pasty or mushy. The result is a fluffy Upma where you can feel the individual grains.
  • Guaranteed Non-Sticky Results: This is the primary reason cooks choose Upma Rava. It's the key to an authentic, non-lumpy Upma.
  • Rich, Nutty Flavor when Roasted: Like all semolina, its flavor is tremendously enhanced when roasted before cooking, releasing a beautiful, nutty aroma.
  • Versatile for Other Dishes: While perfect for Upma, its coarse texture is also ideal for making savory cakes like Rava Dhokla or adding a crunchy texture to other preparations.
  • Trusted Annam Quality: Annam is a brand celebrated for its authentic South Indian staples, ensuring this Rava is clean, consistent, and of high quality.

How to Make Delicious Upma with Annam Upma Rava

  1. Roast the Rava (Crucial Step): In a dry, heavy-bottomed pan, dry-roast the required amount of Annam Upma Rava on low-medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring continuously, until it turns fragrant and very lightly golden. Set aside.
  2. Prepare the Tempering (Tadka): In the same pan, heat ghee or oil. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add urad dal, chana dal, and peanuts (optional) and fry until golden.
  3. Sauté Aromatics: Add chopped onions, green chillies, ginger, and curry leaves. Sauté until the onions are translucent.
  4. Add Water: Add hot water to the pan (typically a 1:2 or 1:2.5 ratio of rava to water, check pack for specifics) along with salt. Bring the water to a rolling boil.
  5. Combine: Lower the heat and slowly pour in the roasted Rava while stirring continuously with your other hand to prevent lumps.
  6. Cook: Mix well, cover the pan, and cook on low heat for 3-5 minutes, or until all the water is absorbed and the Rava is soft and fluffy.
  7. Finish: Garnish with fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon juice. Serve hot.

Name: Deshi Chinigura Aromatic Rice 1kg
Short description:
Deshi Chinigura Aromatic Rice (Kalijeera/Baby Basmati) 1kg - For Pulao & Desserts

\n

Experience the exquisite, buttery aroma of authentic Bangladeshi cuisine with Deshi Chinigura Aromatic Rice! This 1kg pack contains premium quality, short-grain Chinigura rice, often called "Baby Basmati" for its fragrance. Perfect for making fragrant pulao, special biryanis, and creamy rice desserts (kheer/payesh).
Description:
The Aromatic Jewel of Bengali Cuisine: Deshi Chinigura Rice

Discover the secret ingredient behind the most fragrant and flavorful dishes of Bangladesh and West Bengal with Deshi Chinigura Aromatic Rice, available in a 1kg pack at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. This unique, short-grain aromatic rice is a culinary treasure, cherished for its incredible natural fragrance, delicate texture, and its ability to elevate festive dishes like pulao and biryani to a gourmet level.

What is Chinigura Rice? A Guide for Food Lovers

When people ask, "What is the most fragrant rice from Bangladesh?", the answer is almost always Chinigura. It's not just rice; it's an experience.

  • Its Name and Origin: "Chinigura" translates to "grains of sugar," referring to its tiny, fine grain size. It is a premium aromatic rice grown primarily in Bangladesh. It's also known by other names like Kalijeera ("black cumin," for the appearance of the raw grain) or affectionately as "Baby Basmati" because it packs a powerful aroma into a tiny grain.
  • The Aroma: This is its most defining characteristic. When cooked, Chinigura rice releases a beautiful, strong, and slightly buttery or nutty aroma that is completely distinct from Basmati or Jasmine rice. It perfumes the entire dish.
  • The Texture: The short grains cook up soft and tender but remain separate, not overly sticky. This texture is perfect for absorbing the flavors of ghee and spices.

How is Chinigura Rice Different from Basmati?

This is a common question. While both are aromatic, they are used differently:

  • Grain Size: Chinigura has short, fine grains, whereas Basmati has very long, slender grains.
  • Texture: Chinigura is softer and more tender. Basmati is known for being exceptionally fluffy and separate.
  • Aroma: Chinigura has a buttery, potent aroma. Basmati has a more delicate, nutty fragrance.
  • Best Use: Chinigura is the star of Bengali Pulao (Polao) and Biryani. Basmati is the king of North Indian/Pakistani Biryani and Pulao.

How to Cook Perfect Chinigura Rice for Pulao

Making a traditional Bengali Pulao with this rice is a rewarding experience.

  1. Wash, Don't Soak: Gently wash 1 cup of Deshi Chinigura Rice until the water is mostly clear. Unlike Basmati, this rice is usually not soaked for a long time, if at all, for pulao.
  2. Sauté the Aromatics: In a pot, heat ghee. Sauté whole spices like cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, and a bay leaf. You can also add sliced onions and sauté until golden.
  3. Toast the Rice: Add the washed and drained rice to the pot. Gently sauté for 1-2 minutes until the grains are coated in ghee and release a beautiful aroma.
  4. Add Liquid: Add approximately 1.75 to 2 cups of hot water or broth for every 1 cup of rice. Add salt to taste.
  5. Cook: Bring to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to the lowest setting, cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until all the water is absorbed. Do not open the lid during this time.
  6. Rest: Let the cooked rice stand, covered and off the heat, for at least 10 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork before serving.

Chick Peas: Central to Indian, Pakistani & Global Culinary Entities

The chick pea is a truly indispensable entity within the culinary traditions of both India and Pakistan, as well as other global cuisines. Its adaptability shines in a vast array of beloved recipes:

  • Chana Masala: The quintessential Indian curry, a robust and spicy preparation of chick peas cooked with onions, tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a blend of garam masala and other South Asian spices.
  • Hummus: A globally recognized creamy dip made from blended chick peas, tahini, lemon juice, and garlic, frequently enjoyed alongside naan bread or pita in South Asian households.
  • Chole Bhature: A popular Punjabi breakfast or lunch entity featuring a tangy, spicy chana curry (often called chole) served with fluffy, deep-fried bhature bread.
  • Pakoras & Fritters: Ground chick pea flour (besan) is used to make the batter for crispy pakoras (vegetable fritters), a popular Indian and Pakistani snack. (This relates the whole chick pea entity to its flour entity).
  • Salads & Stews: Chick peas add substantial protein and texture to fresh vegetable salads and hearty stews, making them a versatile component in countless healthy recipes.

  2. Rinsing: After soaking, thoroughly drain the water and rinse the chick peas under fresh, cold running water.
  3. Boiling: Place the rinsed chick peas in a large pot, cover them generously with fresh water (at least two inches above the legumes), and bring to a rolling boil. Reduce the heat to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook until tender.

Brown Lentils: An Indispensable Entity in Indian & Pakistani Gastronomy

Brown lentils (whole masoor dal) are an incredibly versatile and indispensable entity within both Indian cuisine and Pakistani cuisine, as well as other global food cultures. Their adaptability shines in a vast array of beloved recipes:

  • Masoor Dal: The classic dal preparation, where brown lentils are simmered with ginger, garlic, tomatoes, and tempered with a fragrant tarka (tarka dal) of spices like cumin and mustard seeds.
  • Koftas & Kebabs: Ground or mashed brown lentils can serve as a binding agent or a primary ingredient in vegetarian koftas and kebabs, offering a rich, meaty texture.
  • Hearty Soups & Stews: These lentils provide body and nutrition to comforting soups and rich stews, absorbing various flavors beautifully.
  • Salads: Cooked brown lentils add substance, protein, and texture to vibrant grain salads or fresh vegetable salads.
  • Curries & Sabzis: They can also be incorporated into various vegetable curries (sabzis) to enhance their nutritional profile and texture.

Achieving Perfection: How to Cook Hadeel Brown Lentils for Best Results

Preparing Hadeel Brown Lentils is straightforward, yielding delicious and nutritious results. Unlike some other pulses, brown lentils often require minimal soaking:

  1. Rinsing (Essential First Step): Measure your desired quantity of Hadeel Brown Lentils. Thoroughly rinse them under cold, running water to remove any dust or debris. Soaking is generally optional for brown lentils, though a quick 30-minute soak can slightly reduce cooking time.
  2. Boiling: Place the rinsed brown lentils in a large pot, cover them generously with fresh water (about 3 cups of water for every 1 cup of lentils), and bring to a boil.
  3. Simmering: Reduce the heat to a gentle simmer, cover the pot partially, and cook until the lentils are tender.
  4. Cooking Time: Typically 20-30 minutes. Be careful not to overcook, as they can become mushy.
  5. Pro Tip: Avoid adding salt at the very beginning of cooking, as it can sometimes toughen the lentils. Add salt towards the end of the cooking process.
  6. Flavor Integration: Once cooked to your desired tenderness, your Hadeel Brown Lentils are ready to be integrated into your dals, soups, curries, or salads, soaking up all the delightful flavors of your chosen spices and ingredients.

  • Qust al-Hindi: This is perhaps its most common name, especially within Islamic tradition and Unani medicine, where it holds particular esteem. "Hindi" refers to its Indian origin.
  • Kuth: Another widely recognized Indian name, frequently used in Ayurvedic traditions.
  • Saussurea Costus Root: The scientific botanical name for the plant, identifying the specific species from which this root is derived.

The whole root form of Indian Costus allows for versatile preparation, whether you prefer to grind it fresh for specific applications or use it in its natural state.

  • Traditional Medicine Systems: It is a well-documented herb in Ayurveda, Unani, and other South Asian traditional practices, where specific preparations of the root are used.

Name: Annam Peanut Oil (Groundnut Oil/Kadalai Ennai) 1 Litre - For High-Heat Cooking & Frying
Short description:
Peanut Oil (Groundnut Oil/Kadalai Ennai)

\n

Shop Annam Peanut Oil (Groundnut Oil) 1L at Ideal Indiska, Stockholm. A versatile cooking oil with a high smoke point, perfect for deep frying, stir-frying & authentic South Indian dishes.
Description:
The Go-To Oil for High-Heat Cooking & Frying

Discover the versatility and reliability of Annam Peanut Oil (Groundnut Oil/Kadalai Ennai) in a convenient 1-litre bottle. This high-quality cooking oil from the trusted Annam brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a staple in many Indian, Pakistani, and Southeast Asian kitchens. Extracted from peanuts (groundnuts), this refined oil is celebrated for its high smoke point and mild flavor, making it an excellent all-purpose choice for a wide variety of cooking methods, especially deep frying and stir-frying.

Why Peanut Oil is a Chef's Favourite

Peanut oil, known as Kadalai Ennai in Tamil, is a culinary workhorse prized for several key characteristics:

  • High Smoke Point: Refined peanut oil can be heated to high temperatures (around 232°C / 450°F) before it starts to smoke. This makes it a safe and ideal choice for deep frying, ensuring foods cook evenly and become crispy without the oil breaking down or imparting a burnt taste.
  • Mild & Neutral Flavor: Unlike unrefined or roasted peanut oils, this refined version has a very light, neutral, and subtly nutty taste. It doesn't transfer flavor to the foods cooked in it, making it perfect for a wide range of dishes.
  • Ideal for Asian & Indian Cuisine: It's the traditional oil of choice for many Chinese stir-fries and a popular option for deep frying classic Indian snacks like samosas, pakoras, bhajis, and puris.
  • Rich in Monounsaturated Fats: A good source of heart-healthy monounsaturated fats.
  • Trusted Annam Quality: Annam is a leading brand for authentic South Indian ingredients, ensuring you receive a high-quality and reliable cooking oil.

A Swedish Connection: A Superior Frying Oil

För våra svenska kunder som älskar att fritera, tänk på Annam Peanut Oil som ett överlägset alternativ till vanlig frityrolja eller rapsolja när du lagar asiatisk mat. Dess höga rökpunkt garanterar krispiga resultat, och dess milda nötsmak kompletterar kryddiga rätter perfekt. Det är det perfekta valet för att få den där autentiska, restaurangkvalitetskänslan på dina hemmagjorda vårrullar eller friterade kyckling.

How to Use Annam Peanut Oil in Your Kitchen

  • Deep Frying: The ideal choice for making perfectly crispy and golden samosas, pakoras, spring rolls, french fries, and fried chicken.
  • Stir-Frying: Perfect for quick, high-heat stir-fries in a wok, common in Chinese and other Southeast Asian cuisines.
  • Sautéing & Tempering (Tadka): Can be used as the base oil for sautéing onions and spices for your curries and dals.
  • General Cooking: A reliable and versatile all-purpose oil for most of your daily cooking needs.

What Does "Unpolished" Mean and Why is it Better?

When you see "unpolished" on a pack of dal, it signifies a more natural and wholesome product.

  • Retains Nutrients: The polishing process can strip away the outer layer containing valuable fiber and nutrients.
  • Authentic Flavour: Many cooks believe unpolished dal has a richer, earthier flavor.
  • Healthier Choice: Higher in fiber, it aids digestion and provides sustained energy.

The Versatility of Unpolished Chana Dal (Split Chickpeas) - Low GI, nutty flavor, zinc & folate

Chana Dal Fry & Puran Poli is just the beginning.

A Swedish Connection: Delade gula ärtor

How to Use Fortune Unpolished Chana Dal (Split Chickpeas) - Low GI, nutty flavor, zinc & folate

  1. Preparation: Rinse well. For best results, soak for 30 minutes to an hour.
  2. Cooking: Pressure cook for 3-4 whistles.
  3. Serving: Enjoy hot with steamed Basmati rice, Roti, or fresh Swedish bread.

Why Choose Petra Jordanian Dates?

Short description:
Alibaba Gram Flour (Besan/Chickpea Flour) 2kg 

\n

Description:
The Versatile Heart of the Indian Kitchen

Unlock a world of culinary possibilities with Alibaba Gram Flour (Besan/Chickpea Flour) in a convenient 2kg pack. This fundamental ingredient in Indian and Pakistani cuisine, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a finely milled flour made from chana dal (split brown chickpeas). Renowned for its nutty flavor, excellent binding properties, and incredible versatility, Alibaba's quality besan is perfect for a wide array of savory snacks, traditional sweets, batters, and even as a gluten-free thickening agent.

The Goodness of Gram Flour (Besan)

Besan is much more than just flour; it's a culinary cornerstone and a pantry essential:

  • Nutty & Earthy Flavor: Imparts a distinct, slightly sweet, and nutty flavor to dishes, which deepens beautifully when roasted.
  • Excellent for Batters & Binding: Its unique properties make it the ideal choice for creating the light and crispy batters for pakoras, bhajis, and other fried snacks. It also acts as a great binding agent in vegetarian cutlets and koftas.
  • Foundation for Sweets (Mithai): The star ingredient in beloved Indian sweets like Besan Ladoo, Mysore Pak, and Besan Barfi.
  • Naturally Gluten-Free: A fantastic alternative to wheat flour for those with gluten sensitivities or celiac disease, suitable for many gluten-free recipes.
  • Rich in Protein & Fiber: Gram flour is a good source of plant-based protein and dietary fiber.
  • Trusted Alibaba Quality: Milled and packed in the UK, Alibaba is a trusted brand ensuring a consistent and high-quality gram flour for optimal results in your cooking.

Culinary Uses for Alibaba Gram Flour

  • Savory Snacks:

    • Pakoras & Bhajis: Create crispy, deep-fried fritters with vegetables like onions, potatoes, spinach, paneer, or chillies.
    • Cheela/Pudla: Make savory, protein-rich pancakes.
    • Khandvi & Dhokla (often with other flours): A key component in these popular steamed Gujarati snacks.
  • Sweets (Mithai):
    • Besan Ladoo: Classic sweet balls made with roasted besan, ghee, and sugar.
    • Besan Barfi/Mysore Pak: Rich, fudge-like sweets.

  • Curries & Gravies:

    • Thickening Agent: Used to thicken some curries and particularly Kadhi (a yogurt-based gravy).
    • Koftas: As a binder for vegetable koftas.

  • Batters: For coating vegetables or fish before frying.

  • Traditional Skincare: Besan is also famously used in homemade face packs (ubtan) for its cleansing and exfoliating properties. (Note: this product is sold as food-grade).

Your Stockholm Source for Quality Flours & Staples

Ideal Indiska is committed to providing essential, high-quality ingredients like Alibaba Gram Flour. This 1kg pack is perfect for stocking your Stockholm kitchen for all your authentic Indian and Pakistani cooking, sweet-making, and snacking.

Description:
The Soulful & Refreshing Heart of Iftar

Welcome the holy month of Ramadan with the cherished and unmistakable taste of Qarshi Jam-e-Shirin Syrup 800ml. This legendary beverage concentrate from the trusted Qarshi brand, a household name in Pakistan, is an essential part of Iftar tables across the world. Available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, Jam-e-Shirin ("Sweetness of the Soul") is a unique, all-natural blend of pure floral distillates and herbal extracts, creating a beautifully fragrant, sweet, and incredibly refreshing sharbat.

The Timeless Tradition of Jam-e-Shirin in Ramadan

For generations, the sight of a jug of ruby-red Jam-e-Shirin has been synonymous with breaking the fast. Its significance during Ramadan is profound:

  • The Perfect Iftar Beverage: After a long day of fasting, this sweet and cooling sharbat is traditionally consumed to rehydrate, replenish energy levels, and soothe the body. Its sweet, floral aroma is instantly revitalizing.
  • A Unique Aromatic Blend: Unlike single-flavor syrups, Jam-e-Shirin is a complex symphony of natural extracts. It's crafted from distillates of Rose, Sandalwood, Vetiver (Khas), and various herbs, giving it a multi-layered flavor that is both sweet and deeply aromatic without being overpowering.
  • 100% Natural (as per brand claims): Made from pure, natural extracts without any artificial flavors, making it a wholesome choice for your family.
  • Thirst-Quenching & Cooling: The herbal formulation is designed to be exceptionally thirst-quenching and is believed to have a cooling effect.
  • Trusted Qarshi Heritage: Qarshi is a leader in natural and herbal products in Pakistan, with a legacy of quality and purity that is trusted by millions.

How to Prepare and Enjoy Your Jam-e-Shirin

The versatility of this syrup makes it a year-round favourite, but it truly shines during Ramadan.

  • Classic Sharbat: The most popular use. Mix 2-3 tablespoons of Qarshi Jam-e-Shirin into a tall glass of chilled water. Add ice and a squeeze of lemon or lime for an extra refreshing kick.
  • In Milk: Stir into a glass of cold milk for a delicious, rose-flavored milkshake-like beverage, often called "Doodh Jam-e-Shirin."
  • Flavoring for Desserts: Drizzle over ice cream, kulfi, falooda, or traditional puddings like kheer and firni for a unique floral sweetness and beautiful color.
  • With Yogurt (Lassi): Add a spoonful to sweet lassi for a fragrant twist.

Name: Rang Barse Holi Colours (3-Pack Gulal) 300g - Vibrant & Skin-Friendly
Description:
Let the Colours of Joy Rain Down This Holi!

Get ready to immerse yourself in the vibrant spirit of the Festival of Colours with the Rang Barse Holi Colours (3-Pack Gulal) 300g. "Rang Barse," meaning "the colors are raining," perfectly captures the exuberant joy of Holi, and this pack from Ideal Indiska in Stockholm is your essential companion for a fun-filled and safe celebration. This box contains three distinct, brilliant shades of gulal (dry color powder), ready to be shared and playfully applied.

What is Holi and Why Do We Play with Colours?

Holi is a joyous and ancient Hindu festival that celebrates the arrival of spring, the triumph of good over evil, and a day to forgive, forget, and mend relationships. The most iconic part of the celebration is the playful throwing and smearing of colored powders (gulal) and colored water on friends, family, and even strangers in a spirit of unity and merriment.

Why Choose Rang Barse Holi Colours?

When you're searching for "Holi colors in Stockholm" or "safe gulal online," quality is key.

  • Vibrant & Joyful Colours: This pack includes three brilliant colors, perfect for creating a beautiful and festive rainbow. The pink box itself adds to the festive feel!
  • Skin-Friendly & Non-Toxic (Verify Claim): These colours are formulated to be gentle on the skin, making them safer for both adults and children to enjoy the festivities without worry.
  • Fine & Smooth Powder: The gulal is finely milled to be soft and smooth, making it pleasant to apply.
  • Easy to Wash Off (Typically): Formulated to be easier to wash off skin and hair compared to harsher industrial colors.
  • Perfect for Parties & Gatherings: The 300g quantity, split into three colors, is great for a small family celebration or a party with friends.
  • The Spirit of Celebration: Having these colours on hand is the first step to creating wonderful memories and participating in this beautiful tradition.

How to Celebrate a Fun and Safe Holi

  • Protect Your Skin & Hair: Applying a light layer of coconut oil to your skin and hair before playing can make it easier to wash the colors off later.
  • Wear Older Clothes: Holi is all about getting messy! Wear clothes you don't mind getting stained. White is traditional to make the colors pop!
  • Protect Your Eyes: Avoid getting the powder directly in your eyes. If it happens, rinse thoroughly with clean water.
  • Play with Joy & Respect: The spirit of Holi is "Bura na mano, Holi hai!" (Don't feel offended, it's Holi!). Play with happiness and respect for everyone.

Your Stockholm Source for a Colourful Holi Celebration

At Ideal Indiska, your dedicated Indian and Pakistani grocery store in Stockholm, we love being a part of your festive preparations. We are proud to offer essential Holi items like Rang Barse Colours. Name: Laziza Chaat Masala 100g
Short description:
Chaat Masala

\n

Add an explosion of tangy, zesty, and savory flavor with Laziza Chaat Masala! This 100g pack offers a unique, aromatic blend of spices, black salt, and mango powder. It's the essential finishing spice for authentic chaats, fruit salads, dahi vadas, and for adding a distinctive kick to snacks and beverages.
Description:
The Zesty Kick of Authentic South Asian Street Food

Elevate your snacks, salads, and street food creations with the unique, mouth-watering tang of Laziza Chaat Masala 100g. This distinctive spice blend from Pakistan's beloved Laziza brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is an essential seasoning for anyone looking to recreate the authentic "chatpata" (tangy and savory) taste of Indian and Pakistani street food. It's expertly crafted with a complex mix of zesty, tangy, and savory spices, including the crucial ingredients of black salt (kala namak) and amchur (dry mango powder).

What is Chaat Masala?

Chaat Masala is a quintessential South Asian spice blend used primarily as a finishing garnish. Unlike other masalas that are cooked into a dish, chaat masala is usually sprinkled on top just before serving to provide a final burst of flavor. Its signature taste is a delightful explosion of savory, sour, spicy, and pungent notes all at once.

  • Authentic Zesty & Tangy Flavor: Laziza's blend delivers that signature "chatpata" taste that is irresistible in chaats, salads, and other snacks.
  • Key Ingredients for "Chaat": Contains essential components like Amchur (dry mango powder) for tartness, Black Salt (kala namak) for its unique sulphurous tang, cumin, coriander, black pepper, and other spices that define the taste of chaat.
  • Incredibly Versatile Seasoning: While a must-have for chaats, its uses extend far beyond. It's fantastic on fruit salads, vegetable salads, yogurt preparations (raita, dahi vada), sprinkled over fries or pakoras, and even in some refreshing beverages.
  • Enhances Savory Snacks: A simple sprinkle can transform everyday snacks, fruits, and vegetables into an exciting and flavorful treat.
  • Trusted Laziza Quality: Laziza is a well-regarded brand known for its authentic Pakistani spice blends and consistent quality.

How to Use Laziza Chaat Masala

Chaat Masala is best used as a finishing spice to preserve its fresh aroma and zesty flavor.

  • For Chaat Preparations: The essential seasoning for all types of chaat, including Aloo Chaat, Bhel Puri, Papdi Chaat, Sev Puri, and especially Dahi Vada/Bhalla.
  • On Fruit Salads: Sprinkle generously over mixed fruit salads (Fruit Chaat) for a delicious sweet, savory, and tangy contrast. It's particularly good on fruits like watermelon, guava, apples, and bananas.
  • On Vegetable Salads: Adds a zesty kick to a simple cucumber, tomato, and onion salad (kachumber).
  • With Yogurt: Mix into plain yogurt or various vegetable raitas to give them a tangy lift.
  • On Snacks: Sprinkle over French fries, pakoras, samosas, roasted nuts, or even popcorn.
  • In Beverages: Add a pinch to Nimbu Pani (Indian lemonade) or Jaljeera for a unique, savory twist.

Name: Falak Extreme Basmati Rice 1kg - Premium Extra-Long & Aromatic Grains
Short description: Shop Falak Extreme Basmati Rice 1kg at Ideal Indiska, Stockholm. A premium, aged extra-long grain rice for exceptionally fluffy, separate & aromatic biryani, pulao & special dishes.
Description:
Experience the Pinnacle of Basmati Rice Perfection

Discover a truly superior rice experience with Falak Extreme Basmati Rice, available in a 1kg pack at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. The name "Falak" evokes a sense of heavenly quality, and the "Extreme" designation signifies a commitment to exceptional grain length and purity. Sourced from the finest Basmati fields and carefully aged, Falak Extreme promises incredibly long, slender grains that elongate further upon cooking, exuding a captivating aroma and cooking to a wonderfully fluffy, non-sticky perfection.

The Hallmarks of Falak Extreme Basmati

Falak Extreme is a premium selection designed for those who seek the very best in Basmati rice:

  • "Extreme" Grain Length: This variety is celebrated for its exceptionally long and slender grains. When cooked, they can more than double in length, creating a stunningly elegant presentation that is perfect for show-stopping dishes.
  • Aged for Perfection: Proper aging is the secret to the best Basmati. This process reduces the moisture content, which concentrates the natural nutty flavor, enhances the aroma, and ensures the grains cook up separate and fluffy without breaking.
  • Exquisite Natural Aroma: Delivers the signature, delightful fragrance that is the hallmark of pure, high-quality Basmati rice. Its inviting scent is a promise of the delicious meal to come.
  • Superb Fluffy & Non-Sticky Texture: Each grain remains distinct and separate after cooking, providing the perfect light and airy texture for layered biryanis and rich curries.
  • A Gourmet Choice: This is the rice chosen by discerning home cooks and chefs for dishes where quality, appearance, and texture are of the utmost importance.

The Ideal Choice for Your Most Cherished Dishes

Its superior characteristics make Falak Extreme Basmati the perfect choice for:

  • Luxurious Biryanis: The foundation for a truly majestic Chicken, Mutton, or Vegetable Biryani where every long, fragrant grain stands proud.
  • Elegant Pulaos & Pilafs: Create beautiful and flavorful pulaos, from a simple vegetable pilaf to a rich Zafrani (Saffron) Pulao.
  • Gourmet Accompaniment: Serve as a sophisticated side dish that elevates any rich curry, korma, or dal.
  • Festive & Special Occasion Meals: The definitive choice for impressing guests at dinner parties and celebrating traditions with the highest quality ingredients.

Cooking Instructions for Perfect Falak Extreme Basmati Rice

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of Falak Extreme Basmati Rice in cold water until the water runs clear.
  2. Soak (Crucial): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30-45 minutes. This is essential for premium varieties to achieve maximum length.
  3. Cook (Open Pot Method): Drain the soaked rice. Add to 1.5 to 1.75 cups of boiling water with a pinch of salt. Cook on medium-low heat, covered, for 10-12 minutes, until all water is absorbed.
  4. Rest (Vital): Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the rice stand covered and undisturbed for 10-15 minutes. This steaming process is key for the grains to firm up and separate.
  5. Fluff: Gently fluff the rice with a fork, being careful not to break the long, delicate grains.

Short description:
Buy Maharani Basmati Rice (5kg) Online | Ideal Indiska Stockholm

\n

Shop Maharani Basmati Rice 5kg value pack at Ideal Indiska, Stockholm. Premium, aged long grain aromatic rice for fluffy, separate grains in your biryani, pulao & everyday meals.
Description:
Experience the Royal Standard of Basmati Rice

Elevate your dining to a regal experience with Maharani Basmati Rice, available in a generous 5kg value pack at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. "Maharani," meaning "the Great Queen," perfectly captures the essence of this premium quality Basmati rice. Sourced from the fertile foothills of the Himalayas and carefully aged, Maharani promises long, elegant, slender grains that cook up beautifully separate, wonderfully fluffy, and exude the captivating aroma that makes Basmati the most celebrated rice in the world.

The Hallmarks of a Truly Royal Grain

Maharani Basmati Rice is selected for discerning home cooks who seek perfection in every meal:

  • Premium Long Grains: Characterized by its elegant, slender grains that elongate significantly upon cooking, offering a beautiful presentation worthy of a feast.
  • Aged for Perfection: Proper aging is the secret to the best Basmati. This process enhances its distinct nutty aroma, deepens its delicate flavor, and ensures the grains cook up separate and non-sticky, every single time.
  • Exquisite Natural Aroma: Delivers the signature, delightful fragrance that is characteristic of pure, high-quality Basmati. Its inviting scent fills the kitchen as it cooks, promising a delicious meal.
  • Fluffy & Separate When Cooked: Each grain remains distinct and fluffy, providing the perfect texture for a wide range of dishes, from complex layered biryanis to simple steamed rice.
  • A Taste of Luxury for Everyday: While perfect for festive meals, its consistent quality makes it an excellent choice to make your everyday meals feel more special.
  • Value 5kg Pack: This large pack is ideal for families and regular consumers of Basmati rice, offering excellent value and ensuring your pantry is always stocked.

The Perfect Choice for Your Culinary Masterpieces

  • Biryani & Pulao: The long, separate, and aromatic grains make it the ideal foundation for creating luxurious biryanis and flavorful pulaos.
  • Plain Steamed Basmati: Serves as an elegant and fragrant accompaniment to a wide array of Indian and Pakistani curries, dals, and sabzis.
  • Jeera Rice & Flavored Rice: Its fluffy texture is perfect for simple yet delicious flavored rice dishes like Jeera Rice.
  • Special Occasion Meals: The definitive choice for impressing guests and celebrating traditions with the highest quality ingredients.

Cooking Perfect Maharani Basmati Rice

  1. Rinse: Gently rinse 1 cup of Maharani Basmati Rice in cold water until the water runs clear.
  2. Soak (Highly Recommended): Soak the rinsed rice in fresh water for at least 30 minutes. This allows the grains to achieve maximum length.
  3. Cook (Open Pot Method): Drain the soaked rice. Add it to 1.5 to 2 cups of boiling water with a pinch of salt. Cook on medium-low heat, covered, for 10-12 minutes (or until water is absorbed).
  4. Rest: Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the rice stand covered for 5-10 minutes. This allows the steam to finish cooking the grains.
  5. Fluff: Gently fluff the rice with a fork before serving.
